translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት፤ የጤና ሴክተሩን ቁልፍ ሚና የሚገልጸውን የኬሚካል ፍኖተ ካርታ ጨምሮ፤ አባል ሀገራት ያላቸውን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ስትራቴጂዎች ትግበራ እንዲያጠናክሩ ይበረታታሉ። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ የጤና ሚኒስቴሮች፤ መንግስታት የሳይንስ ፖሊሲን ለማቅረብ በሚያደርጉት ጥረት ላይ እንዲሳተፉ እና የፕላስቲክ ብክለትን ለማስቆም በሚደረግ ስምምነት ላይ እንዲደራደሩ አሳስቧል። ውሳኔው ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የሚከተሉትን እንዲያደርጉ ጠይቋል። ኬሚካሎች፣ ብክነት እና ብክለት እንዴት ጤናን እንደሚጎዱ ከ"አንድ ጤና" (“One Health”) እይታ አንጻር ሪፖርት እንዲያሳትሙ። ከተባበሩት መንግስታት የአካባቢ ጥበቃ ፕሮግራም ጋር በመተባበር፤ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2012 የወጣውን ሰነድ፤ ይህም ስለ “Endocrine”ን (በደም ውስጥ የሚዘዋወሩ ሆርሞኖችን የሚያመርቱ እና የሚያሰራጩ የአካል ክፍሎችን የሚቆጣጠር የሰውነት ስርዓት) የሚጎዱ ኬሚካሎች ማዘመን እንዲሁም
translations.reference: Member States were urged to bolster the implementation of existing WHO strategies including implementation of the WHO Chemicals Roadmap which outlines key roles of the health sector in implementing the Strategic Approach to International Chemicals Management. The resolution also encourages the Ministries of Health to engage in efforts to prepare proposals for an intergovernmental science policy panel and negotiations for a treaty to end plastic pollution. The resolution called upon the Director-General to undertake several actions, including: publishing a report on the health implications of chemicals, waste and pollution from a "One Health" perspective; updating the 2012 document on the State of the Science of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals jointly with the United Nations Environment Programme; and
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

The World Health Organization (WHO) encourages member countries to strengthen the alignment of their WHO strategies, including the chemical safety landscape, which outlines the key role of the health sector. The resolution emphasized the need for health ministers and governments to participate in efforts to develop science policy and to reach agreements to halt plastic pollution. The resolution requested the Director-General to: publish a report from a "One Health" perspective on how chemicals, pollution, and contamination affect health; and, in collaboration with the United Nations Environment Programme, update the document issued in 2012 concerning chemicals that harm the "Endocrine" system (the body system that controls hormones circulating in the blood).

translations.source: በብሔራዊ ወይም በክልል ደረጃ ለሰው ልጅ ጤና አሳሳቢ የሆኑ ኬሚካሎችን ለመቆጣጠር አገሮችን መርዳት ናቸው። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ A76/A/CONF./2 - የኬሚካል፣ ብክነት እና ብክለት በሰው ጤና ላይ የሚያሳድረው ተጽዕኖ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በኬሚካል ደህንነት እና የጤና ደህንነት ላይ የሚሰራው ስራ፡- ሁሉም ዜጎች ሙሉ አካላዊ እና አእምሯዊ ጤናቸውን በህይወት ዘመናቸው እና በትውልዳቸው ሁሉ እንዲያሳኩ ለማስቻል ያለመውን "የጤና ማስተዋወቅ ዘዴን በመጠቀም ደህንነትን ከህብረተሰብ ጤና ጋር ለማዋሃድ አለምአቀፍ መዋቅር"ን ለመቀበል አባል ሀገራት ተስማምተዋል። የአለም አቀፍ መዋቅሩ ትኩረት ሊሰጣቸው የሚገቡ ስድስት ቁልፍ ስትራቴጂካዊ አቅጣጫዎችን ይመክራል፡- ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን፣ ፍትሃዊ ኢኮኖሚ፣ አለማችንን መጠበቅ፣ የማህበራዊ ጥበቃ ስርዓቶች፣ የጤና ዲጂታል ስርዓቶች እና ደህንነትን መለካት እና መከታተል ናቸው።
translations.reference: supporting countries in developing national or regional human biomonitoring programmes for chemicals of concern. Related document: A76/A/CONF./2 - The impact of chemicals, waste and pollution on human health WHO’s work on chemical safety and health Achieving well-being: a global frameworkMember States agreed to adopt the "Global framework for integrating well-being into public health utilizing a health promotion approach", which strives to enable all people to flourish and achieve their full physical and mental health potential throughout their lives and across generations. The Global framework recommends six key strategic directions that focus on: universal health coverage, equitable economies, protecting the planet, social protection systems, digital systems to enable health, and measuring and monitoring well-being.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"They are helping countries to control chemicals that are harmful to human health at the national or regional level. Related document: A76/A/CONF./2 - The impact of chemicals, pollution and contamination on human health. The World Health Organization's work on chemical safety and health safety: Member states have agreed to adopt an "international framework for integrating safety with public health using a health promotion approach – designed to enable all citizens to achieve their full physical and mental health throughout their lives and for generations to come." The international framework recommends six key strategic directions to focus on: universal health coverage, a fair economy, protecting our planet, social protection systems, digital health systems, and measuring and monitoring safety."

translations.source: ጤናማነትን ለማስተዋወቅ እና ጤናን ለመጠበቅ፤ ከጤና ውጭ ካሉ ሴክተሮች ጋር የቅርብ ትብብር ማድረግን መዋቅሩ ይጠቁማል። ሁሉም ባለድርሻ አካላት ወደ አንድ የጋራ ግብ፤ በአንድነት እና በትብብር እንዲሳተፉ መመሪያ ሆኖ ይሰራል። ጤናን ለሰዎች እና ለአለም ዘላቂ እና ፍትሃዊ በሆነ መንገድ ለማሳደግ ይጠቅማል። ለሰዎች ሁሉ እና ለዓለማችን ጤናን በዘላቂነት እና በፍትሃዊነት ለማሳደግ ያለመ ነው። ጉባኤው እ.ኤ.አ. በ2024፣ 2026 እና 2031 የአለምአቀፍ መዋቅሩን አፈፃፀም ሪፖርት እንዲያቀርብ ለዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ጠይቋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ: A76/7 Add.2 - ደህንነት እና ጤናን ማስተዋወቅ (ሰዎች የጤና ሁኔታቸውን እንዲቆጣጠሩ እና ጤንነታቸውን እንዲያሻሽሉ የማድረግ ሂደት።)
translations.reference: The framework proposes close collaboration with sectors outside the health sector to promote and protect health. It serves as a guide for all stakeholders to engage in a coherent and coordinated manner around a common purpose: promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. The Assembly requests the Director-General to report on implementation of the global framework in 2024, 2026 and 2031. Related document: A76/7 Add.2 - Well-being and health promotion
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"To promote health and protect well-being, it suggests establishing close collaboration with sectors outside of health. It serves as a guide for all stakeholders to work together towards a common goal, united and collaboratively. It is beneficial for increasing health for people and the world in a sustainable and equitable way. It is intended to sustainably and equitably increase health for all people and our world. The assembly requested the Executive Director to submit a report on the implementation of the global framework in 2024, 2026, and 2031. Related document: A76/7 Add.2 - Promoting Safety and Health (The process of empowering people to control their health status and improve their health.)"

translations.source: ምግብን በጥቃቅን ንጥረ-ነገሮች ማበልጸግ ላይ ጥረቶችን ለማፋጠን አዲስ መፍትሄ። ልዑካኑ የምግብ ጥቃቅን ንጥረ-ነገር እጥረቶችን (micronutrient deficiencies) በአስተማማኝ እና ውጤታማ በሆነ ምግብን በማበልጸግ ለመከላከል የሚደረገውን ጥረት ለማፋጠን ያለመ አዲስ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ አጽድቀዋል። በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ፤ ሃምሳ በመቶ የሚሆኑ እድሜያቸው ከ5 ዓመት በታች የሆኑ ልጆች እንዲሁም ሁለት ሶስተኛ የሚሆኑ እድሜያቸው ከ15 እስከ 49 የሆኑ ሴቶች እንደ ፎሌት (folate)፣ አይረን (Iron)፣ ቫይታሚን ኤ (vitamin A) እና ዚንክ (Zinc) ባሉ ጠቃሚ ንጥረ ነገሮች እጥረት ይሰቃያሉ። እነዚህ የንጥረነገር እጥረቶች እንደ spina bifida (ከውልደት ጀምሮ የሚከሰት የነርቭ በሽታ) እና ሌሎች የነርቭ ቱቦ ጉድለቶች ያሉ ከባድ የጤና ችግሮችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ። እነዚህን ችግሮች ለመፍታት መጠነ ሰፊ የምግብ ማበልጸግ እንደ የመፍትሄ አካል ሆኖ ይታያል። ሃገራቱ እንደየሃገር አቀፍ የፍጆታ ዘይቤ እና የንጥረነረሮች እጥረት መሰረት፤ እንደ ስንዴ እና የበቆሎ ዱቄት፣ ሩዝ፣ የምግብ ዘይት እና ጨው የመሳሰሉት ዋና ዋና ምግቦች እንዲሁም ማጣፈጫዎች ላይ አስፈላጊ የሆኑትን ቫታሚኖች እና ማዕድኖች በመጨመር የሚታየውን የጥቃቅን ንጥረነገሮች እጥረት ማስተካከል እና የበለጠ መከላከል ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: New resolution to accelerate efforts on food micronutrient fortification The delegates approved a resolution on accelerating efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through safe and effective food fortification. Deficiencies in vitamin and mineral status, particularly of folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all preschool aged children and 67% of all women of reproductive age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects.Large scale food fortification is part of the solution. By adding essential vitamins and minerals to staple foods and condiments, such as wheat and maize flours, rice, cooking oil and salt in accordance with national consumption patterns and deficiencies, countries can correct and further prevent a demonstrated micronutrient deficiency.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**A New Solution to Accelerate Efforts in Fortifying Food with Micronutrients**

Heads of state have endorsed a new initiative aimed at accelerating efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through effective and sustainable food fortification. Globally, fifty percent of children under five years old and two-thirds of women aged 15 to 49 suffer from deficiencies in essential nutrients such as folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc. These nutrient deficiencies can lead to serious health problems, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects. Large-scale food fortification is seen as a key component in addressing these challenges. Countries can address and further prevent the observed micronutrient deficiencies by adding essential vitamins and minerals to staple foods and supplements, such as wheat and bean flour, rice, edible oil, and salt, based on country-specific consumption patterns and the underlying nutrient deficiencies.

translations.source: አገሮች በምግብ ማበልጸግ ላይ ፖሊሲዎችን እንዲያዘጋጁ እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እና የክትትል ስርዓቶችን የሚያጠናክሩበትን መንገድ እንዲያስቡ ይበረታታሉ። ይህ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ ከተባበሩት መንግስታት የአስር ዓመት የተመጣጠነ ምግብ የማቅረብ ውሳኔ (United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition) (እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2016 እስክ 2025) ጋር ይጣጣማል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ EB152/CONF./5 የጥቃቅን ንጥረነገሮችን እጥረትና የሚያመጡትን ችግሮች ለምሳሌ የነርቭ ጉድለቶችን ለመከላከል፤ አስተማማኝ እና ውጤታማ የምግብ ማበልጸጊያዎችን በመጠቀም የሚደረገውን ጥረት ማፋጠን። ተዛማጅ አገናኞች (Related links)
translations.reference: The resolution urges Member States to develop policies on food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplementation, and to consider ways of strengthening financing and monitoring mechanisms. The resolution was agreed under the umbrella of the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025) report. Related document: EB152/CONF./5 Accelerating efforts for preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects, through safe and effective food fortification Related links
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Countries are encouraged to develop policies on food fortification and to consider ways to strengthen financial support and monitoring systems. This decision aligns with the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025). Related document: EB152/CONF./5 to accelerate efforts using safe and effective food fortification strategies to prevent micronutrient deficiencies and the problems they cause, such as neurological disorders. Related links"



I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.source: ስለ መፍትሄው የበለጠ ያንብቡ። ቫይታሚኖችን እና ማዕድኖችን በምግብ ላይ ለመጨመር የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እየሰራ ያለው ስራ። ሀገራት ሀሰተኛ እና ጥራት የሌላቸው መድሃኒቶችን ማስቆም በሚቻልበት መንገድ ላይ ተስማምተዋል። በግንቦት 27፣ ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ሃሰተኛ እና ጥራት የሌላቸው መድሃኒቶችን ለመቆጣጠር “ሜምበር ስቴት መካኒዝም” (Member State Mechanism (MSM)) ለመገምገም ተስማምቷል። ይህ እ.ኤ.አ. በጥር 2023 የተደረገውን የዋና ዳይሬክተሩን ጥሪ ይደግፋል። ግምገማው እ.ኤ.አ. በ2023 በሚቀጥለው የ“ሜምበር ስቴት መካኒዝም” (Member State Mechanism (MSM)) መሪ ኮሚቴ ስብሰባ ይጀምራል።
translations.reference: Read more about the resolution WHO's work on food fortification Member States approve actions to counter substandard and falsified medicines On 27 May, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly approved a resolution to conduct an independent review of the Member State Mechanism (MSM) for incidents, substandard and falsified medicines, reinforcing the Director-General’s January 2023 call for increased vigilance and action in this important area. The review will be initiated at the next MSM steering committee meeting later in 2023.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**Read more about the solution. The World Health Organization's work on increasing vitamins and minerals in food. Countries have agreed on ways to stop counterfeit and substandard medicines. On May 27, the 76th World Health Assembly agreed to evaluate the “Member State Mechanism” (MSM) to control counterfeit and substandard medicines. This supports the Director-General's call made in January 2023. The evaluation will begin at the next “Member State Mechanism” (MSM) Steering Committee meeting in 2023.**

translations.source: የ“ሜምበር ስቴት መካኒዝም” (Member State Mechanism (MSM)) እ.ኤ.አ. በ2012 የተቋቋመው፤ ሃሰተኛ እና ጥራት የሌላቸውን የህክምና ምርቶች፤ ፍትሃዊ እና ግልጥ በሆነ መንገድ ለመቋቋም ነው። አላማውም የህብረተሰብን ጤና መጠበቅ እና በተመጣጣኝ ዋጋ፣ ጥራት ያላቸውን የህክምና ምርቶች ተደራሽ ማድረግ ነው። አባል ሀገራቱ ደህንነታቸውን ያልተጠበቁ እና ሀሰተኛ የሆኑ የህክምና ምርቶችን የማስቆም፣ የመለየት እና ምላሽ የመስጠት አቅማቸውን ለማጠናከር ድጋፍ አስፈላጊ መሆኑን ጠቁመዋል። በተበከሉ መድኃኒቶች ምክንያት የሚደርሱ አሳዛኝ ሁኔታዎችን ለመከላከል እንደ የላብራቶሪ ምርመራዎችን ማጠናከር እና መደበኛ ያልሆኑ ገበያዎች እንዲሁም የመስመር ላይ ሽያጮችን ጠንከር ባለ መንገድ መቆጣጠር ያስፈልጋል። አባል ሀገራቱ እነዚህን ገበያዎች ለመቆጣጠር የክትትል ቴክኖሎጂዎችን መቀበል እና መተግበር እንዳለባቸው አሳስበዋል፤ በተጨማሪም እንደ ህግ አስከባሪ እና የጉምሩክ ኤጀንሲዎች ካሉ የሀገር ውስጥ እና አለምአቀፍ አጋሮች ጋር ትብብር እንዲኖር ጠይቀዋል ። አባል ሀገራቱ ህጎችን ለማሻሻል የሚደረጉ ስምምነቶችን እንዲያከብሩ እንዲሁም ሀሰተኛ እና ደህንነታቸው ያልተጠበቁ የህክምና ምርቶች ላይ ጥብቅ ቅጣቶችን እንዲያጠናክሩ አሳስበዋል።
translations.reference: The Assembly established the MSM in 2012 to tackle substandard and falsified medical products from a public health perspective and in a transparent and inclusive way. The goal of the MSM is to protect public health and promote access to affordable, safe, efficacious and quality medical products. Noting recent events of contaminated medicines found on various markets and related preventable deaths, Member States emphasized the support needed to improve their capacity to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products through access to appropriate technologies, including laboratory testing facilities, along with improved oversight on informal markets and advertising, and the online sale of medical products. Member States called for embracing and implementing track and trace technologies for market control and surveillance, as well as the need for formal collaboration by relevant local and global partners such as law enforcement and customs administrations. Member States were encouraged to align with conventions aimed at improvement of legislation and imposition of prohibitive sanctions for wrongful dealing in substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The Member State Mechanism (MSM), established in 2012, aims to combat counterfeit and substandard medical products in a fair and transparent manner. Its goal is to protect public health and make quality medical products accessible at an affordable price. Member states have indicated the need for support to strengthen their capacity to prevent, detect, and respond to unsafe and counterfeit medical products. To prevent tragic situations caused by substandard medicines, it is necessary to strengthen laboratory testing and rigorously control informal markets as well as online sales. Member states have emphasized the need to adopt and implement monitoring technologies to control these markets; furthermore, they have called for collaboration with domestic and international partners such as law enforcement and customs agencies. Member states have urged adherence to agreements to amend laws and to strengthen strict penalties on counterfeit and unsafe medical products."

translations.source: ሴክሬተሪያቱ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 የስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርዱን የማዘመን ኃላፊነት ተሰጥቶታል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ EB152/9 - ደህንነታቸው ያልተጠበቁ የህክምና ምርቶች ተዛማጅ አገናኞች (Related links) የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት አደጋዎችን፣ ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች መቆጣጠሪያ መንገድ፤ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች ላይ እየሰራ ነው።
translations.reference: The Secretariat is requested to report on progress in this area to the Executive Board in 2025. Related document: EB152/9 - Substandard and falsified medical products Related links WHO Member State Mechanism on incidents, substandard and falsified medical products WHO's work on substandard and falsified medical products

Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"The Secretariat has been assigned the responsibility of updating the Executive Board in 2025. Related document: EB152/9 - Unsafe Medical Products Related Links: A method for controlling substandard and falsified medical products by WHO member states; WHO is working on substandard and falsified medical products."

translations.source: ለተሻለ ጤና ሲባል ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ በባህሪ ሳይንስ (Behavioral Science) ላይ ውሳኔ ተወሰደ። ዛሬ አባል ሀገራቱ የባህሪ ሳይንስ ንድፈ ሃሳቦችን እና ዘዴዎችን ከጤና ርእሶች እና የህዝብ ጤና ስልቶች ጋር ማቀናጀትን የሚያስተዋውቅ ውሳኔ አሳለፉ። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ አባል ሀገራት የጤና ውጤቶችን በማሻሻል ሂደት ውስጥ የባህሪ ሳይንስን አስፈላጊነት እንዲገነዘቡ እንዲሁም የባህሪ ሳይንስ ማስረጃዎችን በመጠቀም ፖሊሲዎችንና ፕሮግራሞችን የሚያግዙ ቡድኖች እንዲያዋቅሩ አሳስቦል። በተጨማሪም ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የባህሪ ሳይንስን በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስራ ውስጥ እንዲያካትቱ እና ለአባል ሀገራት መመሪያ እና ድጋፍ እንዲሰጡ ጥሪውን ያቀርባል። አባል ሀገራቱ በሴክሬታሪያት የሚመራውን “የባህሪ ሳይንስ ለተሻለ ጤና” ተነሳሽነት ስኬት እውቅና ሰተዋል፤ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ላደረገችው ማሌዢያ እንዲሁም ሌሎች የተቀላቀሉ 19 ሃገራትን እና ለዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ምስጋናቸውን አቅርበዋል ።
translations.reference: First ever resolution on behavioural sciences for better health is adopted Today Member States adopted the behavioural sciences for better health resolution with Member States showing broad consensus towards the need for integrating systematically behavioural science theory, methods and approaches across health topics and public health functions. The resolution urges Member States to acknowledge the role of behavioural science in achieving better health outcomes, to identify opportunities for increased use and, to establish functions and units for generating and translating evidence to inform policies and programmes. It also requests the Director-General to mainstream the use of behavioural science within the organization and to provide support to Member States through the development of guidance and the provision of technical assistance. Member States recognized the achievement of the Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative led by the secretariat, congratulated Malaysia, sponsor of the resolution, as well as the other 19 countries that joined as co-sponsors, and thanked the Director-General for the report.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**For Better Health, a Decision Was Made for the First Time in Behavioral Science.** Today, member states adopted a resolution to integrate behavioral science concepts and methods with health topics and public health strategies. The resolution's idea is to encourage member states to recognize the importance of behavioral science in the process of improving health outcomes and to form teams that utilize behavioral science evidence to support policies and programs. Furthermore, the Director-General calls for the integration of behavioral science into the World Health Organization's work and to provide guidance and support to member states. Member states recognized the success of the "Behavioral Science for Better Health" initiative, led by the Secretariat; they expressed their gratitude to Malaysia, which provided financial support, as well as to the other 19 participating countries and to the Director-General.

translations.source: በዚሁ ዘርፍ በተለይም በክልል መሥሪያ ቤቶች ክህሎትን ማሳደግ እንዲሁም የመረጃ ቋትን በማቋቋም በተለያዩ ዘርፎች ማለትም በትምህርት እና በግሉ ሴክተር ላይ ትብብር ማድረግ እንደሚያስፈልግ አስገንዝበዋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነዶች (Related documents) : EB152 (23) - ለተሻሻለ ጤና የባህሪ ሳይንስ EB152/25 ፦ ለተሻለ ጤና የባህሪ ሳይንስ፣ የዋናው ዳይሬክተር ሪፖርት “የባህሪ ሳይንስ ለተሻለ ጤና” ተነሳሽነትን በተመለከተ የተጠቀሱትን ጉዳዮች በሰነድ A76/7 ራዕ.1 ላይ ተዘርዝረዋል። - የጄኔራል ዳይሬክተር አጠቃላይ ሪፖርት ቀርበዋል።
translations.reference: They stressed the importance of building capacity in this area, particularly in regional offices, and of creating a repository of evidence and synergies between sectors, including with academia and the private sector. Related documents: EB152(23) - Behavioural sciences for better health EB152/25 - Behavioural sciences for better health, Report by the Director-General Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative The above items were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"It was emphasized that it is necessary to enhance the capacity, particularly in regional offices, and to collaborate in various sectors, including education and the private sector, by establishing a data platform. Related documents: EB152 (23) - For Improved Health Behavioral Science EB152/25: Regarding the "Behavioral Science for Improved Health" initiative, the report of the main director, issues mentioned in document A76/7 rev.1 are listed. - A comprehensive report from the General Director was submitted."

**Notes on the translation:**

*   I've tried to maintain the formal tone of the original Amharic.
*   "Behavioral Science" is the most accurate translation of "የባህሪ ሳይንስ" in this context.
*   "rev.1" is used to represent "ራዕ.1" which likely indicates a revision number.

translations.source: ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ስብሰባ - ዕለታዊ መረጃ፡ እ.ኤ.አ. ግንቦት 27፣ 2023 በኢንፌክሽን (ሰውነት ጎጂ በሆኑ ረቂቅ ተዋህሲያን መጠቃት) መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ላይ የመጀመሪያው ዓለም አቀፍ ስልት፦ የዓለም ጤና ጥበቃ ጉባኤ ወደ ሁለት አስርት ዓመታት በሚጠጋው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና አጋሮቹ መሪነት ጥረት የተዘጋጀውን የመጀመሪያውን ዓለም አቀፍ የኢንፌክሽን መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ስልት ዛሬ አጽድቋል። ይህ ስትራቴጂ ፀረ ተሕዋስያን መድሃኒቶችን የሚቋቋሙትን ጨምሮ ከጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር የተገናኙ ኢንፌክሽኖችን (health care-associated infections (HAIs)) አደጋ በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ ለመቀነስ ለአባላት ሀገራት ስትራቴጂካዊ መመሪያ ይሰጣል። ከጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር የተገናኙ ኢንፌክሽኖች (HAI) በጤና እንክብካቤ አሰጣጥ ወቅት በጣም ከተለመዱት አሉታዊ ክስተቶች መካከል አንዱ ነው። ኮቪድ-19 እና በቅርብ ጊዜ የተከሰቱ ወረርሽኞች እንደ ኢቦላ ፣ የመካከለኛው ምስራቅ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ (the Middle East respiratory syndrome) እና የሱዳን ቫይረስ በሽታዎች በዓለም ዙሪያ በኢንፌክሽን መከላከል ፕሮግራሞች ላይ ያሉ ክፍተቶችን በግልፅ አሳይተዋል።
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 27 May 2023 First global strategy on infection prevention and controlThe World Health Assembly agreed today on the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), which builds on almost two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides Member States with strategic directions to substantially reduce the ongoing risk of health care-associated infections (HAIs), including those that exhibit antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most frequent adverse events occurring in the context of health service delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major disease outbreaks such as Ebola virus disease, the Middle East respiratory syndrome and the Sudan virus disease have clearly exposed the existing gaps in IPC programmes in all countries.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**76th World Health Assembly - Daily Information: May 27, 2023**

**First Global Strategy on Infection Prevention and Control:** The World Health Assembly today endorsed the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control, prepared under the leadership of the World Health Organization and its partners over two decades of effort. This strategy provides member states with strategic guidance to significantly reduce the risk of infections associated with healthcare (health care-associated infections (HAIs)). HAIs are among the most common adverse events during healthcare delivery. COVID-19 and recent outbreaks such as Ebola, Middle East Respiratory Syndrome (MERS), and Sudan Virus diseases have clearly demonstrated gaps in infection prevention programs around the world.

translations.source: ስልቱ ግልጽ የሆነ ግብ አለው ይህም እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 ሁሉም የጤና አገልግሎት የሚያገኙ ወይም የሚያቀርቡ ሰዎች ከኢንፌክሽን መጠበቅ ነው። በሦስት ቁልፍ ዓላማዎች ላይ ያተኩራል እነዚም፦ በጤና አጠባበቅ ተቋማት ውስጥ ኢንፌክሽንን መከላከል፣ የኢንፌክሽን መከላከል ፕሮግራሞችን ትግበራ ማረጋገጥ እና ጥረቶችን ከሌሎች ዘርፎች ጋር ማስተባበር ናችው። ስልቱ በጤና አጠባበቅ ስርዓቱ ውስጥ ባሉ ሁሉም ቦታዎች ላይ ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ የጤና እንክብካቤ አቅርቦትን በማረጋገጥ ላይ ያተኩራል። እንደ መሰረታዊ የጤና መብት ተቆጥሮ፤ ተጠያቂነት እና ዘላቂነት ባለው መንገድ ፍትሃዊ እንክብካቤ የመስጠት መርህ ላይ አፅንዖት ይሰጣል። ከአለም አቀፍ የኢንፌክሽን መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ስልት ጎን ለጎን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2023 እና 2024 መካከል የተዘጋጀ ተዛማጅ አለም አቀፍ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር እና የክትትል ማዕቀፍ ይኖራል፣ ይህም ውጤታማነቱን ያሳድጋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ EB152/9 - በዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ስለኢንፌክሽን መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር የተዘጋጀ አለም አቀፍ ስትራቴጂ ረቂቅ ማጠቃለያ ዘገባ።
translations.reference: The strategy sets a clear vision: by 2030, everyone accessing or providing health care is safe from associated infections. Its three key objectives are: to prevent infection in health care; act to ensure IPC programmes are in place and implemented; and coordinate IPC activities with other areas and sectors. The strategy is focused on any setting where health care is delivered, across the health system; it is based on the principle of clean and safe care as a fundamental component of the right to health, which is equity driven, and which should ensure accountability and sustainability. The global IPC strategy will be complemented by, and used in conjunction with, an associated global action plan and monitoring framework, that will be developed in 2023–2024. Related document EB152/9 - Draft global strategy on infection prevention and control, Executive summary Report by the Director-General
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The strategy has a clear goal: by 2030, all people providing or receiving health services are protected from infection. It focuses on three key objectives: preventing infection within healthcare facilities, ensuring the implementation of infection prevention programs, and coordinating efforts with other sectors. The strategy focuses on ensuring safe healthcare delivery in all areas within the health care system. It emphasizes the principle of providing equitable care in an accountable and sustainable manner, considering health as a basic human right. Alongside the Global Infection Prevention and Control Strategy, a related global action plan and monitoring framework will be developed between 2023 and 2024 to enhance its effectiveness. Related document: EB152/9 - Summary report on the draft global strategy on infection prevention and control prepared by the Director-General."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ዛሬ ታሪካዊ ውሳኔን አጽድቋል፣ ይህም የመልሶ ማቋቋሚያ አገልግሎቶችን በጤና ስርዓቶች ውስጥ እንዲዋሃድ አድርጓል። የመልሶ ማቋቋሚያ አገልግሎቶች ሰብአዊ መብቶችን ለማስከበር ቁልፍ ሚና ይጫወታሉ ይህም ከፍተኛውን የአካላዊ እና የአዕምሮ ጤና ጨምሮ ነው። የጾታ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤናን እንዲሁም የስራ እና የትምህርት መብቶችንም ለማስከበር ይጠቅማል። ይህ አስደናቂ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ በመልሶ ማቋቋም ላይ ያሉ ተግዳሮቶችን ለመፍታት ያለመ ሲሆን እነዚህም፡ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸው የጤና ሁኔታዎች እና የምርምር አጀንዳዎች ሲመጡ ስለመልሶ ማቋቋም ያለን ግንዛቤ ማሳደግ፣ አስፈላጊ ግብዓቶችን መመደብ፣ ትብብርን ማጎልበት እና የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ማድረግ፣
translations.reference: WHO's work on infection prevention and control Historic resolution paves the way for strengthening rehabilitation in health systemsToday the World Health Assembly agreed a landmark resolution on strengthening rehabilitation in health systems. Rehabilitation services play a key role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights including the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health. It also promotes sexual and reproductive health, and recognizes the right to work and the right to education. This landmark resolution aims to address the challenges in rehabilitation such as the need to: increase awareness of rehabilitation when setting health priorities and research agendas, allocating resources, promoting cooperation and enabling technology transfer;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Today, the World Health Assembly endorsed a historic decision to integrate rehabilitation services into health systems. Rehabilitation services play a crucial role in upholding human rights, including the highest levels of physical and mental health. They are beneficial in ensuring sexual and reproductive health, as well as the rights to work and education. This remarkable decision aims to address challenges related to rehabilitation, including raising awareness about rehabilitation when prioritized health conditions and research agendas arise, allocating necessary resources, strengthening collaboration, and facilitating technological transfer."

translations.source: በድንገተኛ አደጋዎች ምክኒያት የእርዳታ ቴክኖሎጂን ጨምሮ የመልሶ ማቋቋም ፍላጎት መጨመር በሚኖርበት ጊዜ አገሮች ምላሽ ለመስጠት ዝግጁ መሆናቸውን ማረጋገጥ፤ በአስቸጋሪ እና ተጋላጭ በሆኑ ሁኔታዎች ውስጥ ያሉ ሰዎች ተመጣጣኝ፣ ጥራት ያለው እና ተገቢ የሆነ የመልሶ ማቋቋም አገልግሎቶችን እንዲያገኑ ማድረግ፤ ሰዎች ለማገገሚያ አገልግሎቶች እና አጋዥ ቴክኖሎጂዎች ከኪሳቸው ከፍተኛ ወጭ እንዳያወጡ ማድረግ እና የህዝቡን ፍላጎት ለማሟላት አሁን ያለውን በቂ ያልሆነ የሰለጠነ የሰው ሃይል ችግር መፍታት ናቸው። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሴክሬታሪያት የሚወሰዱ የተለያዩ እርምጃዎችን ይዘረዝራል፣ እነዚህም፡-
translations.reference: ensure countries are better equipped to respond to the sudden increase in rehabilitation needs including assistive technology due to health emergencies; ensure persons in marginalized and vulnerable situations have access to affordable, quality and appropriate rehabilitation services including assistive technology; avoid high out-of-pocket costs for people to access rehabilitation services and assistive technology that can cause financial hardships; and address the current insufficient level of rehabilitation workforce to serve the needs of the population. The resolution lists a range of actions to be taken by the WHO Secretariat such as:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"To ensure countries are prepared to respond when there is an increased need for recovery, including assistive technology, due to sudden disasters; to ensure that people in difficult and vulnerable situations receive appropriate, quality, and timely recovery services; to prevent people from incurring high out-of-pocket expenses for recovery services and assistive technologies; and to address the problem of insufficient trained human resources to meet the public's needs. The resolution outlines various actions to be taken by the World Health Organization Secretariat, including:"

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በ2026 መገባደጃ ላይ ሀገራት የመልሶ ማቋቋሚያ አገልግሎቶችን እንዴት በጥሩ ሁኔታ እንደሚሰጡ የሚያሳይ ሪፖርት እንዲያሳትሙ፤ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 ሁሉም ሰው የሚፈልገውን የመልሶ ማቋቋም አገልግሎት ማግኘቱን ለማረጋገጥ ግቦችን እና መለኪያዎችን ማውጣት፤ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሃገራትን በቴክኒካል መመሪያዎችና ግብዓቶች ለመደገፍ በቂ ገንዘብ እንደመደበ ማረጋገጥ እና፤ አባል ሀገራት ድንገተኛ አደጋዎችን ለመቋቋም እቅዳቸው ውስጥ የመልሶ ማቋቋም እና አጋዥ ቴክኖሎጂ እንዲያካትቱ ናቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሴክሬታሪያት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2026፣ 2028 እና 2030 የዚህን የውሳኔ ሃሳብ አፈፃፀም ለጤና ጉባኤው ሪፖርት ያደርጋል።
translations.reference: publishing a baseline report by the end of 2026 with information on the capacity of Member States to respond to rehabilitation needs; developing targets and indicators for effective coverage of rehabilitation services by 2030; ensuring appropriate resources are allocated at WHO to support Member States in implementing technical guidance and resources; and supporting Member States to integrate rehabilitation and assistive technology in their emergency preparedness and response plan. The WHO Secretariat will report on progress in the implementation of this resolution to the Health Assembly in 2026, 2028 and 2030.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"To publish a report by the end of 2026 showing how countries can effectively provide restoration services; to set goals and indicators to ensure that everyone receives the restoration service they need by 2030; to ensure that the World Health Organization member states are adequately supported with technical guidance and inputs; and that member states include restoration and assistive technology in their plans to respond to sudden disasters. The World Health Organization Secretariat will report to the Health Assembly on the implementation of this resolution in 2026, 2028, and 2030."

translations.source: ተዛማጅ ሰነድ (Related document)፡ EB152/8 - በዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የተዘጋጀ በጤና ስርዓት ውስጥ የመልሶ ማቋቋም አገልግሎቶችን የተሻለ ስለማድረግ ሪፖርት። ተዛማጅ አገናኝ (Related link) የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመልሶ ማቋቋም ስራ፤ ከላይ የተዘረዘሩት በዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የተጠናከረው A76/7 Rev.1 ሰነድ አካል ናቸው። የምርመራ አቅምን ለማጠናከር ውሳኔ፤

Resolution on strengthening diagnostics capacity

**Related document:** EB152/8 - Report prepared by the Executive Director on improving rehabilitation services within the health system. **Related link:** WHO rehabilitation work; the above-mentioned document, strengthened by the Executive Director, A76/7 Rev.1, is part of it. Decision to strengthen assessment capacity;

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በግንቦት 26 አባል ሀገራት የምርመራ አቅምን ለማጠናከር እና የምርመራ አገልግሎት ተደራሽነት ለማሻሻል ውሳኔን አጽድቀዋል። ይህ ሰፊ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ ተላላፊ ለሆኑ፣ ተላላፊ ላልሆኑ፣ ችላ ለተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች፣ ላልተለመዱ በሽታዎች፣ ለድንገተኛ አደጋ እና ለአካል ጉዳት መከላከል፣ ክትትል፣ ምርመራ እና ህክምና የምርመራ አገልግሎቶች ወሳኝ መሆናቸውን ግንዛቤ ውስጥ ያስገባል። የምርመራ አገልግሎቶች በሽታዎችን በትክክል ለይቶ ለማወቅ ያስችላሉ፤ ስለዚህም ለተሻለ የጤና ውጤት ትክክለኛ ሕክምና በወቅቱ ለመጀመር ጠቃሚ ናቸው። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ በላብራቶሪ ከሰውነት በብልቃጥ (in vitro) ናሙና ተወስዶ የሚደረጉ ምርመራዎችን እንደ (rapid diagnostic tests) እና (polymerase chain reaction (PCR)) እንዲሁም በሰውነት አካል ውስጥ እንዳለ (in vivo) የሚደረጉ ምርመራዎች እንደ ኢሜጅንግ ወይም የደም ግፊት መለኪያ ያሉትን ጨምሮ ሁሉንም አይነት የመመርመሪያ ሙከራዎች ያካትታል። ለምርምርና ልማት፣ ለምርታማነት (የአገር ውስጥ ምርትና ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግርን ጨምሮ)፣ ለመመሪያዎች፣ ለምርጫና ግዥ፣ ለግንዛቤ መፍጠር እና በአጠቃላይ የተደራሽነት ማነቆዎችን ለመፍታት እርምጃዎችን ያካትታላል።
translations.reference: On 26 May Member States endorsed a resolution to strengthen diagnostics capacity in countres and to improve access to diagnostic services. The broad ranging resolution recognizes that diagnostic services are vital for the prevention, surveillance, diagnosis, case management, monitoring and treatment of communicable, noncommunicable, neglected tropical and rare diseases, injuries, and disabilities. Diagnostics allow for the precise identification of diseases, and therefore the timely initiation of the correct treatments for better health outcomes. The resolution considers the full spectrum of “diagnostics”, thus including both “in vitro” laboratory tests e.g. rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and “non in vitro” diagnostics e.g. imaging or blood pressure measurement devices. It covers actions for research and development, manufacturing (including local production and technology transfer), regulation, selection and procurement, awareness, advocacy and addressing access barriers in general.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"On May 26, 2023, member states adopted a decision to strengthen diagnostic capacity and improve the accessibility of diagnostic services. This broad decision recognizes the critical role of diagnostic services in preventing, detecting, monitoring, diagnosing, and treating communicable, non-communicable, neglected tropical diseases, emerging diseases, emergencies, and injuries. Diagnostic services enable the accurate identification of diseases, which is essential for initiating appropriate treatment promptly and achieving better health outcomes. The decision encompasses all types of diagnostic tests, including laboratory tests performed on bodily samples *in vitro* (such as rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR)) as well as *in vivo* tests performed within the body (such as imaging or blood pressure measurement). It includes actions related to research and development, productivity (including domestic production and technology transfer), guidelines, procurement and supply, awareness creation, and, overall, addressing accessibility barriers."

translations.source: የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ ትግበራ ሀገራት የተሻለ የምርመራ አገልግሎትን እንዲያገኙ ለመርዳት ቀደም ባሉት ጊዜያትና እና በአሁኑ ጊዜ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በሶስት ደረጃዎች እየተከናወነ ያለውን ስራ ለመገንባትና ለማስፋፋት ያስችላል። ሴክሬታሪያቱ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 የአፈጻጸም ሂደቱን ሪፖርት እንዲያቀርብ ተጠይቆል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ EB152/6 የምርመራ አቅምን ማጠናከር
translations.reference: The implementation of the resolution will build and expand on previous and current work at three levels of WHO to help countries improve access to diagnostic services. The Secretariat is requested to report on implementation progress in 2025. Related document EB152/6 Strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"The decision's idea allows for building and expanding the work that the World Health Organization has been conducting in three stages to help conflict-affected countries receive better review services, both in the past and currently. The Secretariats have been asked to submit a report on the implementation process by 2025. Related document EB152/6 Strengthening Review Capacity."



Let me know if you have any other Amharic documents you'd like me to translate!

translations.source: በሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም የጤና ጉባኤ ወቅት ለህብረተሰብ ጤና አስተዋጾ ላደረጉ ሽልማት ተበርክቷል። በጄኔቫ በተካሄደው ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ላይ ለሕዝብ ጤና ከፍተኛ አስተዋጽኦ ላደረጉ ከዓለም ዙሪያ የተውጣጡ ግለሰቦች እና ተቋማትን ሽልማት ተበርክቷል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ "እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023ቱ የህብረተሰብ ጤና ሽልማት ለአለም አቀፍ ጤና ከመደበኛ ስራቸው በላይ ከፍተኛ አስተዋፅዖ ያደረጉ ግለሰቦችን እውቅና ይሰጣል" ብለዋል። "ዓለማችን ፈታኝ ጊዜያት ሲያጋጥሟት፤ እያንዳንዱ ግለሰብ ለህብረተሰብ ጤና እና ደህንነት መሻሻል ልናሳካው የምንችለውን እድገት ማሳያ ሆኖ ያገለግላል።" የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ከተጠናቀቀ በኋላ ለእያንዳንዱ ሽልማት እጩ በየዓመቱ መጥሪያ ይላካል።
translations.reference: Awards for outstanding contributions to public health presented during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly During a moving ceremony at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly in Geneva, awards were presented to persons and institutions from around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. “The 2023 public health prizes and awards celebrate people who have made a significant contribution to global health, and whose work has extended far beyond the call of normal duty,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “At a time when the world faces many challenges, each is an inspiration and a reminder of the progress that can be made to improve health and wellbeing for all.” The call for nominations of candidates for each prize is sent out each year after closure of the World Health Assembly.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Awards were presented at the 76th World Health Assembly for contributions to public health. At the 76th World Health Assembly held in Geneva, awards were presented to individuals and institutions from around the world who have made significant contributions to public health. World Health Organization Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said, "The 2023 Public Health Award recognizes individuals who have made exceptional contributions to global health beyond their regular work." He added, "As our world faces challenging times, each individual serves as an example of the progress we can achieve to improve public health and safety." Following the conclusion of the World Health Assembly, an invitation will be sent annually to each award nominee."

translations.source: እጩዎቹ የሚመረጡት በአለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት ብሄራዊ የጤና አስተዳደር ወይም ከዚህ ቀደም ሽልማቶችን በተቀበለ ሰው ሊሆን ይችላል። እ.ኤ.አ. በጥር 2023 ባካሄደው 152ኛው ክፍለ ጊዜ የስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርዱ የ2023 ሽልማት አሸናፊዎችን ሰይሟል። ሰባቱ ተሸላሚዎች በየሀገራቸው እና በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለህብረተሰብ ጤና ያበረከቱትን ልዩ አስተዋፅኦ እውቅና ለመስጠት ተመርጠዋል። ተሸላሚዎቹም አራት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልሎችን ይወክላሉ፡- አሜሪካ፣ ምስራቃዊ-ሜዲትራኒያን፣ ደቡብ-ምስራቅ እስያ እና ምዕራባዊ ፓስፊክ ናቸው። ከአምስቱ የግል አሸናፊዎች መካከል ሦስቱ ሴቶች ናቸው።
translations.reference: Nominations can be made by national health administrations of a WHO Member State and by any former recipient of the prizes. At its 152nd session in January 2023, the Executive Board designated the 2023 winners of the prizes, on the basis of proposals made by the dedicated selection panels for each prize. The seven laureates have been selected to celebrate their unique role and contribution for public health in their countries and globally. They come from four WHO Regions – for the Americas, the Eastern-Mediterranean, South-East Asia and the Western Pacific. Five winners are individuals, and three among them are women.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Nominees for the award can be a national health administration of a WHO member country or someone who has previously received awards. The 152nd session of the Executive Board, held in January 2023, announced the winners of the 2023 award. The seven winners were selected to recognize their unique contribution to public health at the national and global levels. The winners also represent four WHO regions: the Americas, Eastern Mediterranean, South-East Asia, and Western Pacific. Of the five individual winners, three are women."

translations.source: በተጨማሪም እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2023 ሽልማቶችን የተቀበሉት ሁለት ተቋማት በሴቶች ይመራሉ ። እነዚህም እ.ኤ.አ. የ 2023 የ”ሳሳካዋ” የጤና ሽልማት አሸናፊዎች፡ የ”ኒክ ሲሞንስ” ተቋም ከኔፓል እና ፕሮፌሰር ቪቻይ ቲየንታቮርን ከታይላንድ ናቸው። “ኒክ ሲሞንስ” ተቋም በኔፓል ገጠራማ አካባቢዎች የጤና እንክብካቤን ለማሻሻል የተቋቋመ መንግሥታዊ ያልሆነ ድርጅት ነው። ከኔፓል የህብረተሰብ ጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር ጋር በቅርበት በመስራት ተቋሙ በገጠር 7000 የሚሆኑ የህክምና ባለሙያዎችን (እንደ አዋላጆች እና የማደንዘዣ ባለሙያዎች) አሰልጥኗል፤ እነዚህም ባለሙያዎች አጠቃላይ ሐኪሞች እና ስፔሻሊስቶች እጥረት ባለበት አካባቢ በጣም ጠቃሚ ናቸው። ተቋሙ በ44 ሆስፒታሎች ያለውን የህክምና ባለሙያዎች እጥረት ለመቅረፍ አስፈላጊ የሆኑ ቁሳቁሶችንና ተጨማሪ የጤና ባለሙያዎችን አቅርቧል እንዲሁም ከ100 በላይ የገጠር ሆስፒታሎች የተሻለ ጥራት ያለው አገልግሎት ለመስጠት እንዲችሉ የተግባር እቅድ ነድፎ ድጋፍ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: In addition, the two institutions that have received a 2023 award are led by women. Winners of the 2023 prizes Sasakawa Health PrizeThe 2023 laureates: The Nick Simons Institute from Nepal, and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand The Nick Simons Institute is a nongovernmental organization aiming to improve health care in the rural areas of Nepal. Working closely with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the Institute has trained 7000 medical professionals (such as midwives and anaesthetic assistants) in rural areas, who now provide most-needed services to local communities in the absence of a sufficient pool of general practitioners and specialists. The Institute has supplied essential equipment and provided additional health personnel to alleviate the lack of medical professionals in 44 hospitals, and supported the design of an action plan for better quality services in more than 100 rural hospitals.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Furthermore, two institutions that received awards in 2023 are led by women. These are the winners of the 2023 "Sasakawa" Health Prize: the "Nick Simons" Institute from Nepal and Professor Vichai Tientavorapong from Thailand. The "Nick Simons" Institute is a non-governmental organization established in Nepal to improve healthcare in remote areas. Working closely with Nepal's Ministry of Public Health, the institute has trained 7,000 healthcare professionals (such as nurse midwives and dispensing pharmacists) in rural areas; these professionals are invaluable in areas lacking general practitioners and specialists. The institute has provided essential supplies and additional healthcare professionals to address the shortage of healthcare workers in 44 hospitals, and has developed and supported operational plans to enable over 100 rural hospitals to provide higher quality services."

translations.source: ተቋሙ በኔፓል በሚገኙ የገጠር የጤና ሰራተኞች ላይ ተጽእኖ የሚያሳድሩ አመለካከቶችን እና ፖሊሲዎችን ለመቅረጽ የምርምር እንቅስቃሴዎችን ያካሂዳል። ፕሮፌሰር ቪቻይ ቲየንታቮርን በታይላንድ የህዝብ ጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር የ”ፕራቦራማራጅቻኖክ” (Praboromarajchanok) ተቋም ፕሬዝዳንት ናቸው። ከመሯቸው ሀገር አቀፍ ውጥኖች አንዱ ታላሴሚያን (በዘር የሚተላለፍ የቀይ የደም ሴሎችን የሚያጠቃ በሽታ) ለመቅረፍ የሚረዳ አዲስ የጤና ፖሊሲ ሲሆን በስራው ውጤትም በሽታውን ለመከላከል እና ለመቆጣጠር ውጤታማ የሆነ ሀገራዊ ፖሊሲ ቀርቧል። በተጨማሪም የስኳር በሽታ እና የደም ግፊትን ለመከላከልና ለመቆጣጠር ዜጎች አመጋገብ እና አኗኗራቸውን እንዲያስተካክሉ የሚረዳ ውጤታማ የቅደመ ምርመራ ዘዴ ነድፏል። በዚህም ጥረት ምክኒያት በሀገሪቱ የጤና ገጽታ ላይ ከፍተኛ አወንታዊ ለውጦችን በማምጣት ሀገራዊ ፖሊሲ ተነድፏል።
translations.reference: The Institute also conducts research and advocacy activities to shape perceptions and influence policies that affect rural health workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is President of the Praboromarajchanok Institute at the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand. One of the nation-wide initiatives he has led is an innovative health promotion policy to help address thalassemia, with his work resulting in the adoption of an effective national policy for thalassemia prevention and control. He also designed an effective communications tool and screening method to help citizens to adapt their behaviours in order to prevent and control diabetes and hypertension. As a result of this work, a national policy was adopted with significant positive impact on the country’s disease burden.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The institution conducts research activities to formulate perspectives and policies that impact rural health workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tientavoranont is the President of the "Praboromarajchanok" (Praboromarajchanok) Institution of the Thailand Ministry of Public Health. One of his national initiatives is a new health policy to address thalasseamia (a genetic disease affecting red blood cells), and as a result of his work, an effective national policy for preventing and controlling the disease has been presented. Furthermore, he has developed an effective screening method that helps citizens adjust their diet and lifestyle to prevent and control diabetes and high blood pressure. As a result of this effort, significant positive changes have been made in the country's health landscape, leading to the development of national policies."

translations.source: ፕሮፌሰር ቲየንታቮርን በተጨማሪም በተለይ በታይላንድ ድንበር እና ገጠራማ አካባቢዎች የጤና እንክብካቤ ሰራተኞችን ያልተመጣጠነ ምደባ ችግር ለመፍታት ወሳኝ ሚና ተጫውተዋል ። የሁለተኛ ደረጃ ተማሪዎችን በመመልመል፣ ሥልጠና እና የተፋጠነ የሕክምና ሥርዓተ ትምህርት በመስጠት ወደ ማህበረሰቡ እንዲቀላቀሉ በማድረግ በመንደር ከሚገኙ ትምህርት ቤቶች ጋር አብረው ሰርተዋል። የተባበሩት አረብ ኤሚሬቶች የጤና ፋውንዴሽን ሽልማት እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተቀባይ፡ ዶ/ር ማሪያ አሱንሲዮን ሲልቬስትሬ ሲሆኑ በፊሊፒንስ የታወቁ ሃኪም እና ተመራማሪ ከመሆናቸው በተጨማሪ፣ ዶ/ር ማሪያ ልጆችን እስከ ስድስት ወር ድረስ የእናት ጡትን ብቻ ማጥባት ተከራካሪ እንዲሁም የ”Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina” (የእናት እና ልጅ ጤና) - በፊሊፒንስ ውስጥ መንግስታዊ ያልሆነ ድርጅት መስራች ናቸው። ዶ/ር ሲልቬስትሬ እንደ ማህበረሰብ ባለሙያ ያላትን ልምድ ምርምር ለማድረግ እና የእናቶችን እና ጨቅላ ሕፃናትን ጤና ለማሻሻል ያለመ ፕሮቶኮል ለማዘጋጀት ተጠቅማለች። እሷ ያዘጋጀችው የፈጠራ ፕሮቶኮል እናቶችን በወሊድ ወቅት እንዲሁም ጨቅላ ሕፃናትን ከወሊድ በኋላ ለሚከታተሉ የጤና አጠባበቅ ሠራተኞች ቀላል እርምጃዎችን ይዘረዝራል።
translations.reference: Professor Tienthavorn also played an essential role in addressing the challenge of uneven distribution of the health workforce, especially in border and rural areas of Thailand. He led the establishment of partnerships with village schools to recruit high-school students, train them and help them settle back locally after having accomplished an accelerated medical curriculum. United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Prize2023 award laureate: Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre from the PhilippinesIn addition to being a renowned clinician and researcher, Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre is a passionate advocate for exclusive breastfeeding and the founder of Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Health of Mother and Child) – a Philippine non-governmental organization. Dr Silvestre has leveraged her experience as a community practitioner to conduct research and design a protocol to help improve the health of mothers and newborns. The pioneering protocol she created describes a simple set of actions to be undertaken by health workers attending a mother during delivery and her newborn after birth.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Professor Tientavorn has also played a crucial role in addressing the problem of unequal distribution of healthcare workers, particularly in border and remote areas of Thailand. He has worked alongside schools in rural areas by enrolling, training, and integrating secondary school students into the community through a dispersed medical education system. The United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Award recipient for 2023 is Dr. Maria Asuncion Silvestre. In addition to being a renowned physician and researcher in the Philippines, Dr. Maria is a staunch advocate for exclusive breastfeeding for infants up to six months of age and the founder of "Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina" (Mother and Child Health) – a non-governmental organization in the Philippines. Dr. Silvestre has utilized her experience as a community expert to conduct research and develop protocols aimed at improving the health of mothers and young children. The innovative protocol she developed outlines simple steps for healthcare workers who monitor mothers during childbirth and young children after childbirth."

translations.source: ይህ ፕሮቶኮል በሁሉም ሁኔታዎች ውስጥ ተፈጻሚ ሊሆን የሚችል ሲሆን በእናቶች እና ህጻናት ጤና አጠባበቅ ፍትሃዊነት ላይ ያሉትን ክፍተቶች ለማስተካከል ይረዳል። ከፊሊፒንስ መንግስት እና ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምዕራባዊ ፓሲፊክ ክልል አካባቢ ካሉ 17 ሀገራት ጋር በመተባበር ለጨቅላ ህጻናት እንክብካቤ የመጀመሪያዉን ዘመቻ በማካሄድ ረገድ አስተዋፅዖ አበርክታለች፤ ይህም በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የሚመከር እና ወጪ ቆጣቢ የሆነ መፍትሄ ነው። የተከበሩ ሼክ ሳባህ አል-አህመድ አል-ጀብር አል-ሳባህ በአረጋውያን ጤና አጠባበቅ እና ጤናማነትን ማስተዋወቅ ላይ ያበረከቱት ሽልማት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚዎች፦ የቻይናው ሥር የሰደዱ እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች መቆጣጠሪያ እና መከላከያ ብሔራዊ ማዕከል እና ዶክተር አብላ መሂዮ ሲባይ ከሊባኖስ ናቸው። የቻይና የበሽታ መቆጣጠሪያና መከላከያ ማዕከል አካል የሆነው ሥር የሰደዱ እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች መቆጣጠሪያ እና መከላከያ ብሔራዊ ማዕከል እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2013 ጀምሮ የአረጋውያንን ጤና በማስተዋወቅ ረገድ ንቁ ተሳትፎ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: This protocol, which is applicable in all resource settings, helps to bridge the health equity gap in mother-and-child care. In partnership with the national government of the Philippines as well as 17 countries in the WHO Western Pacific Region, she has played a key role in scaling up the First Embrace campaign for Early Essential Newborn Care – a set of simple WHO-recommended and cost-effective interventions. His Highness Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Prize for Research in Health Care for the Elderly and in Health Promotion 2023 award laureates: The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention from China; and Dr Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention, part of the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), has hosted a department focusing on the health of older people since 2013.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"This protocol can be applied in all situations and helps to address gaps in the fairness of maternal and child health care. In collaboration with the Philippine government and 17 countries from the World Health Organization's Western Pacific Region, it has made a contribution to conducting the first campaign for the care of young children, which is a recommended and cost-effective solution by the World Health Organization. The award presented to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah for his contribution to the promotion of the health and well-being of the elderly; the winners for 2023 are the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention of Non-Communicable and Communicable Diseases and Dr. Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon. The Center for Disease Control and Prevention of Non-Communicable and Communicable Diseases, which is part of the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention, has been actively involved in promoting the health of the elderly since 2013."

translations.source: ይህ ማዕከል የተለያዩ ፕሮግራሞችን እና ተነሳሽነቶችን ተግባራዊ ያደርጋል፤ ከነዚህም መካከል በአገር አቀፍ ክትትል እና ጥናቶች የበሽታዎችን መንስኤ መረዳት እንዲሁም በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረተ ፖሊሲዎችን ጨምሮ የጤና ማስተዋወቅን ማስፋፍት ይጠቀሳሉ። አዳዲስ ተነሳሽነቶቹም የሚከተሉትን ያጠቃልላሉ፤ ለአረጋውያን የሚሆን ልዩ መሣሪያ፣ ለ2000 የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ እንክብካቤ ሠራተኞች ሥልጠና፣ የአረጋዊያንን ጤና ማስተዋወቂያ ሳምንት እንዲሁም በከተማ እና በገጠር የሚኖሩ ከግማሽ ሚሊዮን በላይ የሚሆኑ አዛውንቶችን የአእምሮ ጤና ለማሳደግ የሚያስችል አዲስ ፕሮጀክት ያካትታሉ። ዶ/ር አብላ ሜሂዮ ሲባይ በቤይሩት ሊባኖስ የሚገኘው አሜሪካ ዩኒቨርሲቲ የ”Epidemiology” (የበሽታ ስርጭት ጥናት) ፕሮፌሰር እና የጤና ሳይንስ ፋኩልቲ ዲን ናቸው። በሊባኖስ እና በአካባቢው ላሉ አረጋውያን ጤና ከፍተኛ አስተዋፅዖ አበርክተዋል። ጤናማ እርጅናን ለማስፋፋት ሁለንተናዊ አቀራረብን አስተዋውቃለች፣ ይህም በየዓመቱ ወደ 600 የሚጠጉ አረጋዊያንን በቤሩት የአሜሪካ ዩኒቨርሲቲ መመዝገብ እና የህዝብ ጤና ባለሙያዎችን ማሰልጠንን ጨምሮ ነው።
translations.reference: It delivers a broad range of programmes and activities that advance health promotion for older people, ranging from better understanding the causes of morbidity and mortality through national surveillance for noncommunicable diseases, surveys, death registries and research, to tailored programmes to promote health literacy and evidence-based policies. Initiatives include a dedicated toolkit for older people, capacity‑building of 2000 primary care staff, a health advocacy week dedicated to older people, and an innovative project to improve the mental and cognitive health of more than half a million older persons in both urban and rural areas. Dr Abla Mehio Sibai is Professor of Epidemiology and Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made an outstanding contribution to older people’s health in Lebanon and the region, developing a holistic approach to the promotion of healthy ageing. She led the innovative initiative of enrolling close to 600 older people every year at the University for Seniors at the American University of Beirut, in addition to training public health professionals.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"This center implements various programs and initiatives, including understanding the causes of diseases through nationwide surveillance and studies, and expanding health promotion, including evidence-based policies. The new initiatives also include a special tool for the elderly, training for 2000 primary care workers, a week of health promotion for the elderly, and a new project to improve the mental health of over half a million older adults living in urban and rural areas. Dr. Abla Mehio Sibai is a professor of "Epidemiology" (Disease Transmission Study) and Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut, Lebanon. She has made a significant contribution to the health of the elderly in Lebanon and the region. She has introduced a holistic approach to promoting healthy aging, which includes registering approximately 600 elderly people annually at the American University of Beirut and training public health professionals."

translations.source: ዶ/ር ሲባይ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በሊባኖስ የዓለም አቀፍ የበሽታ ጥናትን መርተዋል (እ.ኤ.አ. በ2000) እና ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአደጋ መንስኤዎች ላይ የመጀመሪያውን ሀገር አቀፍ የዳሰሳ ጥናት፣ የፖሊሲ ማውጣት እና ጤና ማስተዋወቅ የፕሮግራም እቅድ አዘጋጅተዋል። በርካታ ስኬቶቿን በማስፋት፣ ዶ/ር ሲባይ በሊባኖስ (እ.ኤ.አ. 2020–2030) “የአረጋውያን ብሄራዊ ስትራቴጂ” ቀርጻለች። ዶ/ር ሲባይ በአከባቢ፣ በክልላዊ እና በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ በምርምር እና በፖሊሲ መካከል ያለውን ልዩነት ለማስተካከል የእርጅና ጥናት ማዕከል “Centre for Studies on Ageing” እና “Arab Ageing Network” አቋቁሟል። የዶ/ር ሊ ጆንግ-ዎክ ለሕዝብ ጤና የመታሰቢያ ሽልማት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚ፦ ዶ/ር ሆርጅ ፍራንሲስኮ መንሴስ ከጓቲማላ
translations.reference: She co-led the landmark WHO Global Burden of Disease Study in Lebanon (2000) and conducted the first national survey on noncommunicable diseases and risk factors, informing advocacy, policy-making and programme planning for health promotion. Building on her numerous achievements, Dr Sibai has developed the “National Strategy for Older Persons” in Lebanon (2020–2030). Dr Sibai also created the Arab Ageing Network and the Centre for Studies on Ageing to create links between research and policy at the national, regional and international levels. Dr LEE Jong-wook Memorial Prize for Public Health 2023 award laureate: Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses from Guatemala
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Dr. Sibai evaluated the World Health Organization's international disease studies in Lebanon (in 2000) and prepared the first national survey study on non-communicable diseases and determinants of health, policy development, and health promotion program planning. Expanding on numerous successes, Dr. Sibai developed Lebanon's "National Strategy for the Elderly" (2020–2030). Dr. Sibai established the "Centre for Studies on Ageing" and the "Arab Ageing Network" to bridge the gap between research and policy at the local, regional, and international levels. The Dr. Lee Jong-wook Memorial Prize for Public Health; Winner for 2023: Dr. Horacio Francisco Mansés from Guatemala.

translations.source: ከሁለት አስርት ዓመታት በላይ የማህበረሰብን ተሳትፎ በሆስፒታል እንክብካቤ ውስጥ በማስተዋወቅ ባላቸው ልምድ፣ ዶ/ር ሆርጅ ፍራንሲስኮ ሜነስ በታካሚዎች፣ በቤተሰቦቻቸው እና በማህበረሰቡ ደህንነት እና እርካታ ላይ ያተኮሩ ማህበረሰቡን ያማከሉ ኮሚቴዎችን መስርተዋል። የታካሚ እንክብካቤ ጥራትን ለማሳደግ ያደረገው ጥረት ያመጣውን አወንታዊ ተፅእኖ በመገንዘብ የነደፈው ታካሚን ማዕከል ያደረገ የእንክብካቤ ሞዴል በአገር አቀፍ ደረጃ ተተግብሯል። በአሁኑ ጊዜ በጓቲማላ የብሔራዊ ሆስፒታል ተቆጣጣሪ ሆኖ በማገልገል ላይ ሲሆን፣ ለታካሚዎች እና ለቤተሰቦቻቸው የአገልግሎት ጥራትን ለማሳደግ ያለመ ፕሮግራም አዘጋጅቷል። ዶ/ር መንሴ በቅርቡ በደቡብ ጓቲማላ አካባቢ የሀገሪቱ የመጀመሪያ የሆነውን የተቀናጀ የጤና አገልግሎት መረብ ለመመስረት የሚያስችል የፈጠራ ተነሳሽነትን መርተዋል፤ በዚህም አገልግሎት ለማያገኙ የማህበረሰብ ክፍሎች ቅድሚያ ሰጥተዋል። የእሳቸው ተነሳሽነት በመካከለኛው አሜሪካ የመጀመሪያው ብሔራዊ የተቀናጀ የጤና አገልግሎት አውታር እንዲፀድቅ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: Having promoted community participation in hospital care for over 20 years, Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses set up community-based committees focusing on the safety and satisfaction of patients, their families and the community. Given the positive impact of his work in improving the quality of patient care, the model of ethical and patient-centred care that he designed was scaled up at the national level. Nowadays, as the national supervisor for hospitals of Guatemala, he has designed a programme to improve the quality of services for hospital patients and their families. Dr Meneses has recently coordinated an innovative pilot project in the Guatemala South Health Area to build the first national integrated network of health services, with a priority focus on underserved communities. His work led to the adoption of a national strategy of integrated health services networks, the first in Central America.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"With over two decades of experience in promoting community participation in hospital care, Dr. Horacio Francisco Ménez has established community-centered committees focused on the safety and satisfaction of patients, their families, and the community. Recognizing the positive impact of his efforts to improve the quality of patient care, a patient-centered care model he developed has been implemented nationwide. Currently serving as the National Hospital Supervisor in Guatemala, he has designed a program aimed at improving the quality of service for patients and their families. Dr. Ménez recently spearheaded an innovative initiative to establish the country's first integrated health service network in southern Guatemala, prioritizing service to underserved communities. His initiative led to the approval of the first national integrated health network in Central America."

translations.source: የኔልሰን ማንዴላ የጤና ሽልማት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚ፦ ዶ/ር ማርያም አትቢ አል ጃላህማ ከባህሬን። የባህሬን ብሔራዊ የጤና ቁጥጥር ባለስልጣን ዋና ስራ አስፈፃሚ ሆነው ከተጫወቱት ሚና ጎን ለጎን ዶ/ር ማሪያም አቲቢ አል ጃላህማ በሀገራቸው ውስጥ ባሉ የመንግስት እና የትምህርት ተቋማት ውስጥ ቁልፍ የአመራር ቦታዎች ላይ ሠርተዋል። ዶ/ር አል ጃላህማ በመጀመሪያ ደረጃ የጤና እንክብካቤ፣ የህጻናት እና የጉርምስና ዕድሜ ጤና፣ ሥር የሰደዱ በሽታዎች እና ማጨስ ማቆም ላይ ያተኮሩ በርካታ ሀገራዊ እና ክልላዊ ስልቶችን እና እቅዶችን በመቅረጽ ትልቅ አስተዋፅዖ አድርገዋል። ዶ/ር አል ጃላህማ አሁን ባለችበት የስራ መደብ ለጤና ተቋማት ብሄራዊ እውቅና አሰጣጥ ስርዓት ትግበራ፣ የብሄራዊ ጤና ቁጥጥር ባለስልጣን ወደ ዲጂታል አሰራር ለውጥ እንዲሁም በጤና አጠባበቅ ዘርፍ ውስጥ ቅልጥፍና እና የላቀ ደረጃ ላይ ያተኮሩ የተለያዩ ውጤታማ ስራዎችን በመምራት ላይ ትገኛለች። .
translations.reference: Nelson Mandela Award for Health Promotion 2023 award laureate: Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma from Bahrain In addition to being the Chief Executive Officer of the National Health Regulatory Authority of Bahrain, Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma has held various leadership positions in government and academic institutions in her country. Dr Al Jalahma has played a major role in the development of numerous national and region-wide strategies and plans in the field of primary healthcare, child and adolescent health, chronic diseases and antismoking. In her current role, Dr Al Jalahma has led major successful initiatives, such as the national accreditation system for health institutions, the digital transformation of the National Health Regulatory Authority, and several initiatives leading to increased efficiency and excellence in the health sector.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

**The Nelson Mandela Health Prize; Winner for 2023: Dr. Mariam Atbi Al-Jalahma from Bahrain.**

Alongside her role as CEO of the Bahrain National Health Regulatory Authority, Dr. Mariam Atbi Al-Jalahma has served in key leadership positions within government and educational institutions in her country. Dr. Al-Jalahma has made a significant contribution by formulating numerous national and regional strategies and plans primarily focused on healthcare, child and adolescent health, non-communicable diseases, and smoking cessation. Currently, in her position, Dr. Al-Jalahma is leading various effective initiatives focused on national accreditation of health institutions, the transition of the National Health Regulatory Authority to a digital system, and efficiency and advanced levels within the healthcare sector.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ (የባዮሎጂ ስርዓቶችን በመጠቀም ምርትን ማምረት) አቅምን ለማሳደግ ከፍተኛ ስምምነት ላይ ደርሰዋል። በባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ (የባዮሎጂ ስርዓቶችን በመጠቀም ምርትን ማምረት) ላይ ያተኮረ ዓለም አቀፍ የሥልጠና ማዕከል ለመፍጠር የመግባቢያ ስምምነት ተፈራርመዋል። ይህ የሥልጠና ተቋም ሁሉንም ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸውን አገሮች ያገለግላል፣ ይህም እንደ ክትባቶች፣ ኢንሱሊን፣ antibodies (ያልተፈለገ ንጥረ ነገር ወደ ሰውነት ሲገባ የሚከላከሉ ፕሮቲኖች) እና የካንሰር መድኃኒቶች ያሉ ምርቶችን እንዲያመርቱ ያስችላቸዋል። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ አስፈላጊ የጤና ምርቶችን ፍትሃዊ ተደራሽነት ለማረጋገጥ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ የአካባቢ እና ክልላዊ የክትባት ምርትን የማስፋፋት አስፈላጊነት አሳይቷል። የmRNA (በሴሎች ውስጥ መልዕክትን አስተላላፊ ፕሮቲን) ክትባቶችን ለማምረት የሚያስችል እውቀት ለሃገሮች ለማጋራት፤ በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ከአጋር ድርጅቶች ጋር በመተባበር የmRNA ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር መርሃ ግብርን በደቡብ አፍሪካ ማዕከል እ.ኤ.አ. በሚያዝያ 2023 በይፋ ተመርቋል።
translations.reference: WHO and Republic of Korea sign landmark agreement to boost biomanufacturing capacity WHO and the Republic of Korea have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to establish a global training hub in biomanufacturing. This global training centre will serve all low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) to produce biologicals, such as vaccines, insulin, monoclonal antibodies, and cancer medicines. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need to expand the local and regional production of vaccines and other essential health products in LMICs to ensure equitable access. To provide countries with the know-how to rapidly produce mRNA vaccines, WHO, in collaboration with partners, established the mRNA Technology Transfer programme, with the formal inauguration of the Hub facility in South Africa in April 2023.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

The World Health Organization (WHO) and the Republic of Korea have reached a high level of agreement to increase biomanufacturing capacity (producing products using biological systems). A memorandum of understanding has been signed to create an international training center focused on biomanufacturing (producing products using biological systems). This training institution will serve all low- and middle-income countries, enabling them to produce products such as vaccines, insulin, antibodies (proteins that protect the body when an unwanted substance enters), and cancer medications. The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the need to expand local and regional vaccine production in low- and middle-income countries to ensure equitable access to essential health products. To share the knowledge needed to produce mRNA (messenger protein that transmits messages within cells) vaccines, the WHO officially launched the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub, in collaboration with partner organizations, in South Africa in April 2023.

translations.source: ይህ የጋራ ተነሳሽነት እና ሌሎች የቅርብ ጊዜ የሀገር ውስጥ የምርት ውጥኖች በምርት ውስጥ ልዩ ችሎታ ያላቸው በእነዚህ ዘርፎች ውስጥ የሚሰሩ ሰራተኞችን ለመቅጠር፣ ለማሰልጠን እና የተካኑ ሰራተኞችን ለማቆየት አጠቃላይ የሰው ሃይል ልማት ስትራቴጂዎችን ይፈልጋል። ይህንን የክህሎት ክፍተት ለመቅረፍ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ ከአንድ አመት በላይ በቅርበት ሲሰሩ የቆዩ ሲሁን ይህም አለምአቀፍ የስልጠና ማዕከል ለመመስረት ያለመ ነው። "ይህንን የመግባቢያ ሰነድ እውን በማድረግ፣ አጋርነታችንን ወደ ላቀ ደረጃ በማሸጋገር እና አጠቃላይ የሥልጠና ሥርዓተ ትምህርት በማዘጋጀት ላይ በመተባበራችን በጣም ደስተኞች ነን። አላማችን ይህ ከፍተኛ ደረጃ ያለው የስልጠና ማዕከል፤ የሰለጠነ የሰው ሀይል ማፍራት እና ጥራት ያለው የጤና ምርቶችን በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ማምረት ማስቻል ነው።" ሲሉ የመድሀኒት እና የጤና ምርቶች ተደራሽነት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ዩኪኮ ናካታኒ ተናግረዋል። የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ መንግሥት አጠቃላይ ባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግን (የባዮሎጂ ስርዓቶችን በመጠቀም ምርትን ማምረት) የሚሸፍኑ በርካታ ብሔራዊ የሥልጠና ተቋማትን እና መርሃ ግብሮችን እያቀረበ ሲሆን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 ሊጀመር በተያዘው ዓለም አቀፍ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ ትምህርት ቤት ግንባታ ሲጠናቀቅ ከሌሎች ሀገራት ብዙ ሰልጣኞችን ለማስተናገድ ተደራሽነቱን በማስፋት ላይ ይገኛል።
translations.reference: This multilateral effort and other recent local production initiatives call for ambitious workforce development strategies to recruit, upskill and retain the staff working at these facilities, with unique competencies required in bioproduction. To address this gap, WHO and the Republic of Korea have engaged in closed collaboration for over a year and are now formalizing their commitment towards the establishment of a global training hub in biomanufacturing. “We are delighted to sign this Memorandum of Understanding to elevate our partnership to the next level and collaborate in the development of a comprehensive training curriculum. We hope this world-class training hub will help to build a skilled workforce and ensure quality health products can be manufactured around the world,” said Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General, Access to Medicines and Health Products. The Government of the Republic of Korea is offering a large range of national training facilities and programmes covering the full spectrum of biological manufacturing and is expanding its operations to accommodate more trainees from other countries with the construction of a Global Bio Education Campus to be launched in 2025.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"This joint initiative and other recent national product agreements seek comprehensive human resource development strategies to recruit, train, and retain skilled workers with specialized expertise in production within these sectors. To address this skills gap, the World Health Organization and the Republic of Korea have been working closely together for over a year to establish an international training center. "We are delighted to be collaborating to this level by realizing this memorandum of understanding, developing a comprehensive training system. Our goal is to create a high-quality training center; to produce a skilled workforce and enable the production of quality health products on a global scale," said Dr. Yukiko Nakatani, Director of Access to Medicines and Health Products. The Republic of Korea's government is offering several national training institutions and programs covering the entire biomanufacturing (producing products using biological systems) spectrum, and is expanding its reach to accommodate many trainees from other countries as the International Biomanufacturing School, planned to be completed in 2025, is finalized."

translations.source: "ይህ የመግባቢያ ስምምነት በኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት መካከል ያለውን ትብብር እንደሚያጠናክር አምናለሁ፣ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸውን አገሮችን የማምረት አቅም ያሳድጋል።" ያሉት የኮሪያ ሪፐብሊክ የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴርና የዓለም አቀፍ የክትባት ማዕከል ዋና ዳይሬክተር የሆኑት ሁዋንግ ሴንጊዮን ናቸው። በተመሳሳይም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የቁጥጥር ስርዓቶችን ለማጠናከር እንዲሁም የጤና ምርቶችን ጥራት እና ደህንነት ለማረጋገጥ ጥረቶችን በማድረግ ላይ ይገኛል። የተጠናከሩ የቁጥጥር ተቋማት መኖር በአገር ውስጥ በተመረቱ ምርቶች ላይ እምነትን ያሳድጋል እንዲሁም የተሳሳቱ፣ ደህንነታቸውን እና ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ ወይም የተጭበረበሩ መድሃኒቶችን ለማስቆም ይጠቅማል።
translations.reference: “I hope this Memorandum of Understanding will enhance cooperation between the Republic of Korea and WHO to strengthen biomanufacturing capacities of LMICs,” said Mr Hwang Seunghyun Director General, Global Vaccine Hub Office at the Ministry of Health and Welfare from Republic of Korea. In parallel, WHO is intensifying regulatory system strengthening to ensure the quality and safety of health products. Stronger regulatory agencies will also enhance confidence in locally produced products and counter misinformation and the availability of unsafe substandard or falsified medicines.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"I believe this memorandum of understanding will strengthen the cooperation between the Republic of Korea and the World Health Organization, and enhance the manufacturing capacity of low- and middle-income countries," said Huang Seung-geon, Minister of Health and Welfare of the Republic of Korea and Director-General of the International Vaccine Center. Similarly, the World Health Organization is working to strengthen regulatory systems and ensure the quality and safety of health products. The existence of strengthened regulatory institutions builds confidence in domestically produced products and helps to prevent substandard, unsafe, or counterfeit medicines."

translations.source: የአለም ጤና ድርጅት መንግስታት ለትምባሆ እርሻ የሚያደርጉትን ድጎማ እንዲያቆሙ ጠይቋል። በአለም የትምባሆ አልባ ቀን፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ለትምባሆ ልማት የሚሰጠውን የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እንዲያቆሙ እና በምትኩ በሚሊዮኖች ለሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ምግብ የሚያቀርቡ ተጨማሪ ዘላቂ ሰብሎችን እንዲያስተዋውቁ አሳስቧል። "ትንባሆ በዓመት 8 ሚሊዮን ሰዎችን ይገድላል፣ ነገር ግን በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ መንግስታት ለትንባሆ ልማት በሚሊዮኖች የሚቆጠሩ የገንዘብ ድጋፎችን ያደርጋሉ።" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ተናግረዋል። "ከትንባሆ ይልቅ የምግብ ሰብሎችን በማምረት ለጤና ቅድሚያ እንሰጣለን፣ ስነ-ምህዳርን እንጠብቃለን እንዲሁም ለሁሉም ሰው የምግብ ዋስትናን እናሻሽላለን።" ሲሉም አክለዋል። በዓለም ዙሪያ ከ300 ሚሊዮን በላይ ሰዎች ለከፋ የምግብ ዋስትና እጦት የተዳረጉ ናቸው።
translations.reference: WHO urges governments to stop subsidizing life-threatening tobacco crops On World No Tobacco Day, WHO urges governments to stop subsidizing tobacco farming and support more sustainable crops that could feed millions. “Tobacco is responsible for 8 million deaths a year, yet governments across the world spend millions supporting tobacco farms,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “By choosing to grow food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, preserve ecosystems, and strengthen food security for all.” More than 300 million people globally are faced with acute food insecurity.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

The World Health Organization (WHO) has called on governments to stop subsidizing tobacco farming. On World No Tobacco Day, the WHO urged them to end financial support for tobacco production and instead introduce more sustainable crops that feed millions of people. "Tobacco kills 8 million people each year, but governments around the world provide millions of dollars in subsidies for tobacco production," said WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Let's prioritize health, protect our environment, and improve food security for all by producing food crops instead of tobacco." Over 300 million people worldwide are facing severe food insecurity.

translations.source: ይህ በእንዲህ እንዳለ ከ120 በሚበልጡ አገሮች ውስጥ ከ3 ሚሊዮን ሄክታር በላይ መሬት ጎጂ የሆኑ ትምባሆዎችን ለማምረት ይውላሉ፣ ሌላው ቀርቶ ሰዎች በረሃብ በሚሰቃዩባቸው አገሮችም ጭምር። “ትምባሆ ሳይሆን፤ ምግብ እናሳድግ” በሚል ርዕስ የወጣው አዲሱ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሪፖርት የትምባሆ እርሻን ጉዳቶች እንዲሁም ወደ ዘላቂ የምግብ ሰብሎች መሸጋገር ለገበሬዎች፣ ለማህበረሰቡ፣ ለኢኮኖሚ፣ ለአካባቢ እና ለአለም ህዝብ ያለውን ፋይዳ ይዘረዝራል። ሪፖርቱ የትምባሆ ኢንዱስትሪው አርሶ አደሮችን በእዳ አዙሪት ውስጥ በማጥመድ፣ የትምባሆ ልማትን ኢኮኖሚያዊ ጠቀሜታ በማጋነን እና በግብርና ግንባር ቡድኖች በኩል በግድ እንዲያመርቱ በማስገደድ እንደሚሰራ አጋልጧል። የትምባሆ እርባታ የአርሶ አደሮችን ጤና አደጋ ላይ ይጥላል፣ ከ1 ሚሊየን በላይ ህጻናት በጉልበት ሰራተኝነት በትምባሆ እርሻ ላይ እንደሚሰሩ ይገመታል፣ በዚህም የትምህርት እድሎችን እያጡ ነው። "ትምባሆ ለምግብ ዋስትና ብቻ ሳይሆን ለአጠቃላይ ጤና፣ የትምባሆ ገበሬዎችን ጨምሮ ከፍተኛ ስጋት ይፈጥራል።
translations.reference: Meanwhile more than 3 million hectares of land across more than 120 countries are being used to grow deadly tobacco, even in countries where people are starving. A new WHO report, “Grow food, not tobacco,” highlights the ills of tobacco growing and the benefits of switching to more sustainable food crops for farmers, communities, economies, the environment, and the world at large. The report also exposes the tobacco industry for trapping farmers in a vicious cycle of debt, propagating tobacco growing by exaggerating its economic benefits and lobbying through farming front groups. Tobacco farming causes diseases to the farmers themselves and more than 1 million child laborers are estimated to be working on tobacco farms, missing their opportunity for an education. “Tobacco is not only a massive threat to food insecurity, but health overall, including the health of tobacco farmers.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"While over 120 countries allocate more than 3 million hectares of land to produce harmful tobacco, even in countries where people are suffering from hunger, a new World Health Organization report titled "Food, Not Tobacco" details the harms of tobacco farming and the benefits of transitioning to sustainable food crops for farmers, communities, the economy, the environment, and the global population. The report reveals how the tobacco industry traps farmers in debt, exaggerates the economic benefits of tobacco cultivation, and forces agricultural front-line groups to produce it. Tobacco farming endangers the health of farmers; it is estimated that over 1 million children are forced into labor on tobacco farms, depriving them of educational opportunities. "Tobacco poses a threat not only to food security but also to overall health, particularly for tobacco farmers."

translations.source: አርሶ አደሮች ለኬሚካላዊ ፀረ ተባይ መድኃኒቶች፣ የትምባሆ ጭስ እና በ50 ሲጋራዎች ውስጥ ለሚገኘው ኒኮቲን ተጋልጠዋል፤ ይህም እንደ ሥር የሰደደ የሳንባ ሕመም እና የኒኮቲን መመረዝ ላሉ በሽታዎች ይዳርጋል።” ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የጤና ማስተዋወቅ ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ሩዲገር ክሬች አብራርተዋል። የትምባሆ ልማት ዓለም አቀፍ ችግር ነው። ትኩረቱ በአብዛኛው በእስያ እና በደቡብ አሜሪካ ላይ ቢሆንም፣ የቅርብ ጊዜ መረጃዎች እንደሚያሳዩት የትምባሆ አምራች ድርጅቶች አሁን ሥራቸውን ወደ አፍሪካ እያስፋፉ ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2005 ጀምሮ በመላው አፍሪካ የትምባሆ እርሻ መሬት ስፋት 20 በመቶ ገደማ ጨምሯል። በኬንያ እና በዛምቢያ የሚገኙ ከ5000 በላይ አርሶ አደሮች ከትንባሆ ይልቅ ወደ ዘላቂ የምግብ ሰብሎች እንዲሸጋገሩ ለማድረግ ያለመ ከትንባሆ ነፃ እርሻዎች ተነሳሽነትን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፣ የምግብ እና የእርሻ ድርጅት እንዲሁም የዓለም የምግብ ፕሮግራም ድጋፍ እየሰጡ ይገኛሉ።
translations.reference: Farmers are exposed to chemical pesticides, tobacco smoke and as much nicotine as found in 50 cigarettes – leading to illnesses like chronic lung conditions and nicotine poisoning,” said Dr Ruediger Krech, Director of Health Promotion at WHO. Tobacco growing is a global problem. The focus has so far been in Asia and South America, but the latest data show tobacco companies are expanding to Africa. Since 2005, there has been a nearly 20% increase in tobacco farming land across Africa. WHO, the Food and Agriculture Organization and the World Food Programme support the Tobacco Free Farms initiative that will provide help to more than 5000 farmers in Kenya and Zambia to grow sustainable food crops instead of tobacco.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Farmers are exposed to chemical pesticides, tobacco smoke, and nicotine found in 50 cigarettes, which can lead to diseases such as root-based lung disease and nicotine intolerance," explained Dr. Rudiger Krech, Director of Health Promotion at the World Health Organization. Tobacco development is a global problem. While the focus has largely been on Asia and South America, recent data shows that tobacco manufacturing companies are now expanding their operations to Africa. Since 2005, the area of land used for tobacco farming has increased by approximately 20 percent across Africa. The World Health Organization, the Food and Agriculture Organization, and the World Food Programme are supporting a tobacco-free farming initiative aimed at encouraging more than 5,000 farmers in Kenya and Zambia to switch to sustainable food crops instead of tobacco.

translations.source: በየዓመቱ የዓለም “ትምባሆ የሌለበት ቀን” ትንባሆን ለመቆጣጠር በሚደረገው ጥረት አስተዋጽኦ የሚያደርጉ ግለሰቦችን እና ድርጅቶችን አውቀና ይሰጣል። በዚህ አመት ከተሸላሚዎቹ መካከል ወይዘሮ ስፕሪና ሮቢ ቻቻ የተባሉት ኬንያዊት ሴት አርሶ አደር አንዷ ሲሆኑ ከትንባሆ እርባታ ወደ ከፍተኛ ባቄላ ልማት በመሸጋገር እንዲሁም በመቶዎች የሚቆጠሩ አርሶ አደሮችን ተመሳሳይ ስራ እንዲሰሩ በማሰልጠን ሽልማት ተሰጥቷቸዋል። 182 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባላት “ለትንባሆ ሠራተኞች እና አብቃዮች አዋጭ ኢኮኖሚያዊ አማራጮችን ለማስተዋወቅ” ቃል ገብተዋል። ይህንን ቁርጠኝነት የሚወጡበት ቁልፍ መንገድ ለትምባሆ ልማት የሚደረገውን ድጎማ ማቋረጥ እና በምትኩ ጤናማ ሰብሎችን ማምረትን በመደገፍ ነው። ከትንባሆ ይልቅ ምግብ ለማምረት በመምረጥ ለጤንነት ቅድሚያ እንሰጣለን፤ ስነ-ምህዳሮችን እንጠብቃለን እናም የምግብ ዋስትናን እናሳድጋለን።
translations.reference: Every year World No Tobacco Day honours those making a difference in tobacco control. This year one of the Awardees, Ms Sprina Robi Chacha, a female farmer from Kenya, is being recognized for not only switching from growing tobacco to high protein beans, but also training hundreds of other farmers on how to do this to create a healthier community. 182 Parties to the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control have committed to “...promote economically viable alternatives for tobacco workers and growers”. A crucial way that countries can fulfill this obligation is by ending subsidies for tobacco growing and supporting healthier crops. By choosing to grow food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, preserve ecosystems, and increase food security.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Every year, World "No Tobacco Day" recognizes and honors individuals and organizations contributing to the effort of controlling tobacco. Among this year's winners is Mrs. Sprina Robi Chacha, a Kenyan farmer, who has been awarded for transitioning from tobacco farming to high-yield bean cultivation and for training hundreds of other farmers to do the same. 182 World Health Organization member states have pledged "to promote viable economic alternatives for tobacco workers and farmers." A key pathway to fulfilling this commitment is to end subsidies for tobacco cultivation and instead support the production of healthy crops. By choosing to produce food instead of tobacco, we prioritize health, protect ecosystems, and build food security."

translations.source: ኢኮኖሚን እንዴት መቀየር እንደሚቻል የሚያሳይ ጠቃሚ ሪፖርት፤ ይህም ሁሉም ሰው ጥሩ የጤና እንክብካቤ እንዲያገኝ ለማረጋገጥ ነው። በዓይነቱ ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ ባዘጋጀው ሪፖርቱ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምክር ቤት ለሁሉም ማህበረሰብ የጤና ተደራሽነትን ለማረጋገጥ ኢኮኖሚን እንደገና ለማዋቀር ቆራጥነት የተሞላው አዲስ መንገድን አቅርቧል። በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ እ.ኤ.አ. በህዳር 2020 ለኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምላሽ ለመስጠት የተቋቋመው እና በፕሮፌሰር ማሪያና ማዙካቶ የሚመራው ምክር ቤቱ ያለፉትን ሁለት አመታት ለሁሉም ህብረተሰብ የጤና ተደራሽነት ለማረጋገጥ ሲባል ኢኮኖሚውን በመገምገም አሳልፏል፤ የሰዎች እና የዓለማችን ደህንነት የማህበራዊ፣ የጤና እና የኢኮኖሚ ስርዓታችን እንዲሁም የፖሊሲያችን የመሰረት ድንጋይ መሆን አለበት በሚል መርህም ይመራል ። ምክር ቤቱ ከታች በተዘረዘሩት አራት ተያያዥ ጉዳዮች ላይ በማተኮር ኢኮኖሚውን ለመቀየር በአዲስ እውቀት ላይ የተመሰረተ ቆራጥነት የተሞላው አዲስ መንገድን አቅርቧል።
translations.reference: Landmark report charts route for reorienting economies to deliver health for all In the first-ever report of its kind, the WHO Council on the Economics of Health for All has outlined a bold new path to reorient economies to deliver what matters - health for all. The Council, created by WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus in November 2020 in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, and chaired by Professor Mariana Mazzucato, has spent that last two years rethinking the economy from a health for all perspective, and pushing forcefully the principle that human and planetary health must be at the heart of how we design our social, health and economic systems and policies. The Council has put forward a bold new narrative grounded in new economic wisdom to reorient economies to deliver health for all across four interrelated themes:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**A Useful Report on How to Transform the Economy; to Ensure Everyone Receives Quality Healthcare.**

For the first time of its kind, the World Health Organization (WHO) Executive Board has presented a new, determined approach to restructuring the economy to ensure health accessibility for all communities. According to WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the Executive Board, established in November 2020 to respond to the COVID-19 pandemic and led by Professor Mariana Mazzucato, has spent the past two years examining the economy in order to ensure health accessibility for all society. It is guided by the principle that the safety of people and our world should be the cornerstone of our social, health, and economic systems, as well as our policies. The Executive Board presents a new, determined approach based on new knowledge to transform the economy, focusing on the four interconnected issues listed below.



I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.source: እሴት - በአዳዲስ ኢኮኖሚያዊ አመለካከቾች አስፈላጊ የሆኑትን ነገሮች ማወቅ እና መመዘን፤ ፋይናንስ - የጤና ተደራሽነትን ከአጭር ጊዜ ወጪ ይልቅ ወደ ረጅም ጊዜ ኢንቨስትመንት መለወጥ፤ ፈጠራ - ለጋራ ጥቅም የጤና ፈጠራን ማራመድ፤ አቅም - ሁሉንም የጤና ተደራሽነት እውን ለማድረግ ቀልጣፋ የመንግስት ሴክተር አቅምን ማሳደግ ናቸው። “ከሁለት ዓመት በፊት፣ መሪ የሴት ኢኮኖሚስቶችን እና የጤና ባለሙያዎችን የፓራዳይም (የአስተሳሰብ) ለውጥ እንዲያደርጉ ኃላፊነት ሰጥቻለሁ።
translations.reference: Value - valuing and measuring what matters through new economic metrics; Finance - how to finance health for all as a long-term investment, not a short-term cost; Innovation - how to advance health innovation for the common good; Capacity - how to strengthen dynamic public sector capacity to achieve health for all. “Two years ago, I asked a team of the world’s leading economists and public health experts – all women – to create a paradigm shift.
translations.hypothesis:
