Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Value - Understanding and measuring essential elements in new economic perspectives; Finance - Transforming health accessibility from short-term expenses to long-term investment; Innovation - Fostering health innovation for common benefit; Capacity - Strengthening the efficient capacity of the public sector to realize all health accessibility. “Two years ago, I tasked a group of women economists and health professionals with leading a paradigm (thinking) shift.”

translations.source: የጤና አገልግሎትን ኢኮኖሚያዊ ዕድገትን ለማምጣት ብቻ የሚረዳ ነገር አድርገን ከመመልከት ይልቅ በአሁኑ ወቅት ኢኮኖሚያዊ እንቅስቃሴዎችን በማዋቀር ሁለንተናዊ የጤና አገልግሎቶችን ተደራሽነት ለማሳካት የሚያስችል ፍኖተ ካርታ አዘጋጅተናል።” ሲሉ ዶ/ር ቴድሮስ ተናግረዋል። የምክር ቤቱ ሊቀመንበር ፕሮፌሰር ማሪያና ማዙካቶ “ባለፉት ሁለት ዓመታት ውስጥ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የጤና ኢኮኖሚክስ ምክር ቤቱ አዲስ ሃሳብ ለመቅረጽ ጥረት አድርጓል፤ ይህም የጤና ፋይናንስን ከወጪ ወደ ኢንቬስትመንት ለመቀየር ነው።” ብለዋል። "የፈጠራ ባለቤትነት ማሻሻያ፣ በመንግስትና በግሉ ዘርፍ ሽርክናዎች እና የበጀት ድልድልን ጨምሮ ጤና ለሁሉም እንዲደርስ የሚያረጋግጥ ኢኮኖሚን ለመፍጠር የሚያስፈልጉትን ለውጦችን መርምረናል። በመጨረሻው ዘገባችን ከገበያ እርማት ባለፈ ለሰዎች እና ለዓለማችን ደህንነት ቅድሚያ የሚሰጥ በጥንቃቄ እና በትብብር የሚቀርፅ አዲስ የኢኮኖሚ ፖሊሲ እንዲኖር እናበረታታለን።” ዛሬ ከሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ጎን ለጎን ይፋ የሆነው ዘገባ “ጤና ለሁሉም፤ አስፈላጊውን አገልግሎት ለማድረስ ኢኮኖሚን መለወጥ” (በድህረ ገጽ: https://www.who.int/groups/who-council-on-the) በሚል ርዕስ ከላይ በተጠቀሱት አራት ዋና ዋና ሃሳቦች ላይ የተመሰረተ አዲስ ማዕቀፍ ያቀርባል፣ ለእያንዳንዱም ዝርዝር ጥቆማዎች አሉት።
translations.reference: Now, instead of health for all being seen as the servant of economic growth, we have a roadmap for structuring economic activity in a way that will allow us to reach the goal of seeing all people with access to essential health services faster with better results,” said Dr Tedros. “Over the past two years, the WHO Council on the Economics of Health for All has worked to craft a new economic narrative – one that transforms financing for health from an expenditure to an investment,” said the Council’s Chair, Professor Mariana Mazzucato. “We have examined the changes needed – including to the structure of patents, public-private partnerships, and budgets – to design an economy that delivers Health for All. In our final report, we call for new economic policy that is not about market fixing but about proactively and collaboratively shaping markets that prioritize human and planetary health.” Launched today in conjunction with the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly, the report, entitled Health for All: Transforming economies to deliver what matters (https://www.who.int/groups/who-council-on-the-economics-of-health-for-all), provides a new framework built on the above four pillars, with specific recommendations under each – drawing from the Council’s previous work.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Rather than viewing health services solely as a means to economic growth, we have prepared a roadmap to achieve universal health service accessibility by structuring economic activities in a way that supports it." Dr. Tedros stated. The chairperson of the board, Professor Marianna Mazza, said, "Over the past two years, the WHO Health Economics and Policy Review Board has strived to present a new idea: to transform health financing from an expenditure to an investment." She added, "We have examined the changes needed to create an economy that ensures health for all, including innovation improvement, public-private partnerships, and budget allocation. In our final report, we encourage a new economic policy that prioritizes the safety of people and our world, beyond market protection, and is presented carefully and collaboratively." The report, released alongside the 76th World Health Assembly today, titled "Health for All: Transforming Economies to Deliver Essential Services" (back page: https://www.who.int/groups/who-council-on-the), presents a new framework based on the four key ideas mentioned above, with detailed recommendations for each.

translations.source: ጥቆማዎቹም፡ የኢኮኖሚያዊ እድገትን ብቻ ከማሳደድ እና ውጤቱን ሳናስብ አጠቃላይ የሃገር ውስጥ ምርትን ለማሳደግ ከመሞከር ይልቅ በእውነትም አስፈላጊ የሆኑት ነገሮችን መገምገም እና ማተኮር አለብን። ለሁሉም ህብረተሰብ የጤና ተደራሽነትን እውን ለማድረግ መንግስታት እሴቶችን እንደገና ማጤን እና በአዲስ መለኪያዎች በመመራት በማህበራዊ እና በዓለማችን ደህንነት ላይ የተመሰረት ኢኮኖሚን ማዋቀርና አቅጣጫ መቀየር አለባቸው። የጤና ወጪን እንደ የረጅም ጊዜ ኢንቨስትመንት በመመልከት የብሔራዊ እና ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ፋይናንስ ሥርዓቶች መሠረታዊ ማሻሻያ ማድረግ አስፈላጊ ነው። ጤናን ለሁሉም ማድረስ ተጨማሪ የገንዘብ ድጋፍን ብቻ ሳይሆን ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው ፋይናንስንም ይጠይቃል፤ ፈጠራ የጋራ እውቀትን የሚፈልግና በጭራሽ የአንድ ኩባንያ ወይም የመንግስት ኤጀንሲ ውጤት አይደለም። ነገር ግን ፈጠራን ለጋራ ጥቅም ካላዋልነው ብዙ ሰዎች ከጥቅሞቹ መገለላቸው ይቀጥላል።
translations.reference: Key recommendations include: We need to value and measure the things that truly matter - human and planetary flourishing - rather than pursuing economic growth and GDP maximization regardless of the consequences. To achieve health for all, governments must rethink value and reshape and redirect the economy based on social and planetary well-being, guided by new metrics. A fundamental overhaul of national and international systems for financing health is needed, so that spending on health is treated as a long-term investment. Delivering Health for All will require both more money, and higher quality financing.Innovation requires collective intelligence—it is never the fruit of just one company or government agency. But unless innovation is governed for the common good, many people remain excluded from its benefits.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Instead of solely focusing on increasing economic growth and the country's Gross Domestic Product without considering the consequences, we must assess and prioritize truly essential things. Governments need to re-evaluate their values and lead with new metrics to establish an economy based on social and global security, making healthcare accessible to all. It is essential to fundamentally reform national and international health financing systems, viewing health expenditure as a long-term investment. Achieving health for all requires not only additional financial support but also high-quality financing; it demands innovation, a shared knowledge base, and is not the result of a single company or government agency. However, if we don't harness innovation for the common good, many people will continue to be excluded from its benefits."

translations.source: ለጋራ ጥቅም ቅድሚያ የሚሰጥ አዲስ የጤና ፈጠራ ስነ-ምህዳር አስፈላጊ ነው። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ የመንግስት አቅም አስፈላጊነት አጉልቶ አሳይቷል። ውጤታማ መንግስታት የግድ ትንንሾቹ አይደሉም፤ ነገር ግን በጥሩ ሁኔታ የተደራጁ እና በቂ ሀብት እና መሰረተ ልማት ያላቸው ናቸው። ጤናን ለሁሉም ለማዳረስ በመንግስት አቅም ላይ እንደገና መዋዕለ ንዋይ ማፍሰስ አስፈላጊ ነው። ሪፖርቱ በተጨማሪም እነዚህ ለውጦች እንዴት እውን እንዲሆኑ ማድረግ እንደሚቻል፤ ለምሳሌ የኢኮኖሚያዊ እሴትን፣ የጤና ፋይናንስን፣ ፈጠራን እና የህዝብ ሴክተርን አቅም ማሳደግ ያሉ ምክሮችን ይሰጣል።
translations.reference: A new end-to-end health innovation ecosystem that prioritises the common good is needed. As the COVID-19 pandemic has made clear, the quality and capacity of government matters. Effective governments are not the smallest, but those that are well-designed and properly resourced, both financially and in terms of their people and infrastructure. Re-investing in government capacity is crucial to delivering Health for All. The report also provides suggestions on what can be done in practice to implement the changes needed to reorient measures of economic value, the financing of health, innovation and building public sector capacity in the service of health for all.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"A new health innovation ecosystem that prioritizes shared benefit is essential. The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of government capacity. Effective governments are not necessarily large; rather, they are well-organized and have sufficient resources and infrastructure. It is necessary to reinvest in government capacity in order to reach health for all. The report also provides recommendations on how to make these changes a reality, for example, by increasing economic value, health finance, innovation, and the capacity of the public sector."

translations.source: በሪፖርቱ ውስጥ የተጠቀሱት አንዳንድ ምሳሌዎች የሚከተሉትን ያካትታሉ፤ በደቡብ አፍሪካ ያለው የmRNA (በሴሎች ውስጥ መልዕክትን አስተላላፊ ፕሮቲን) ቴክኖሎጂ ማዕከል፡- ፈጠራን፣ ፋይናንስን እና የአቅም ፍላጎቶችን በንቃት ለመፍታት ያለመ በእሴቶች የሚመራ ስርዓት፤ ለጋራ ጥቅም ለሚያገለግል የጤና-ኢንዱስትሪ የብራዚል ህዝብ የሚያፈሰው መዋእለ ንዋይ፤ በ ደቡቡ የዓለማችን አካባቢ ያሉ ለውጥን የሚያመቻቹ የክልል ልማት ባንኮች ከምሳሌዎቹ መካከል ናቸው። ”Wellbeing Economy Alliance” (ደህንነትን የሚያረጋግጥ የኢኮኖሚ ጥምረት) በርካታ መንግስታትን እና ከ 600 በላይ ሌሎች ድርጅቶችን የያዘ ቡድን ሲሆን ይህም የኢኮኖሚ ስርዓቶችን ለመለወጥ እና ህይወትን የተሻለ ለማድረግ የተመሰረተ ነው።
translations.reference: Among these, the report mentions several examples, including: The mRNA technology hub facility in South Africa: a values-driven system that tries to get the innovation, financing and capacity right ex-ante; Brazil’s public investment in a health-industrial complex that serves the common good; Regional development banks as enablers of change in the Global South; The Wellbeing Economy Alliance – an alliance of several governments and over 600 other organizations working together to transform economic systems in the service of life;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Some of the examples mentioned in the report include: the mRNA (messenger protein that transmits messages within cells) technology center in South Africa – a value-driven system designed to actively address innovation, finance, and capacity needs; dedicated funding for the Brazilian population serving the health industry for common benefit; and regional development banks in the Southern Hemisphere that facilitate change. The 'Wellbeing Economy Alliance' is a group encompassing numerous governments and over 600 other organizations, established to transform economic systems and improve lives."

translations.source: ብዙዎቹ ፀረ ተህዋሲያን (መድሃኒቶች) በሽታ የመቋቋም አቅማቸውን ለማጎልበት የሚወጡ ብሄራዊ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብሮች በቂ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ስለሌላቸው የተለያዩ ዘርፎች የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እንዲያደርጉ ማድረግ አንዱ ቡድኑ የተቋቋመበት አላማ ነው። በዘገባው ውስጥ የተገለጹት ምክሮች ሀገራት ጤናን የሚገነዘቡበትን እና በጀት የሚያደርጉበትን እይታ የመቀየር አቅም አላቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፖሊሲ አውጪዎች፣ ሲቪል ማህበረሰቦች እና የጤና እና ኢኮኖሚክስ ማህበረሰቦች አባላት እነዚህን ምክሮች ሙሉ በሙሉ እንዲያጤኑ እና አዲስ የኢኮኖሚ ፖሊሲዎችን እና አወቃቀሮችን በማዘጋጀት እንደ መመሪያ ተጠቅመው ጤና ለሁሉምን እውን እንዲያደርጉ ያሳስበናል።
translations.reference: Approaches to financing national action plans (NAPs) on antimicrobial resistance through multi sector joint budgeting, given that most NAPs remain unfunded. The recommendations included in the report could change the way countries view and finance health. WHO calls on policy-makers, civil society, and members of the health and economics communities to give full consideration to the recommendations and use them as a compass to develop new economic policies and structures that can move us along the road to making health for all a reality.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Many national action plans aimed at strengthening the capacity of countries to resist diseases (through medicines) are failing due to a lack of sufficient financial support. One of the goals of the group established is to ensure that various sectors provide financial support. The recommendations outlined in the report have the potential to change the way countries view and budget for health. The World Health Organization urges policymakers, civil society organizations, and members of the health and economics communities to fully consider these recommendations and use them as a guide in developing new economic policies and frameworks to make health for all a reality."

translations.source: ታዋቂዎቹ አርቲስቶች ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና ፕሪቲ ዬንዴ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደሮች ሆነው ተሹመዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ታዋቂ የሆኑትን ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና ፕሪቲ ዬንዴን የኪነጥበብ እና ጤና የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደሮች አድርጎ በመሾሙ የተሰማውን ደስታ ገልጿል። ይህ ትልቅ ዜና የተነገረው እ.ኤ.አ. በግንቦት 22 ቀን 2023 በተካሄደው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት መታሰቢያ በዓል አካል በሆነው በሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ወቅት ነው። በዘመናችን በጣም ታዋቂ ድምጻዊ የሆኑት ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና በአስደናቂ ድምጾና ትርኢትዋ የምትታወቀው ፕሪቲ ዬንዴ ለኪነጥበብ እንዲሁም በተለያዩ የፈጠራ ዘዴዎች ለተሻሻለ የጤና ውጤት ባደረጉት አስተዋጾ ምክኒያት ተመርጠዋል። እ.ኤ.አ. ሚያዚያ 8 2023 ፍሌሚንግ እና ዬንዴ “የፈውስ ኪነጥበብ” በሚል ርዕስ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት የምስረታ በዓል ላይ በጄኔቫ ስዊዘርላንድ በተዘጋጀ ልዩ ኮንሰርት ዝግጅታቸውን አቅርበዋል።
translations.reference: Renowned artists Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende appointed as WHO Goodwill Ambassadors for Arts and Health WHO is delighted to announce the appointment of internationally acclaimed artists Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende as Goodwill Ambassadors for Arts and Health. This prestigious announcement was made as part of WHO's 75th Anniversary commemoration during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly on 22 May 2023. Renée Fleming, one of the most celebrated singers of our time, and Pretty Yende, an extraordinary soprano known for her dazzling performances, have been selected for their artistic achievement and advocacy for improved health outcomes through creative means. On 8 April 2023, Fleming and Yende performed for a special concert titled Healing Arts, dedicated to WHO's 75th anniversary in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

**René Fleming and Pretty Yende Appointed Goodwill Ambassadors for the World Health Organization**

The World Health Organization (WHO) has announced the appointment of renowned artists René Fleming and Pretty Yende as Goodwill Ambassadors. The WHO expressed its delight at appointing René Fleming and Pretty Yende, who are internationally recognized, as artistic and health Goodwill Ambassadors. This significant news was announced during the 76th World Health Assembly, which took place on May 22, 2023, as part of the World Health Organization’s 75th anniversary celebrations. René Fleming, one of the most famous singers of our time, and Pretty Yende, known for her stunning voice and performances, were selected for their contributions to art and for improved health outcomes through various creative approaches. On April 8, 2023, Fleming and Yende presented a special concert in Geneva, Switzerland, as part of the WHO’s 75th anniversary launch event, under the theme "The Healing Power of Art."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ "የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሬኔ ፍሌሚንግ እና ፕሪቲ ዬንዴ የኪነጥበብ እና ጤና በጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደሮች በመሆን በመቀላቀላቸው ታላቅ ክብር ይሰማናል" ብለዋል። ወደር የለሽ ተሰጥኦአቸው፣ ለኪነጥበብ ያላቸው ቁርጠኝነት እና ጤናን እና ደህንነትን ለማራመድ ያላቸው ፍላጎት ለዚህ አስፈላጊ ዓላማ ተስማሚ አምባሳደሮች ያደርጋቸዋል። የእነርሱ ተሳትፎ ግለሰቦች፣ ማህበረሰቡ እና ፖሊሲ አውጪዎች ኪነ-ጥበብ ለተሻለ ጤና ያለውን ተጽዕኖ እንዲገነዘቡ ያነሳሳል። ፍሌሚንግ በአዲሱ ሚናዋ ብዙ ባለሙያዎችን ታመጣለች። በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ አድናቂዎቿን በብሩህ ድምጿ እና በአስደናቂ የስነጥበብ ስራዋ አስደንቃለች።
translations.reference: Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General, said, "WHO is honoured to have Renée Fleming and Pretty Yende join as Goodwill Ambassadors for Arts and Health. Their unparalleled talent, dedication to the arts, and commitment to advancing health and well-being make them ideal ambassadors for this important cause. Their involvement will inspire individuals, communities and policy-makers to recognize and harness the transformative power of the arts in the pursuit of better health for all. "Fleming brings a wealth of expertise to her new role. She has captivated audiences worldwide with her luminous soprano voice and compelling artistry.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said, "We are deeply honored that Ms. Reine Flemming and Ms. Pretty Yende have joined us as WHO's artistic and health goodwill ambassadors." Their unparalleled talent, commitment to the arts, and desire to improve health and well-being make them ideal ambassadors for this important cause. Their involvement will remind individuals, communities, and policymakers to recognize the impact of art on better health. Ms. Flemming brings a wealth of experience to her new role. She has captivated her fans worldwide with her brilliant voice and stunning artistic work."

translations.source: ፍሌሚንግ ከአስደናቂው የድምጽ ተሰጥኦዋ ባሻገር ኪነጥበብ እና ጤና ያላቸው ግንኙነት ላይ ለምርምር ብርቱ ጠበቃ ነበረች። በዓለም ዙሪያ ከሳይንቲስቶች እና ባለሙያዎች ጋር ያቀረበቻቸው ገለጻዎች፣ በዋና ዋና ተነሳሽነቶች ውስጥ ያላት የመሪነት ሚና እንዲሁም ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ጋር የነበራት ግንኙነት የጥበብን የመለወጥ ሃይል ለመጠቀም ያላትን ቁርጠኝነት ያሳያል። ፍሌሚንግ “ቀዳሚው የሆነው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኪነ-ጥበብ በጤና ላይ ያለውን ከፍተኛ ተፅእኖ በመገንዘቡ በጣም ተደስቻለሁ። በየጊዜው በአዕምሮ ጥናት እና ምርምር ላይ የሚታዩ እድገቶች የኪነጥበብ ሕክምናዎች፣ ልምዶች እና ትምህርቶች ያላቸውን ጥቅም የበለጠ ያሳያሉ፣ ይህም በሰው ልጅ የህይወት ዘመን ሁሉ ለደህንነቱ ከፍተኛ ጥቅም አለው። ሆኖም የዚህን ስራ አስደናቂ አቅም መገንዘብ የጀመርነው ገና አሁን ነው ስለዚህም እንደ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደርነቴ ግንዛቤን እና ተደራሽነትን ለማስፋት እሰራለሁ።” ብላለች።
translations.reference: Beyond her remarkable vocal talent, Fleming has been a fervent advocate for research at the intersection of the arts and health. Her presentations worldwide with scientists and practitioners, her leading role in major initiatives, and her association with WHO demonstrate her commitment to harnessing the transformative power of the arts to better society. "I am elated that the preeminent global health agency is recognizing the powerful impact of the arts in health, and I am deeply honoured to be entrusted to share this message," said Fleming. “Every day, advances in neuroscience and clinical research reveal more about the benefits of arts therapies, experiences, and education, with implications for well-being throughout the human life span. However, we have only just begun to realize the amazing potential of this work, and I look forward to expanding awareness and access as a WHO Goodwill Ambassador.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Fleming, beyond her remarkable vocal talent, was a passionate advocate for the connection between art and health. The presentations she made with scientists and experts around the world, her leadership role in major initiatives, and her connection with the World Health Organization demonstrate her commitment to harnessing the transformative power of art. Fleming stated, "I am delighted that the pioneering World Health Organization recognizes the profound impact of art on health. Advances continually seen in neuroscience and research further demonstrate the benefits of art therapies, practices, and teachings, which are of great benefit to human well-being throughout life. However, we have only just begun to realize the astonishing potential of this work, and that is why, as a Goodwill Ambassador for the World Health Organization, I am working to expand awareness and accessibility."

translations.source: ቆንጅየዋ ዬንዴ በአስደናቂ ድምጿ እና በሚያስደንቅ ትርኢቷ ተመልካቾችን አስገርማለች። የሙዚቃን ኃይል በጥልቀት የተረዳች ድምጻዊ እንደመሆኗ ችሎታዋን በመጠቀም ህብረተሰቡን ለማገልገል ያላትን ቁርጠኝነት አሳይታለች። ዬንዴ የሙዚቃን የመፈውስ አቅም ለማካፈል ያላትን ፍቅር እና ለማህበራዊ ጉዳዮች ያላትን ቁርጠኝነት ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኪነጥበብ ለጤና አጀንዳ ጋር በትክክል ይስማማል። " የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የበጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደር ሆኜ በመሾሜ በጣም ደስተኛ ነኝ።" ብላለች። “ኪነ-ጥበብ አካላዊ፣ አእምሯዊ እና ማህበራዊ ደህንነታችንን ለማሻሻል መሰረታዊ ነገር እንደመሆኑ ለቀጣይ የምርምር ስራዎች መሰረትን ለማስቀመጥ እንደረዳሁ ተስፋ አደርጋለሁ።
translations.reference: "Pretty Yende has mesmerized audiences with her exquisite voice and stunning performances. As a soprano with a deep understanding of the power of music, she has demonstrated her commitment to using her talent to improve society. Yende's passion for sharing the healing aspects of music and her dedication to social causes align perfectly with WHO’s Arts and Health agenda. Yende said, "I am delighted to be appointed a WHO Goodwill Ambassador for Arts and Health. I hope to help establish a foundation for continued investigation into the arts being a fundamental ingredient in improving our physical, mental, and social well-being.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Yende amazed the audience with her stunning voice and captivating performance. Demonstrating her ability as a singer who deeply understands the power of music, she showcased her commitment to serving the community. Yende's love for sharing music's healing power and her dedication to social issues align perfectly with the World Health Organization's Arts for Health initiative. "I am incredibly honored to be appointed a Goodwill Ambassador for the World Health Organization," she said. "I hope to lay the foundation for future research as I believe art is essential to improving our physical, mental, and social well-being."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ለአዲስ ምርምር መንገዱን በመክፈት እና ዓለም አቀፋዊ መድረኮችን በማዘጋጀት ላደረገው ትልቅ አስተዋጾ ምስጋናዬን አቀርባለሁ። በትውልድ ሃገሬ በደቡብ አፍሪካ እና በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለተሻለ እና ጤናማ ለሆነ ነገ አስፈላጊ ስራዎችን ለመስራት ሁሉንም እድሎች እጠቀማለሁ።" ስትል ዬንዴ አክላለች። የፍሌሚንግ እና የዬንዴ ሹመቶች በኪነጥበብ እና በጤና መካከል ያለውን ጥልቅ ግንኙነት ያጎላሉ። እንደ ሙዚቃ፣ ጥበብ እና ዳንስ ባሉ የፈጠራ ስራዎች ላይ መሳተፍ ለአካል፣ ለአእምሮ ጤና፣ ለማህበራዊ ደህንነት እና አጠቃላይ የህይወት ጥራት በጎ ተጽእኖ ይኖረዋል። እንደ በጎ ፈቃድ አምባሳደርነታቸው፣ ፍሌሚንግ እና ዬንዴ በሚጫወቷቸው ሚናዎች ኪነ-ጥበብ ከጤና ጋር እንዲቀናጅ ያበረታታሉ፣ አዳዲስ የኪነጥበብ ህክምናዎች ተደራሽነትን ይደግፋሉ እናም በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ጥበብ የጤና ውጤቶችን ለማሻሻል ያለውን ጠቀሜታ ያጎላሉ።
translations.reference: I am grateful to WHO for paving the way for new research and providing their global platform to spread the word and generate research on the connection of arts and health. "I look forward to all the possibilities to do essential work in my native South Africa and globally for a better and healthier tomorrow," added Yende. The appointments of Fleming and Yende underscore the profound link between arts and health. Engagement in creative activities, such as music, art, and dance, positively impacts physical, mental health, social well-being, and overall quality of life. Through their roles as Goodwill Ambassadors, Fleming and Yende will promote the integration of arts into health-care systems, advocate for access to creative arts therapies, and champion the importance of artistic expression in improving health outcomes globally.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"I would like to express my gratitude to the World Health Organization for opening the way for new research and organizing global platforms. I will use all opportunities to do the necessary work for a better and healthier future, both in my native country, South Africa, and at the global level," said Yvonne. The surnames Fleming and Yvonne highlight the deep connection between art and health. Participating in creative works such as music, art, and dance has a positive impact on physical, mental health, social well-being, and overall quality of life. As goodwill ambassadors, Fleming and Yvonne encourage the integration of art with health in the roles they play, support the accessibility of new artistic therapies, and highlight the importance of art in improving health outcomes globally."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዓለም አቀፍ አውታረ መረብን ጀመረ። ይህም ተላላፊ በሽታዎችን ለመለየት እና ለመከላከል ነው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና አጋሮቹ የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመልን በመጠቀም ሰዎችን ከተላላፊ በሽታ ስጋት ለመጠበቅ የሚረዳ ዓለም አቀፍ አውታረ መረብን እያስጀመሩ ነው። የአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አገሮች እና ክልሎች ያላቸውን ናሙናዎችን የመሰብሰብ እና የመመርመር ስርዓትን ያሻሽላል፤ ይህም የተሻሉ የህዝብ የጤና ውሳኔዎችን ለማድረግ እና መረጃን በስፋት ለማጋራት ይረዳል። የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ጥናት ተዋህሲያኑ ምን ያህል ተላላፊ እና ገዳይ እንደሆኑ እንዲሁም እንዴት እንደሚስፋፉ ለመረዳት የቫይረሶችን፣ ባክቴሪያዎችን እና ሌሎች በሽታ አምጪ አካላትን የዘረመል ኮድ ይተነትናል።
translations.reference: WHO launches global network to detect and prevent infectious disease threats WHO and partners are launching a global network to help protect people from infectious disease threats through the power of pathogen genomics. The International Pathogen Surveillance Network (IPSN) will provide a platform to connect countries and regions, improving systems for collecting and analyzing samples, using these data to drive public health decision-making, and sharing that information more broadly. Pathogen genomics analyzes the genetic code of viruses, bacteria and other disease-causing organisms to understand how infectious they are, how deadly they are, and how they spread.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The World Health Organization (WHO) has launched a global network. This is to identify and prevent infectious diseases. The WHO and its partners are launching a global network to protect people from the threat of infectious diseases by using pathogen surveillance. The Global Pathogen Surveillance Network (GPSN) will improve the systems that countries and regions have for collecting and examining samples, which will help make better public health decisions and share information more widely. Pathogen genomic studies analyze the genetic code of viruses, bacteria, and other pathogens to understand how transmissible and deadly they are and how they spread."

translations.source: በዚህ መረጃ ሳይንቲስቶች እና የህዝብ ጤና ጥበቃ ባለስልጣናት ወረርሽኞችን ለመከላከል እና ምላሽ ለመስጠት እንደ ትልቅ የበሽታ ክትትል ስርዓት ሆኖ በማገልገል መድሃኒቶችን እና ክትባቶችን ለማዘጋጀት ይረዳል። በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ለወረርሽኝ ምርምር በተዘጋጀው ማዕከል ውስጥ ከመንግስታት፣ ከበጎ አድራጎት ድርጅቶች፣ ከሲቪል ማህበረሰብ፣ ከትምህርት እና ከግሉ ሴክተር የተውጣጡ ባለሙያዎችን በዘረመል ጥናት እና በመረጃ ትንተና ላይ ያሰባስባል። . ሁሉም አንድ የጋራ ግብ አላቸው ይህም ወረርሽኞች ከመፈጠራቸው በፊት ማግኘት እና ምላሽ መስጠት እንዲሁም መደበኛ የበሽታ ክትትልን ማመቻቸት ነው። “የዚህ አዲስ አውታር ግብ የተጋነነ ይመስላል ነገር ግን በጤና ደህንነት ላይ ወሳኝ ሚና ሊጫወት ይችላል፤ እያንዳንዱ ሀገር የበሽታ አምጪ ተዋህሲያንን ዘረመል ጥናት እንደ የህዝብ ጤና ስርአት አካል አድርጎ እንዲሰጥ ማድረግ ነው።” ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ቴድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረየሱስ ተናግረዋል። “በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ወቅት በግልፅ እንዳየነው የጋራ የጤና ስጋቶችን ለመዋጋት በጋራ ስንቆም አለም ጠንካራ ትሆናለች።” ሲሉም አክለዋል።
translations.reference: With this information, scientists and public health officials can identify and track diseases to prevent and respond to outbreaks as part of a broader disease surveillance system, and to develop treatments and vaccines. The IPSN, with a Secretariat hosted by the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, brings together experts worldwide at the cutting-edge of genomics and data analytics, from governments, philanthropic foundations, multilateral organizations, civil society, academia and the private sector. All share a common goal: to detect and respond to disease threats before they become epidemics and pandemics, and to optimize routine disease surveillance. The goal of this new network is ambitious, but it can also play a vital role in health security: to give every country access to pathogen genomic sequencing and analytics as part of its public health system,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “As was so clearly demonstrated to us during the COVID-19 pandemic, the world is stronger when it stands together to fight shared health threats.”
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"This information helps scientists and public health authorities prepare medicines and vaccines by serving as a large disease surveillance system to prevent and respond to epidemics. Within the center prepared by the World Health Organization for epidemic research, experts from governments, NGOs, civil society, academia, and the private sector are gathered for in-depth research and data analysis. They all share a common goal: to detect and respond to epidemics before they occur and to facilitate routine disease surveillance. “The goal of this new network may seem ambitious, but it can play a critical role in health security; it is essential that each country treat the study of disease vectors as part of its public health system,” said WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “As we clearly saw during the COVID-19 pandemic, the world becomes stronger when we stand together to combat common health threats.” he added."

translations.source: ኮቪድ-19 በሽታ አምጪ ዘረመልን ማጥናት ለወረርሽኝ ምላሽ መስጠት ያለውን ወሳኝ ሚና ጎላ አድርጎ አሳይቷል። የSARS-COV-2 (የኮሮና ቫይረስ ዝርያ ሲሆን ከባድ እና አጣዳፊ የመተንፈሻ አካላት ችግር የሚያመጣ ነው) ዘረመል ባይጠና ኖሮ ውጤታማ ክትባቶች በፍጥነት አይገኙም ነበር። አዳዲስ እና የበለጠ ተላለፊ የሆኑ የቫይረሱ ዓይነቶችም በፍጥነት ተለይተው ባልታወቁ ነበር። የዘረመል ጥናት ለውጤታማ የወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ መስጠት ቁልፍ ነው፤ እንዲሁም ለተለያዩ በሽታዎች እነዚህም ከምግብ ወለድ በሽታዎች ጀምሮ የመተፈሻ አካል በሽታ፣ ቲቢ እና ኤችአይቪ ጨምሮ ለሚደረጉ የምርምር ስራዎች ቀጣይነት ያለው ነው። ለምሳሌ የኤችአይቪ መድሀኒትን ውጤታማነት ለመከታተል ጥቅም ላይ መዋሉ ለቁጥር የሚያታክቱ ህይወቶችን ታድጓል።
translations.reference: COVID-19 highlighted the critical role pathogen genomics plays in responding to pandemic threats. Without the rapid sequencing of the SARS-COV-2 genome, vaccines would not have been as effective, or have been made available so quickly. New, more transmissible variants of the virus would not have been as quickly identified. Genomics lies at the heart of effective epidemic and pandemic preparedness and response, as well as part of the ongoing surveillance of a vast range of diseases, from foodborne diseases and influenza to tuberculosis and HIV. Its use in monitoring the spread of HIV drug resistance, for example, has led to antiretroviral regimes that have saved countless lives.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The study of the COVID-19 pathogen has highlighted its crucial role in responding to pandemics. Without the study of the SARS-COV-2 genome (a coronavirus strain that causes severe and persistent respiratory problems), effective vaccines would not have been rapidly developed. New and more transmissible variants of the virus would also not have been quickly identified. Genomic research is key to effective pandemic preparedness and response, and it continues to be vital for research efforts addressing various diseases, including foodborne illnesses, respiratory diseases, tuberculosis (TB), and HIV. For example, its use in monitoring the effectiveness of HIV medication has saved countless lives."

translations.source: “ሮክፌለር” የተባለው ማህበር ፕሬዝዳንት ዶ/ር ራጂቭ ጄ ሻህ እንዳሉት “ዓለም ኮቪድ-19ን በጋራ በሚዋጋበት ወቅት በበሽታ አምጪ ተውሳክ ዘረመል ክትትል ላይ የነበረው ዓለም አቀፍ ትብብር ወሳኝ ነበር።” "የአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት በዘርፉ ያሉ አጋሮችን ዕውቀት፣ መሳሪያዎች እና ልምዶች ለመለዋወጥ የሚያስችል ጠንካራ መድረክ በመፍጠር ወረርሽኝን ለመከላከል እና ምላሽ ለመስጠት ለወደፊቱ በዚህ ልምድ ላይ ይገነባል።" በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምክንያት የአገሮች የዘረመል ጥናት አቅም በቅርብ ጊዜ ቢጨምርም ፣ ብዙዎች አሁንም ናሙናዎችን ለመሰብሰብ እና ለማጥናት እንዲሁም በመረጃዎች ተደግፎ የህዝብ ጤና ውሳኔዎችን ለማገዝ የሚችል ውጤታማ ስርዓት የላቸውም ። የተጠናከረ የአለም ጤና ጥበቃ ስርዓትን ለመገንባት በቂ የመረጃ፣ የአሰራር እና የፈጠራዎች መጋራት የለም። ወረርሽኙ በተከሰተበት ወቅት ለዘርፉ ተመድቦ የነበረው የገንዘብ መጠን አሁን ላይ ሀብታም በሆኑ አገሮች ውስጥ እንኳን እየቀነሰ ነው።
translations.reference: “Global collaboration in pathogen genomic surveillance has been critical as the world fights COVID-19 together,” said Dr Rajiv J. Shah, President of The Rockefeller Foundation. “IPSN builds upon this experience by creating a strong platform for partners across sectors and borders to share knowledge, tools, and practices to ensure that pandemic prevention and response is innovative and robust in the future.” Despite recent scale-up in genomics capacity in countries as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, many still lack effective systems for collecting and analyzing samples or using those data to drive public health decision-making. There is not enough sharing of data, practices, and innovations to build a robust global health surveillance architecture. Budgets that soared during the pandemic, allowing a rapid build-up of capabilities, are now being slashed, even in the wealthiest countries.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"According to Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, President of the Rockefeller Foundation, “During a time when the world is collectively fighting COVID-19, the global collaboration on pathogen genomic surveillance was critical.” The International Pathogen Genomics Consortium will build on this experience to prevent and respond to future pandemics by creating a robust platform for stakeholders in the field to share knowledge, tools, and expertise. While the genomic sequencing capacity of countries has recently increased due to the COVID-19 pandemic, many still lack the ability to collect and analyze samples and have effective systems to inform public health decisions with data. There has been insufficient sharing of data, protocols, and innovations to build a stronger global health security system. The funding that was dedicated to the field during the pandemic is now dwindling, even in wealthier countries."

translations.source: "አርጀንቲና የራሳችንን ሀገር እና የሌሎች ሀገራትንም የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ጥናት አቅም ለመገንባት የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ታደርጋለች።" ሲሉ የአርጀንቲና ብሄራዊ ዘረመል ጥናት ማዕከል ዳይሬክተር ጆሴፊና ካምፖስ ተናግረዋል ። "በሽታዎች ድንበር አያከብሩም፤ በአንድ ሀገር ውስጥ ያለ የበሽታ ስጋት ለሌሎችም ስጋት ነው። በሽታን ለመከላከል እና ህይወትን ለማዳን እንዲሁም የጋራ ግባችንን ለማሳካት ከአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አባላት ጋር ለመተባበር ዝግጁ ነን። የአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት እነዚህን ተግዳሮቶች በአለምአቀፍ ደረጃ ሃገራትን እና በሽታ-ተኮር ህብረቶችን በማገናኘት፤ አደጋዎችን በተሻለ ለመለየት፣ ለመከላከል እና ምላሽ ለመስጠት የትብብር ስርዓትን በመገንባት ይሰራል። በአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት በኩል በገንዘብ በመደገፍ፤ ሀሳቦችን እና ፕሮጀክቶችን ለማሳደግ አባላቱ በልዩ ልዩ ተግዳሮቶች ላይ በማተኮር በተዘጋጁ ቡድኖች በጋራ ይሰራሉ።
translations.reference: “Argentina is deeply invested in building our own country capacity in pathogen genomics and the capacity of other countries,” said Josefina Campos, Director of the National Genomics and Bioinformatics Center at ANLIS Malbrán, Argentina. “Diseases do not respect borders: a disease threat in one country is also a threat to others. We look forward to collaborating with IPSN members to achieve our common goal of preventing illness and saving lives.” The IPSN will tackle these challenges through a global network, connecting geographies and disease-specific networks, to build a collaborative system to better detect, prevent and respond to disease threats. Members will work together in dedicated groups focusing on specific challenges, supported by funding through the IPSN to scale-up ideas and projects in pathogen genomics.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Argentina will provide financial support to build our own country's and other countries' capacity for pathogen research," said Josefina Campos, Director of the National Genetic Research Center of Argentina. "Diseases do not respect borders; the risk of disease in one country is a risk to others. We are ready to collaborate with the International Pathogen Research Coalition to prevent disease, save lives, and achieve our common goal. The International Pathogen Research Coalition works by connecting countries and disease-focused organizations globally to better identify, prevent, and respond to threats, building a system of cooperation. Through financial support from the International Pathogen Research Coalition, members work together in specialized teams focused on various challenges, fostering ideas and projects."

translations.source: ሀገሮችን፣ ክልሎችን እና ሰፊ ባለድርሻ አካላትን በማገናኘት ህብረቱ ወሳኝ አቅምን ለመጨመር፣ ክልላዊ እና ሀገር አቀፍ ድምጽን ለማጉላት እና ቅድሚያ የሚሰጧቸውን ነገሮች ለማጠናከር ይረዳል። ስለ አለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አጀማመር፤ ህብረቱ በጄኔቫ የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ በሚካሄድበት ጊዜ በይፋ ይጀምራል። የማስጀመሪያው ዝግጅት እ.ኤ.አ. በግንቦት 20 ቀን 2023 ከቀኑ 17፡00 - 19፡30 ሰአት በጄኔቫ ኢንተር ኮንቲኔንታል ውስጥ ይካሄዳል እናም ለመገናኛ ብዙሃን ክፍት ነው። የሚከተሉት ዋና ዋና ተናጋሪዎችም ይገኙበታል፤
translations.reference: By connecting countries, regions, and wider stakeholders, the IPSN will help to increase critical capacity, amplify regional and country-level voices, and strengthen their priorities. About the IPSN launch The IPSN will be formally launched on the sidelines of the World Health Assembly in Geneva. The launch event takes place on 20 May 2023, 17:00 – 19:30h (CET) in the InterContinental Geneva, and is open to media. It includes keynote speeches from:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The Hub facilitates the enhancement of critical capacity by connecting countries, regions, and broad stakeholder groups, amplifying regional and national voices, and strengthening prioritized areas. The Global Pandemic Prevention Hub is launched; the Hub will be officially launched during the World Health Assembly held in Geneva. The inaugural event will take place on May 20, 2023, from 17:00 - 19:30 at the Geneva InterContinental and is open to the media. The following key speakers will also be present:"

translations.source: ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር፤ ዶ/ር ማይክ ራያን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የድንገተኛ አደጋ ፕሮግራም ዋና ዳይሬክተር እና ዶ/ር ቺክዌ ኢኽክዌዙ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ከወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከል ናቸው። በተጨማሪም ከአባል ሃገሮች እና ከገንዘብ ድጋፍ ሰጪዎች ከፍተኛ ደረጃ ተናጋሪዎች የሚኖሩበት ውይይት ይኖራል። እነርሱም፤ ዴም ጄኒ ሃሪስ የተባበሩት መንግስታት የጤና ጥበቃ ኤጀንሲ ዋና ሥራ አስፈፃሚ፤
translations.reference: Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General, World Health Organization; Dr Mike Ryan, Executive Director, WHO Health Emergencies Programme; and Dr Chikwe Ihekweazu, Assistant Director-General, World Health Organization, Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence. There will also be a panel with high-level speakers from Member State and IPSN funders, including; Dame Jenny Harries, Chief Executive, UK Health Security Agency;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization; Dr. Mike Ryan, Director of the World Health Organization's Emergency Program; and Dr. Chikwe Ihekweazu, Deputy Director-General of the World Health Organization from the Center for Global Health Security. There will also be a discussion with high-level speakers from member states and funding partners. They include; Dem Gen Jenny Harris, Executive Director of the United Nations Health Agency.

translations.source: ዶ/ር ዣን ካሴያ የአፍሪካ የበሽታ መቆጣጠርና መከላከል ማዕከል ዋና ዳይሬክተር፤ ዶ / ር ናቪን ራኦ የ”ሮክፌለር” ድርጅት ምክትል ፕሬዚዳንት፤ ዶ / ር አሌክሳንደር ፒም የተላላፊ በሽታዎች ዳይሬክተር፤ ፕሮፌሰር ቱሊዮ ዴ ኦሊቬራ በደቡብ አፍሪካ ስቴለንቦሽ ዩኒቨርሲቲ የበሽታ ስርጭት ጥናት ማዕከል ዳይሬክተር፤ ቦጅሮን ኩሜል በጀርመን የፌዴራል ጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር ምክትል ኃላፊ፤
translations.reference: Dr Jean Kaseya, Director-General, Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention; Dr Naveen Rao, Senior Vice President, Rockefeller Foundation; Dr Alexander Pym, Director of Infectious Disease, Wellcome Trust; Professor Tulio de Oliveira, Director, Centre for Epidemiology at Stellenbosch University, South Africa; Björn Kümmel, Deputy Head of Unit, Global Health, Federal Ministry of Health, Germany;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Dr. Jean Kaseya, Director of the Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention; Dr. Navin Rao, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation; Dr. Alexander Pym, Director of Communicable Diseases; Professor Tulio de Oliveira, Director of the Center for Infectious Disease Epidemiology at Stellenbosch University in South Africa; Bojorn Kumel, Deputy Head of the German Federal Ministry of Health;

translations.source: ፕሮፌሰር ሊዮ ኢይ ሲን የስንጋፖር ብሔራዊ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች ማዕከል ዋና ዳይሬክተር፤ ዶክተር ማሪዮ ሞሬራ የፊዮክሩዝ ብራዚል ፕሬዚዳንት እና ዶ/ር ጆሴፊና ካምፖስ በአርጀንቲና የብሔራዊ ዘረመል ጥናት ማዕከል ዳይሬክተር ናቸው። ዝግጅቱ የሚከናወነው በድብልቅ ቅርጸት ነው፣ ይህም ለአለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አባላት እና ለሰፊ ታዳሚዎች በመረጡት አማራጭ እንዲሳተፉ እድል ይሰጣል። ስለ አለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት፤ ህብረቱ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝን ጥናት ማዕከል ስር የተመሰረተ ሲሆን የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ጥናት ሂደትን ለማፋጠን እና የህዝብ ጤና ውሳኔ አሰጣጥን ለማሻሻል የተቋቋመ ነው።
translations.reference: Professor Leo Yee Sin, Executive Director of the National Centre for Infectious Diseases; Singapore; Dr Mario Moreira, President, Fiocruz, Brazil; and Dr Josefina Campos, Director of the National Genomics and Bioinformatics Center at ANLIS Malbrán, Argentina. The event will take place in a hybrid format, providing an opportunity for IPSN members and broader audiences to engage with the IPSN and its activities. About the IPSNThe IPSN is a new global network of pathogen genomic actors, hosted by the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, to accelerate progress on the deployment of pathogen genomics and improve public health decision-making.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Professor Leo Yee Sin is the Director of the Singapore National Centre for Infectious Diseases; Dr. Mario Moreira is the President of Fiocruz Brazil, and Dr. Josefina Campos is the Director of the National Center for Genetic Studies in Argentina. The event will be held in a hybrid format, which allows members of the International Society for Infectious Diseases and a wider audience to participate in their preferred option. Regarding the International Society for Infectious Diseases; the society was established under the World Health Organization's Center for Disease Control and Prevention and was founded to accelerate the study of infectious agents and improve public health decision-making.

translations.source: ህብረቱ እያንዳንዱ ሀገር የህዝብ ጤና ክትትል ስርአት አካል ሆኖ የዘረመል ጥናት ዘላቂ አቅም እና ፍትሃዊ ተደራሽነት ያለባትን ዓለም ማየት ግቡ ነው ። ተደራሽነትን እና ፍትሃዊነትን ለማሻሻል የአባላቱን ስራ የሚያጎላ እና የሚያፋጥን እርስ በርስ የሚደጋገፍ አለምአቀፍ የክትትል መረብ ለመፍጠር አቅዷል። ህብረቱ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከል ጽህፈት ቤት የሚደገፉ የተለያዩ ባለድርሻ አካላትን የሚያሰባስቡ ሶስት ዋና የስራ አስፈፃሚ አካላትን ያቀፈ ነው። የመጀመሪያው አጋሮቹ በCommunities of Practice (COPs) በጋራ ተግዳሮቶችን ለመፍታት እንዲተባበሩ ያደርጋል፤ ይህም በበሽታ አምጪ የዘረመል ጥናት ውስጥ መስማማትን እና ፈጠራን ለመጨመር ነው። በሃገር ደረጃ ስኬል አፕ አክስሌሬተር (Country Scale-Up Accelerator) ውስጥ ባለድርሻ አካላቱ ጥረቶችን በማጣጣም እና የደቡብ-ደቡብ ልውውጡን (South-South exchange) በመደገፍ የሀገርን አቅም ግንባታ ለማሳደግ በጋራ እንዲሰሩ ያደርጋል።
translations.reference: The IPSN envisions a world where every country has equitable access to sustained capacity for genomic sequencing and analytics as part of its public health surveillance system. It sets out to create a mutually supportive global network of genomic surveillance actors that amplifies and accelerates the work of its members to improve access and equity. The IPSN consists of three main operational bodies that bring together different sets of stakeholders, supported by a Secretariat at the WHO Pandemic Hub. Partners collaborate in Communities of Practice (COPs) to solve common challenges, aiming to increase harmonization and innovation in pathogen genomics. In the Country Scale-Up Accelerator (CSUA), stakeholders work together to align efforts and enable South-South exchange to scale-up country capacity building.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The Union aims to see a world with sustainable capacity and equitable access for genetic research, functioning as a component of each country's public health surveillance system. To improve accessibility and equity, it plans to create a mutually reinforcing international surveillance network that amplifies and accelerates the work of its members. The Union comprises three main operational bodies that bring together various stakeholders supported by the World Health Organization's Genetic Research Center. The first facilitates collaboration among partners in Communities of Practice (COPs) to solve common challenges, thereby increasing consensus and innovation in pathogen genetic research. The Country Scale-Up Accelerator brings together stakeholders to jointly strengthen national capacity by aligning efforts and supporting South-South exchange."

translations.source: እነዚህ ሁለቱ ለአለም አቀፍ ትብብር እና ፍትሃዊነት እንዲሁም ጥልቅ እውቀት ከተለያዩ ዘርፎች፣ የገቢ ደረጃዎች እና አካባቢዎች የተውጣጡ ድርጅቶችን በአንድነት ያሰባስባሉ። ሦስተኛው አካል የበጎ አድራጎት ድርጅቶች፣ የመንግስት ለጋሾችን እና ሌሎችን በማስተባበር የፓለቲካ ትኩረት እና የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ክትትል ላይ የገንዘብ አያያዝ ውጤታማነትን ለማስተባበር የሚያግዘው “Funders Forum” ነው። ይህ አካል ለአለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አባላት ስራዎች ተጨማሪ የገንዘብ ድጋፍን ያዘጋጃል። የህብረቱ ሲቋቋም የጀርመን መንግስት ለአለም ጤና ድርጅት በሰጠው የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ተደግፏል። ስለ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከል ፤
translations.reference: The COP and CSUA bring together organizations from across sectors, income levels, and geographies, with a commitment to international cooperation and equity, and deep expertise in either genomics or country scale-up of surveillance systems. The third body is the Funders Forum to coordinate philanthropic, multi-lateral and governmental donors around increased political attention and financing efficiency of pathogen genomic surveillance. The Funders Forum also catalyses additional grant funding for projects of IPSN members. The establishment of the IPSN has been supported by German government funding to the WHO Pandemic Hub. About the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"These two [entities] bring together organizations with a deep understanding of international cooperation and fairness, as well as expertise from various sectors, income levels, and regions. The third entity is the “Funders Forum,” which coordinates philanthropic organizations, government donors, and others to align political attention and the financial effectiveness of disease surveillance. This entity will mobilize additional financial support for the work of the International Coalition for Pathogen Research members. The coalition was established with the support of financial assistance provided by the German government to the World Health Organization. Regarding the World Health Organization's Pandemic Research Center..."



**Notes on the translation:**

*   I've tried to maintain a formal and accurate tone, reflecting the nature of the original text.
*   "[entities]" is used for "እነዚህ ሁለቱ" as the context doesn't specify what they are.
*   "disease surveillance" is used for "የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ክትትል" as it's the most accurate and understandable translation.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የድንገተኛ አደጋ ፕሮግራም አካል የሆነው የወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከሉ ሃገራትን የሚደግፉ ከበርካታ ዘርፎች የተወጣጡ አጋሮች መካከል ዓለም አቀፍ ትብብርን ያመቻቻል፤ ይህ ትብብር ለቀጣይ የወረርሽኝ ስጋቶችን ለመቅረፍ አገሮችን እና ባለድርሻ አካላትን በተሻለ የመረጃ ተደራሽነት፣ የተሻሻለ የጥናት አቅም እና የውሳኔ አሰጣጥ ግንዛቤዎችን ይደግፋል። ከጀርመን ፌዴራላዊ ሪፐብሊክ መንግስት በተገኘ ድጋፍ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ማዕከል እ.ኤ.አ. በመስከረም 2021 በበርሊን ለኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምላሽ ለመስጠት ተቋቁሟል፤ ወረርሽኙ ሀገሮች የህዝብ ጤና ስጋቶችን እንዴት እንደሚለዩ፣ እንደሚቆጣጠሩ እና እንደሚያስተዳድሩ በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ ድክመቶችን አጋልጧል። የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ማዕከል መረጃን የመለዋወጥ አቅማቸውን ለማጠናከር ከአባል ሀገራት እና ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልላዊ እና ሀገር አቀፍ ቢሮዎች ጋር በቅርበት ይሰራል፤ ለቅድመ ዝግጅት መረጃን ለመሰብሰብ እና ለማጥናት በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ አጋሮች እንዲተባበሩ እና መሳሪያዎችን እንዲያዳብሩ ያስችላቸዋል። በ150 አገሮች፣ በስድስት የክልል ቢሮዎች እና በጄኔቫ ዋና መሥሪያ ቤቱ የሚገኘው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ማዕከል በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ወረርሽኞችን እና የጤና አደጋዎችን በእኩል አጣዳፊነት እና በትጋት ለመፍታት አቅምን ይሰጣል። የአካባቢ፣ ክልላዊ እና ዓለም አቀፋዊ ተነሳሽነቶችን በማቀራረብ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ወረርሽኝ ማዕከል ለአዲስ ነገር ፈጣሪዎች፣ ለተመራማሪዎች እና ከተለያዩ ዘርፎች ለተውጣጡ ባለሙያዎች የትብብር መድረክን ያሳድጋል፤ ይህም ቴክኖሎጂን በመጠቀም ስራን በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለባለድርሻ ለማቅረብ ያስችላል።
translations.reference: Forming part of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence (the WHO Pandemic Hub), facilitates a global collaboration of partners from multiple sectors that supports countries and stakeholders to address future pandemic and epidemic risks with better access to data, better analytical capacities, and better tools and insights for decision-making. With support from the Government of the Federal Republic of Germany, the WHO Pandemic Hub was established in September 2021 in Berlin, in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, which demonstrated weaknesses around the world in how countries detect, monitor and manage public health threats. The WHO Pandemic Hub works closely with Member States and WHO regional and country offices to strengthen their data-sharing capacities and enable partners from around the world to collaborate and co-create tools to gather and analyse data for early warning surveillance. With a presence in more than 150 countries, six regional offices, and its Geneva headquarters, WHO’s reach gives us the ability to treat pandemic, epidemic and public health risks with equal urgency and diligence around the globe. By linking local, regional, and global initiatives, the WHO Pandemic Hub fosters a collaborative environment for innovators, scientists and experts from across a wide spectrum of disciplines, allowing us to leverage and share cutting-edge technology and anchoring our work in the needs of stakeholders around the world.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The Pandemic Hub, part of the World Health Organization's (WHO) Emergency Preparedness Program, facilitates international collaboration among partners from various sectors supporting countries. This collaboration strengthens countries and stakeholders' ability to address future pandemic threats through improved data accessibility, enhanced research capacity, and informed decision-making. With support from the Federal Republic of Germany, the WHO Pandemic Hub was established in Berlin in September 2021 to respond to the COVID-19 pandemic; the pandemic exposed gaps in how countries around the world identify, control, and manage public health threats. The WHO Pandemic Hub works closely with member states and WHO regional and country offices to strengthen their data exchange capabilities, enabling partners worldwide to collaborate and build capacity for collecting and analyzing data for preparedness. With a presence in 150 countries, six regional offices, and a headquarters in Geneva, the WHO Hub empowers the world to address pandemics and health emergencies with equal urgency and dedication. By bringing together local, regional, and global initiatives, the WHO Pandemic Hub fosters a collaborative platform for innovators, researchers, and experts from diverse sectors, enabling them to deliver work globally using technology."

translations.source: በተለያዩ ዘርፎች እና ክልሎች ያሉ ባለሙያዎችን በመጠቀም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ወረርሽኝ ማዕከል በአለም አቀፍ ትብብር እና እምነት ማዕቀፍ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ዓለም አቀፍ መፍትሄዎችን ለማዳበር ያስችላል።
translations.reference: Building on expertise across disciplines, sectors, and regions, it will leverage WHO’s convening power to foster global solutions built on an architecture of global collaboration and trust.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"By utilizing professionals in various sectors and regions, the World Health Organization's Pandemic Hub facilitates the development of global solutions based on a framework of international cooperation and trust."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመቱን ሲያከብር፤ ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ “ህይወትን በማዳን፤ ጤናን ለሁሉም ማድረስ” በሚል ርዕስ ትኩረት አድርጓል። ዓለማችን የጤና እና የሰብዓዊ ድንገተኛ አደጋ እያጋጠማት ባለበት በዚህ ጊዜ፤ ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ጤናን ለሁሉም ማድረስ ላይ ያተኩራል። የዘንድሮው ክፍለ ጊዜ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅትን የቅርብ እና የረጅም ጊዜ የወደፊት እጣ ፈንታን የሚወስን ሲሆን ይህም ለቀጣዮቹ ሁለት ዓመታት የሚመደበውን የፕሮግራሙን በጀት፣ የድርጅቱን ዘላቂ የገንዘብ አጠቃቀም እና የድርጅቱን ሂደቶች እና ተጠያቂነት ለማሳደግ ማሻሻያዎችን ጨምሮ ሌሎች ውሳኔዎችን ያካትታል። ልዑካኑ በድንገተኛ አደጋ ስራዓት ውስጥ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስላለው ወሳኝ ሚናም ይወያያሉ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ የምስረታ በዓሉን ሲያከብር እ.ኤ.አ. በ1948 ከተመሠረተበት ጊዜ ጀምሮ ያስመዘገበውን ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ስኬቶችን ያቀርባል።
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly to focus on “saving lives, driving health for all” as WHO turns 75 As the world faces ongoing health and humanitarian emergencies, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly will focus on driving forward health for all. This year’s session of the World Health Assembly will determine the immediate and longer-term future of WHO, starting with the program budget for the next two years, key decisions about the sustainable financing of the Organization and changes put in place to improve WHO’s processes and accountability. Delegates will also deliberate about the critical role that WHO has in the Global Health Emergency Architecture. The backdrop to these issues is the organization’s 75th anniversary, with the highlights of its collective global health achievements since WHO’s creation in 1948.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"As the World Health Organization (WHO) celebrates its 75th anniversary, the 76th World Health Assembly has focused on the theme "Saving Lives, Protecting Health." At a time when our world is facing health and humanitarian emergencies, the 76th World Health Assembly is focused on making health for all a reality. This year's session will determine the WHO's near and long-term future, including decisions regarding the program budget for the next two years, the organization's sustainable financial utilization, and improvements to enhance the organization's processes and accountability. Delegates will also discuss the WHO's critical role in emergency response. As the WHO celebrates its 75th anniversary, it will present global health achievements recorded since its establishment in 1948."

translations.source: መጪው 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ካለፈው ዓመት የተከናወኑ ሂደቶችን፣ ስኬቶችን እና ተግዳሮቶችን እንዲሁም የወደፊት ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ቁልፍ ስራዎች በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዋና መለኪያዎች እነዚህም ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን፣ ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች እንዲሁም ጤናን ማስተዋወቅ ይገመግማል። 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 21 እስከ 30 በጄኔቫ ፓሌይስ ዴ ኔሽን ይካሄዳል። ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረየሱስ “የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት የምስረታ በዓልን ስናከብር ከኮቪድ-19 ድንገተኛ አደጋ ምዕራፍ ተላቀን ሲሆን ካለፈው ጊዜ ተምረን ሁሉም ሰው አስፈላጊ የጤና አገልግሎቶችን የሚያገኝበት ነገን ለመፍጠር እየጣርን መሆን አለበት።” ብለዋል። "ይህም ማለት ለድንገተኛ የጤና ችግሮች ዝግጁነት፣ ምላሽ መስጠት እና መቋቋም መቻል ዓለም አቀፉን ስርዓት ማጠናከር፣ ሀገራት ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን እንዲያገኙ መርዳት እንዲሁም ጠንካራ እና ዘላቂ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ያለው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት መገንባትን ያካትታል።" "እ.ኤ.አ. እሁድ ግንቦት 21 ቀን 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤን ለማስጀመር አራተኛው ወክ ዘ ቶክ፡ ጤና ለሁሉም (Walk the Talk: Health for All) ቻሌንጅ በዴስ ኔሽን ከጠዋቱ በ8:30 ላይ ይጀምራል፤ (ማማሞቂያው ከጠዋቱ 7:30 ጀምሮ ይኖራል) ይህም በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት የምስረታ በዓል ላይ የአካል እና የአእምሮ ጤናን ለማስተዋወቅ ነው።
translations.reference: It will review last year’s progress, accomplishments and challenges along with future priorities across the key pillars of WHO’s work: Universal Health Coverage, Emergencies, Promoting Health and Well-being. WHA76 will convene 21-30 May at the Palais des Nations in Geneva. “As we mark WHO’s 75th anniversary, we can be proud of our past achievements, but we must be mindful of lessons learned, as we transition from the emergency phase of COVID-19, and create a future where every person has access to the health services they need,” said Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “This means strengthening the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response and resilience, supporting countries on the road to universal health coverage, and building a stronger, sustainably-funded and accountable WHO. "To kickstart WHA76 on Sunday 21 May, the fourth edition of Walk the Talk: Health for All challenge will start at Place des Nations at 8:30 am CEST (warm-up starts at 7:30 am CEST), to promote physical and mental health and celebrate the importance of partnership during the WHO’s 75th anniversary.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The upcoming 76th World Health Assembly will evaluate the processes, successes, and challenges of the past year, as well as the key work that will be prioritized in the future, according to the World Health Organization's main metrics. These metrics include universal health coverage, emergency preparedness, and health promotion. The 76th World Health Assembly will be held from May 21 to 30 at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.

The Director-General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, stated, "As we celebrate the 75th anniversary of the World Health Organization, we are moving beyond the COVID-19 emergency phase, and having learned from the past, we must strive to create a future where everyone has access to the essential health services they need." He added, "This includes being prepared for, responding to, and recovering from health emergencies, strengthening the global system, helping countries achieve universal health coverage, and building a World Health Organization with strong and sustainable financial support."

"On Sunday, May 21, the launch of the 76th World Health Assembly will begin with the fourth Walk the Talk: Health for All challenge at the Palais des Nations at 8:30 AM (the warm-up will begin at 7:30 AM), which aims to promote physical and mental health during the World Health Organization's 75th anniversary celebration."

translations.source: ከምሽቱ 2፡00 ሰዓት አካባቢ የልዑካን መሪዎቹ የቡድን ፎቶ መርሃ ግብርን ተከትሎ ከፍተኛ የእንኳን ደህና መጣችሁ አቀባበል እንዲሁም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር፣ የሀገር መሪዎች እና ልዩ እንግዶች ንግግሮች ይቀርባል። (ከታች የተናጋሪዎች ዝርዝር አለ።) በዚህ ክፍለ ጊዜ የመርሃ ግብሩ አስተባባሪ የህይወት ዘመን ስኬት ሽልማቶችን ለሁለት የአለም ጤና መሪዎች ያቀርባል። እ.ኤ.አ. ሰኞ ግንቦት 22 ቀን ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ዋና አጀንዳቸውን በ10፡00 ሰዓት አካባቢ ያቀርባሉ። እ.ኤ.አ. አርብ ግንቦት 26 ቀን የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ከአምስት የአለም አቀፍ ማህበራት ተወካዮች ጋር በመሆን አምስት የህዝብ ጤና ሽልማቶችን ለሰባቱ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚዎች ይሰጣሉ። ተሸላሚዎቹም የመጡት ከባህሬን፣ ቻይና፣ ጓቲማላ፣ ሊባኖስ፣ ኔፓል፣ ታይላንድ እና ፊሊፒንስ ነው።
translations.reference: At about 2:00 pm CEST, following a family photo with Heads of Delegations, the Assembly high-level welcome at Palais des Nations will feature speeches from the WHO Director-General, Heads of State and special guests. List of speakers below). During this session, the DG will present life-time achievement awards to two global health leaders. On Monday 22 May, the Director-General will give his main address at approximately 10:00 am. On Friday, 26 May, the WHO Director-General will be joined by representatives of five global foundations, to present five public health awards and prizes to the seven laureates for 2023. They come from Bahrain, China, Guatemala, Lebanon, Nepal, Thailand and the Philippines.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Around 2:00 PM, following the group photo ceremony as per the program, there will be a formal welcome reception, as well as speeches from the Director-General of the World Health Organization, heads of state, and special guests. (A list of speakers is provided below.) During this segment, the program coordinator will present Lifetime Achievement Awards to two World Health leaders. On Monday, May 22, 2023, the Director-General will present their main agenda around 10:00 AM. On Friday, May 26, 2023, the World Health Organization Director-General, along with representatives from five international organizations, will present five Public Health Awards to the seven winners of 2023. The winners are from Bahrain, China, Guatemala, Lebanon, Nepal, Thailand, and the Philippines."

translations.source: ቁልፍ ጉዳዮች፤ በሚከተሉት ላይ ቁልፍ ውይይቶች እና ውሳኔዎች ይጠበቃሉ፤ የአለም ጤና ጥበቃ ደንቦችን ጨምሮ የድንገተኛ የጤና አደጋዎችን በመቋቋም ዝግጁነትን እና ምላሽን በማጠናከር ላይ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስራ ግምገማ፤ እንደ የሴቶች፣ የሕፃናት እና የታዳጊ ወጣቶች ጤና፣ መልሶ ማቋቋም፣ ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ሽፋን እና የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ ጤና አጠባበቅ፣ የባህል ህክምና፣ ተላላፊ በሽታዎችን መከላከልና መቆጣጠር፣ ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ መድሃኒቶች፣ የስደተኞች ጤና፣ ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች፣ የአዕምሮ ጤና፣ አመጋገብ እና የአካል ጉዳት ላይ ስልቶች እና አለም አቀፍ እርምጃዎች፤ የተገመገሙ መዋጮዎችን መጨመር እና ሌሎች ዘላቂ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ጉዳዮች መገምገም ጨምሮ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2024-2025 የአለም ጤና ድርጅትን በጀት ማፅደቅ ናቸው።
translations.reference: Key issues Key discussions and decisions are expected on: A review of WHOs work in health emergencies, including the International Health Regulations and strengthening WHO preparedness for and response to health emergencies; Strategies and global action on areas such as women’s, children’s and adolescents’ health, rehabilitation, universal health coverage and primary health care, traditional medicine, infection prevention and control, substandard and falsified medicines, health of refugees and migrants, non-communicable diseases, mental health, social determinants, nutrition and disabilities; Approval of the WHO Programme Budget for 2024-2025, including the decision to increase assessed contributions and other matters emanating from the Working Group on Sustainable Financing.

translations.source: ዕለታዊ ስልታዊ ውይይቶች በምሳ ሰአት ከሰኞ እስከ ቅዳሜ በ18ኛው ክፍል ውስጥ ይካሄዳሉ። እነዚህ ዝግጅቶች በስብሰባ ተወካዮች፣ አጋር ድርጅቶች፣ የሲቪል ማህበረሰብ ተወካዮች እና የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ባለሙያዎች በሕዝብ ጤና ቅድሚያ የሚሰጧቸው ጉዳዮች ላይ ውይይት ለማድረግ ያስችላል። ክፍለ-ጊዜዎች እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 22 እስከ 27፣ ከ13፡00 እስከ 14፡15 ሰዓት የሚሄዱ ሲሆን በመስመር ላይ ሊከተሏቸው ይችላሉ። የጤና ጉባኤው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከፍተኛ ውሳኔ ሰጪ አካል እንደመሆኑ የድርጅቱን ፖሊሲ ያዘጋጃል እናም በጀቱን ያፀድቃል። በጤና ጉባኤው ከ194ቱ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት የተውጣጡ ልዑካን ይሳተፋሉ።
translations.reference: Daily Strategic roundtables will take place at lunch time from Monday to Saturday in Room XVIII. These events will host discussions among assembly delegates, partner agencies, representatives of civil society and WHO experts on public health priorities. Sessions run from 22-27 May, from 13:00 to 14:15 CEST and can be followed online. As WHO’s highest decision-making body, the Health Assembly sets out the Organization’s policy and approves its budget. WHA is attended by delegations from all WHO 194 Member States.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Daily strategic discussions will be held during lunchtime from Monday to Saturday in the 18th session. These events will allow for discussion on public health priority issues by conference representatives, partner organizations, civil society representatives, and World Health Organization experts. The sessions will take place from May 22 to 27, 2024, from 13:00 to 14:15, and can be followed online. The Health Assembly, as the World Health Organization's highest decision-making body, formulates the organization's policy and approves the budget. Delegates from the 194 World Health Organization member countries will participate in the Health Assembly."

translations.source: የጤና ጉባኤው አጀንዳ እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 17 እስከ 19 2023 በሚካሄደው የስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርድ 30ኛው የፕሮግራም፣ በጀት እና አስተዳደር ኮሚቴ ስብሰባ ቀርቧል። ከጉባዔው በኋላ 153ኛው የሥራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርድ ስብሰባ እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 31 እስከ ሰኔ 1 ቀን ድረስ ይካሄዳል። የጤና ጉባኤው ለተመዘገቡ እና እውቅና ለተሰጣቸው አባል ሀገራት፣ አጋር አባላት፣ ታዛቢዎች፣ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ጥሪ የተደረገላቸው ተወካዮች እና ሌሎች ተሳታፊዎች እንዲሁም መንግስታዊ ያልሆኑ ድርጅቶች ክፍት ነው። 76ኛው የጤና ጉባኤ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ድህረ ገጽ በቀጥታ ይለቀቃል። ጊዜያዊ አጀንዳውን፣ የመጀመሪያውን የጊዜ ሰሌዳ፣ የመሰብሰቢያ ሰነዶች እና ዕለታዊ ማስታወሻዎችን እዚህ ማግኘት ይቻላል።
translations.reference: The Health Assembly’s agenda is preceded by the 30th meeting of the Programme, Budget and Administration Committee of the Executive Board, which will meet on 17-19 May, 2023. After the Assembly, the 153th meeting of the Executive Board will take place from 31 May to 1 June. The Health Assembly is open to registered and accredited Member States, Associate Members, Observers, invited representatives of the UN and other participating inter-governmental organizations and non-State actors. WHA76 will be webcast live from the WHO website. The provisional agenda, preliminary timetable, Assembly documents and daily journal can be accessed here.Media accreditation and updates for the World Health Assembly
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"The agenda of the Health Assembly was presented at the 30th Program, Budget and Administration Committee meeting of the Executive Board, which will be held from May 17 to 19, 2023 (E.C.). Following the Assembly, the 153rd Executive Board meeting will be held from May 31 to June 1, 2023 (E.C.). The Health Assembly is open to registered and accredited member states, partner members, observers, representatives invited by the United Nations, other participants, and non-governmental organizations. The 76th Health Assembly will be broadcast live on the WHO website. The provisional agenda, initial timeline, meeting documents, and daily notes can be found here."

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   **E.C.:** This refers to the Ethiopian Calendar. I've included it in parentheses for clarity.
*   I've aimed for a clear and professional tone suitable for a formal document.

translations.source: የሚዲያ እውቅና በጄኔቫ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት ይሰጣል። በኢንዲኮ (Indico) በኩል ይመዝገቡ፤ ባጅዎን ለመውሰድ፡ እ.ኤ.አ. አርብ ግንቦት 19 (በስራ ሰዓት፡ ከ9፡00 እስከ 16፡30 ከሰዓት) በ “Villa Feuillantines, Place des Nations” ይገኙ። ዝርዝሮች እና አድራሻ ለማግኘት፡ https://unece.org/important-information-how-obtain-your-badge ይጎብኙ። እ.ኤ.አ. ቅዳሜ ግንቦት 20 በ”UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate” ከ13፡00 እስከ 17፡00 ሰዓት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. እሑድ ግንቦት 21 የሚዲያ ባጅዎን በ “UN Palais des Nations Pregny Gate” ከ9፡00 እስከ 16፡00 ሰዓት ድረስ ይሰብስቡ።
translations.reference: Media accreditation for WHA76 will be handled by the United Nations of Geneva. Register via Indico. To collect your badge: On Friday 19 May (working hours: 9:00 am to 16:30 pm CEST) at Villa Feuillantines, Place des Nations . Details and address : https://unece.org/important-information-how-obtain-your-badge On Saturday 20 May: UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate (opposite ICRC Building) from 13:00 to 17:00 CEST. On Sunday 21 May, you shall collect your media badge at the UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate (opposite ICRC Building) from 9:00am to 16:00 pm CEST
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Media accreditation is provided by the United Nations in Geneva. Register via Indico to receive your badge: pick up your badge on Friday, May 19 (during working hours: from 9:00 to 16:30) at “Villa Feuillantines, Place des Nations.” For details and address, visit: https://unece.org/important-information-how-obtain-your-badge. On Saturday, May 20, from 13:00 to 17:00 at the “UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate”; and on Sunday, May 21, collect your media badge from 9:00 to 16:00 at the “UN Palais des Nations Pregny Gate.”

translations.source: በሚቀጥለው ሳምንት እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 22 እስከ 26፤ የመገናኛ ብዙሃን ባጅ ለመሰብሰብ “Villa Les Feuillantines Place des Nations” ይገኙ። በጤናው ጉባኤ ላይ ያሉ ማሻሻያዎች በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ድረ-ገጽ ላይ ይለጠፋሉ፤ እና ተዛማጅነት ያላቸው ጉዳዮች ወደ ዓለም አቀፍ ሚዲያ ይላካሉ። ዜናን ለማግኘት ይመዝገቡ። ፎቶግራፎች እና ቪዲዮዎች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፎቶዎችን ለመገናኛ ብዙኃን አገልግሎት ከፈለጉ በፎቶ ቤተ መጻሕፍት በኩል ይገኛሉ።
translations.reference: Next week - 22-26 May- to collect the media badges: Villa Les Feuillantines at Place des Nations. Updates on the Health Assembly will be posted on the WHO website and relevant materials will be sent to the global media list. Register for updates. Photographs and videos WHO photos are available for media use via the WHO photo library.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"From May 22nd to 26th, 2024, media representatives will be at “Villa Les Feuillantines Place des Nations” to collect accreditation badges. Updates from the Health Assembly will be posted on the World Health Organization website, and relevant issues will be sent to international media. Subscribe to receive news. If media outlets wish to use photos and videos from the World Health Organization, they are available through the photo library."

translations.source: በዚህ ገጽ ላይ "የሚዲያ ምዝገባ" የሚለውን በመንካት ይመዝገቡ። ከፎቶዎች ጋር ለተያያዙ ጥያቄዎች ኢሜል ይጠቀሙ፤ የተቀዳ የስርጭት ቪዲዮ እና ሌሎች ይዘቶች ለመገናኛ ብዙሃን አገልግሎት ከፈለጉ በካንቶ “Canto” ላይ ይገኛሉ። ከቀጥታ ቪዲዮ ስርጭት ጋር ለተያያዙ ማናቸውም ጥያቄዎች፣ እባክዎን በኢሜል ይላኩ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት መደበኛ ዜናዎችን በትዊተር ያቀርባል፤ @WHO እና #WHA76 በማለት ይከተሉ። ከ#WHA76፡ Facebook፣ Instagram፣ LinkedIn፣ YouTube ዜናዎችን ለማግኘት የ WHOን ሌሎች የማህበራዊ ሚዲያ መለያዎችን ይከተሉ።
translations.reference: Register once for media access to the WHO photo library by clicking “media registration” on this page. For questions related to WHO photos email [email protected] Recorded broadcast video material and other press content will be available for media to download on Canto. For any questions related to live video transmission please email [email protected]. Social mediaWHO will provide regular updates from the World Health Assembly via Twitter –follow @WHO and hashtag #WHA76.Follow WHO’s other social media accounts for updates from #WHA76: Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Register by clicking on "Media Registration" on this page. Use email for questions related to photos; for recorded distribution videos and other content for media services, it is available on Canto. For any questions related to live video broadcasting, please send an email. The World Health Organization provides regular news on Twitter; follow @WHO and #WHA76. To get news on #WHA76: Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, follow WHO's other social media accounts."

translations.source: ከጤና ጋር የተያያዙ ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች እንዳይሳኩ ያስቆመውን ተግዳሮት ለመቅረፍ አስቸኳይ እርምጃ ያስፈልጋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ተፅእኖ ላይ አዳዲስ አሃዞችን እንዲሁም ከጤና ጋር በተያያዘ ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ላይ ለመድረስ በሚደረገው እንቅስዋሴ ላይ አዳዲስ መረጃዎችን በመያዝ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 እትሙን እያወጣ ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2000-2015 ከታዩት እንቅስቃሴዎች ጋር ሲነጻጸር የአሁኑ እስከ እ.ኤ.አ. 2022 ያለው መረጃ በቅርብ ዓመታት ውስጥ ቁልፍ በሆኑ የጤና አመልካቾች ላይ የጤና መሻሻል መቀዛቀዝ እንዳለ ያሳያል። በተጨማሪም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ያላቸውን ጉዳት በማጉላት የተቀናጀ እና ጠንካራ ምላሽ እንደሚያስፈልግ ያመለክታል። ኮቪድ-19 በጠፉ ህይወቶች እና በጤና መሻሻል ላይ ያስከፈለው ዋጋ፦ ዘገባው በአለም አቀፍ ጤና ላይ ወረርሽኙ ያስከተለውን አሃዝ አሻሽሏል፣ ይህም ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦችን ለማሳካት ያለው እድገት ማሽቆልቆል ላይ አስተዋፅዖ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: Urgent action needed to tackle stalled progress on health-related Sustainable Development Goals WHO is releasing the 2023 edition of its annual World Health Statistics report with new figures on the impact of COVID-19 pandemic and the latest statistics on progress towards the health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The report with data up to 2022 underscores a stagnation of health progress on key health indicators in recent years compared with trends seen during 2000-2015. It also alerts us to the growing threat of noncommunicable diseases (NCDs) and climate change, and calls for a coordinated and strengthened response. COVID-19 cost in lost lives and health progress The report documents updated statistics on the toll of the pandemic on global health, contributing to the ongoing decline in progress towards the SDGs.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Urgent action is needed to address the challenges that are preventing the achievement of health-related sustainable development goals. The World Health Organization is releasing its 2023 edition, presenting new figures on the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and new information on the obstacles to achieving health-related sustainable development goals. Compared to the activities seen from 2000-2015, the data from 2022 shows a slowdown in health improvements in key health indicators in recent years. Furthermore, it indicates the need for an integrated and robust response to highlight the damage caused by non-communicable diseases and climate change. The cost of COVID-19 in terms of lost lives and setbacks in health improvement: The report revises the figures for the impact of the pandemic on global health, contributing to a slowdown in progress towards achieving sustainable development goals."

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2020-2021 ኮቪድ-19 በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለ336.8 ሚሊዮን ሊኖሩ የሚችሉ አመታት መጥፋት ምክኒያት ሆኖል። ይህ ማለት ለእያንዳንዱ ሞት በአማካኝ ተጨማሪ 22 ሊኖሩ የሚችሉ አመታትን እንደማጣት ነው፤ ይህም በሚሊዮን የሚቆጠሩ ሰዎችን ህይወት ያሳጥራል። እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2000 ጀምሮ በእናቶች እና ህፃናት ጤና ላይ ከፍተኛ መሻሻሎችን አይተናል፤ በዚህም በቅደም ተከተል ሞት በአንድ ሶስተኛ እና በአንድ ሁለተኛ ቀንሷል። እንደ ኤች አይ ቪ፣ ሳንባ ነቀርሳ (ቲቢ) እና ወባ ያሉ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች የመከሰት አጋጣሚ እንዲሁም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና በጉዳት ምክንያት ያለጊዜው የሚመጣ የሞት መጠንም ቀንሷል። እነዚህ አንድ ላይ ሆነው፣ አማካኝ የእድሜ ጣሪያን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2000 67 ዓመታት ከነበረው እ.ኤ.አ. በ2019 ወደ 73 ዓመታት እንዲጨምር አስተዋፅዖ አድርገዋል።
translations.reference: During 2020-2021, COVID-19 resulted in a staggering 336.8 million years of life lost globally. This equates to an average of 22 years of life lost for every excess death, abruptly and tragically cutting short the lives of millions of people. Since 2000, we saw significant improvements in maternal and child health with deaths falling by one-third and one-half, respectively. The incidence of infectious diseases such as HIV, tuberculosis (TB) and malaria also declined, along with a lowered risk of premature deaths from NCDs and injuries. Together, these contributed to an increase in global life expectancy from 67 years in 2000 to 73 years in 2019.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"COVID-19, from 2020-2021, has been a cause for the loss of 336.8 million potential years of life globally. This means an average loss of an additional 22 potential years of life for each death, impacting the lives of millions of people. Since 2000, we have seen significant improvements in maternal and child health, resulting in a reduction in mortality by one-third and one-sixth, respectively. The incidence of infectious diseases such as HIV, tuberculosis (TB), and plague, as well as the premature death rate from non-communicable diseases and injuries, has also decreased. Together, these factors have contributed to an increase in the average life expectancy from 67 years in 2000 to 73 years in 2019."

translations.source: ይሁን እንጂ ወረርሽኙ ከመጣ በኋላ ብዙ የጤና እርምጃዎችን ያባባሰ ሲሆን ጥራት ያለው የጤና እንክብካቤ፣ መደበኛ ክትባቶች እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍን ለማግኘት ፍትሃዊ ያልሆኑ ልዩነቶች ጨምረዋል። በዚህም ምክንያት የወባ እና የቲቢ ህክምና መሻሻል አዝማሚያዎች መልሰው ተባብሰዋል፣ እናም ጥቂት ሰዎች ብቻ ችላ ከተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች ታክመዋል። “የዓለም ጤና አሃዝ ዘገባ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዓመታዊ የክትትል መንገድ ነው። ዘገባው ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችን በተመለከተ ግልጽ ማስጠንቀቂያ ሰቷል፤ በሰው ህይወት፣ በኑሮ፣ በጤና ስርአት፣ በማህበረሰብ እና በኢኮኖሚ ላይ በከፍተኛ ጉዳትንም ያደርሳሉ።” ብለዋል የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ። "ዘገባው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦችን ለማሳካት በጤና እና በጤና ስርዓቶች ላይ የሚደረጉ የገንዘብ ድጋፎች ላይ ከፍተኛ ጭማሪ እንዲያደርጉ ጠይቋል።"
translations.reference: However, the pandemic has put many health-related indicators further off-track and contributed to inequalities in access to high-quality health care, routine immunizations and financial protection. As a result, improving trends in malaria and TB have been reversed, and fewer people were treated for neglected tropical diseases (NTDs). “The World Health Statistics is WHO’s annual check-up on the state of the world’s health. The report sends a stark message on the threat of noncommunicable diseases, which take an immense and increasing toll on lives, livelihoods, health systems, communities, economies and societies,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “The report calls for a substantial increase in investments in health and health systems to get back on track towards the Sustainable Development Goals.”
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"However, after the pandemic struck, many health measures were weakened, and inequities in accessing quality healthcare, routine vaccinations, and financial support increased. As a result, trends in malaria and TB treatment have reversed, and only a few people are being treated for neglected tropical diseases. “The World Health Organization’s report is the World Health Organization’s annual monitoring pathway. The report has issued a clear warning regarding non-communicable diseases; they cause significant damage to human lives, livelihoods, health systems, communities, and economies.” said WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "The report calls for a significant increase in financial support for health and health systems to achieve the sustainable development goals."

translations.source: ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ለመጪው ትውልድ ትልቅ የጤና ስጋት እየሆኑ ነው፤ ምንም እንኳን በአጠቃላይ ጤና ላይ መሻሻሎች ቢኖሩም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ግን በየዓመቱ ያለማቋረጥ እያደጉ መተዋል እናም አሁን በየዓመቱ ሶስት አራተኛ የሚጠጋው ሞት ምክኒያት ናቸው። ይህ አዝማሚያ ከቀጠለ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች በዚህ ክፍለ ዘመን አጋማሽ ላይ ከ90 ሚሊዮን አመታዊ ሞት ውስጥ 86 በመቶውን ይይዛሉ ይህም ማለት 77 ሚሊዮን ሞት ማለት ነው፤ እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2019 ጋር ሲነጽጸር በፍፁም ቁጥሮች ወደ 90% የሚጠጋ ጭማሪ ያሳያል። የዘገየውን እድገት ማፋጠንን ይጠይቃል፤ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ብቻ ሳይሆን ሌሎች ብዙ አመላካቾች አመታዊ የመቀነስ ፍጥነት የመቀዛቀዝ ምልክቶች ያሳያሉ። ለምሳሌ የዓለም አቀፍ የእናቶች ሞት ጥምርታ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2021 እና 2030 መካከል በ11.6 በመቶ መቀነስ ይኖርበታል። በተመሳሳይ እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2015 እስከ 2021 የነበረው የቲቢ ክስተት መቀነስ የዓለም የጤና ድርጅት በሽታውን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 ለማጥፋት ካስቀመጠው ግብ ጋር ለመድረስ የተጓዘው አንድ አምስተኛ ብቻ ነበር።
translations.reference: NCDs ̶ an ever-increasing health threat for future generationsDespite overall health progress, the share of deaths caused annually by NCDs has grown consistently and is now claiming nearly three quarters of all lives lost each year. If this trend continues, NCDs are projected to account for about 86% of the 90 million annual deaths by mid-century; consequently, 77 million of these will be due to NCDs – a nearly 90% increase in absolute numbers since 2019. Stagnating progress calls for acceleration More recent trends show signs of slowdown in the annual rate of reduction (ARR) for many indicators. For example, the global maternal mortality ratio needs to decline by 11.6% per year between 2021 and 2030 to meet the SDG target. Similarly, the net reduction in TB incidence from 2015 to 2021 was only one-fifth of the way to the 2025 milestone of WHO’s End TB Strategy.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Non-communicable diseases are becoming a major health threat for future generations; despite overall improvements in health, non-communicable diseases continue to grow steadily each year and are now the cause of nearly three-quarters of all deaths annually. If this trend continues, non-communicable diseases will account for 86 percent of the 90 million annual deaths by the middle of this century, which equates to 77 million deaths; this represents an increase of nearly 90% compared to 2019 in absolute numbers. It requires accelerating the stalled progress; non-communicable diseases, along with many other indicators, are showing signs of slowing down the rate of annual reduction. For example, the global maternal mortality rate needs to decrease by 11.6 percent between 2021 and 2030. Similarly, the reduction in TB cases from 2015 to 2021, which was only one-fifth of the goal set by the World Health Organization to eliminate the disease by 2025."

translations.source: እንደ ትምባሆ መጠቀም፣ አልኮል መጠጣት፣ የወንጀል ጥቃት፣ የተበከለ ውሃ መጠቀም እና የህጻናት መቀንጨር አደጋዎችን በተመለከተ ተጋላጭነት ቢቀንስም በቂ ግን አልነበረም፤ አንዳንድ እንደ የአየር ብክለት ያሉ አደጋዎች ደግሞ ምንም ለውጥ ያላሳዩ ናቸው። በሚያስደነግጥ ሁኔታ ከመጠን ያለፈ ውፍረት በከፍተኛ ፍጥነት እየጨመረ ነው። በተጨማሪም የተሻለ የጤና አገልግሎቶች ተደራሽነት ከቅድመ እ.ኤ.አ. 2015 ግኝቶች ጋር ሲነፃፀር የቀነሰ ሲሆን በጤና አጠባበቅ ወጪዎች ምክንያት የገንዘብ ችግርን በመቀነስ ረገድ ምንም ጉልህ መሻሻል አላሳየም። ይህ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋንን የማሳካት አቅማችንን በእጅጉ ይገድባል። “የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ እድገት ሁሌ የሚጨምር እንዳልሆነና ዋስትና እንደሌለው ማሳያ ነው።” ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመረጃና ጥናት ረዳት ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ሳሚራ አስማ አስጠንቅቀዋል።
translations.reference: Despite a reduction in exposure to many health risks – such as tobacco use, alcohol consumption, violence, unsafe water and sanitation, and child stunting – progress was inadequate and exposure to some risks such as air pollution remains high. Alarmingly, the prevalence of obesity is rising with no immediate sign of reversal. Furthermore, expanded access to essential health services has slowed compared to pre-2015 gains, coupled with no significant progress in reducing financial hardship due to health-care costs. This drastically limits our ability to achieve Universal Health Coverage by 2030. “The COVID-19 pandemic is an important reminder that progress is neither linear nor guaranteed,” warns Dr Samira Asma, WHO Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"While the vulnerability regarding risks such as tobacco use, alcohol consumption, criminal violence, using contaminated water, and child neglect has decreased, it was not sufficient; some risks, such as air pollution, have not shown any change at all. Surprisingly, obesity is increasing at a rapid pace. Furthermore, access to better health services has decreased compared to pre-2015 findings, and no significant improvement has been shown in reducing financial burdens due to healthcare costs. This severely limits our ability to achieve universal health coverage by 2030. “The COVID-19 pandemic’s progression shows that it is not always increasing and there is no guarantee,” warned Dr. Samira Asma, Assistant Director of Information and Research at the World Health Organization."

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ አጀንዳውን ለማሳካትና በሁሉም ሀገራት ሊለካ የሚችል ተፅእኖ ለማድረስ በቆራጥነት እና በጋራ መስራት አለብን። የዚህ አመት ዘገባ በአየር ንብረት ለውጥ እና በጤና ላይ ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ ራሱን የቻለ ክፍል ያካትታል፤ ይህም ወደፊት በሚወጣው ዘገባ ላይ የበለጠ ጠቃሚ እንደሚሆን እንጠብቃለን። ለዚህ እና ለሁሉም ሌሎች ጉዳዮች ወቅታዊ፣ አስተማማኝ እና ዝርዝር መረጃ እድገትን ለመከታተል እንዲሁም ብሄራዊ እና አለምአቀፍ የጤና ፖሊሲዎችን ለማሻሻል ወሳኝ ናቸው። የአርታዒ ማስታወሻ፡ የአለም ጤና አሃዝ ዘገባ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አመታዊ በጤና እና ጤና ነክ አመላካቾች ላይ የቅርብ ጊዜ መረጃዎችን የሚያሰባስብበት መንገድ ነው። ለጥያቄዎች ኢሜል ይጠቀሙ።
translations.reference: “To stay on track towards the 2030 SDG agenda, we must act decisively and collectively to deliver a measurable impact in all countries.” This year’s report includes for the first time a dedicated section on climate change and health, and we anticipate that this will be of more relevance in the report going forward. For this issue and all other areas timely, reliable and disaggregated data are critical to track progress and improve national and global health policies. Editor’s note: The World Health Statistics report is the World Health Organization’s (WHO) annual compilation of the most recent available data on health and health-related indicators. For inquiries, contact [email protected]
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"We must work resolutely and together to achieve the 2030 Sustainable Development Goals agenda and to make a measurable impact in all countries. This year's report includes a dedicated section on climate change and health for the first time; we expect this to be even more useful in future reports. For this and all other issues, timely, reliable, and detailed data are crucial for monitoring progress and improving national and international health policies. Editor's Note: The World Health Atlas report is the World Health Organization's method of collecting the latest information on health and health-related indicators annually.
