For inquiries, please email."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኃላፊ የአለም አቀፍ የጤና መሪዎች ሽልማቶችን አስታወቀ። ዣን ዣክ ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም እና ፒተር ፒዮት በህይወት ዘመናቸው ለጤና ላደረጉት ትጋት ክብር ሰጥተዋል። በሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኃላፊ ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ በዓለም ጤና ላይ ትልቅ ለውጥ ያመጡ ሰዎችን ለማክበር ሁለት ሽልማቶችን ይሰጣሉ። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2019 የተጀመረው የዳይሬክተሩ አለማቀፍ የመሪዎች ሽልማት ለዣን ዣክ ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም እና ፒተር ፒዮት ይሰጣሉ። ሁለቱም የተከበሩ ተመራማሪዎች እና የህዝብ ጤና መሪዎች ናቸው፤ በአለም አቀፍ ጤና ውስጥ የመሪነት ቦታዎችን ከመያዛቸው በፊትም የኢቦላ ምርመራ አካል ነበሩ።
translations.reference: WHO Director-General announces Global Health Leaders Awards Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot awarded for lifelong commitment to health WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will present two lifetime achievement awards to recognize outstanding contributions to advancing global health during the high-level opening session of the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly. The Director-General’s Global Leaders Awards, which were established in 2019, will go to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both recipients are distinguished scientists and public health leaders who were closely involved in the discovery of Ebola virus disease before advancing to leadership positions in global health.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"The Director-General of the World Health Organization announced the International Health Leaders Awards. Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot were honored for their lifelong dedication to health. At the 76th World Health Assembly, WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus stated that two awards will be given to recognize individuals who have made a significant impact on global health. The Director-General’s International Leaders Award, launched in 2019, will be presented to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both are distinguished researchers and public health leaders; they were part of the Ebola investigation before holding leadership positions in global health."

translations.source: ሽልማቱ የሰዎችን ጤንነት በመጠበቅ የህይወት ዘመን ስኬቶቻቸውን ያከብራል። የዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎ ፕሮፌሰር የሆኑት ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም ይህንን ሽልማት የሚያገኙት ለፈጠራ ሃሳባቸው፣ ግንባር ቀደም መሪ በመሆናቸው እና እንደ ኢቦላ ያሉ በሽታዎችን ለማከም ትልቅ አስተዋፅዖ በማድረጋቸው ነው ። በዴሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎ፣ በአፍሪካ እና በመላው አለም ጤና ላይ ትልቅ መሻሻል እንዲኖር ጥረቶችን አድርጓል። ፕሮፌሰር ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም በኪንሻሳ የዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ብሔራዊ ምርምር ተቋም ኃላፊ ናቸው፤ በኪንሻሳ የሕክምና ትምህርት ቤት መምህር እና የኮንጎ ሳይንስ አካዳሚ የመጀመሪያ ፕሬዚዳንትም ናቸው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ1976 በኢኳተር ግዛት፣ በማዕከላዊ ዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎ የመጀመሪያ የኢቦላ ወረርሽኝ ወቅት ዛየር ኢቦላቫይረስን ካገኙ ሰዎች አንዱ ነበር።
translations.reference: The award honours their lifetime achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions to the management and treatment of diseases such as Ebola virus disease. He has played a critical role in improving health outcomes in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the African region, and the global community. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director General of the DRC’s National Institute of Biomedical Research in Kinshasa, full Professor of Microbiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School and the inaugural president of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was part of the team that discovered the Zaïre ebolavirus during the first recorded outbreak in 1976, in Equateur Province, Central DRC.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"The award honors people's health and their lifelong achievements. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum of the Democratic Republic of Congo is receiving this award for his innovative ideas, his leadership at the forefront, and his significant contribution to treating diseases like Ebola. He has made efforts to bring about significant improvements in health in the Democratic Republic of Congo, in Africa, and throughout the world. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the head of the National Research Institute in Kinshasa, a lecturer at the Faculty of Medicine in Kinshasa, and the first president of the Congolese Academy of Sciences. In 1976, during the first Ebola outbreak in the Equateur province, in the Central Democratic Republic of Congo, he was among those who identified the Zaire Ebola virus."

translations.source: በኋላ ቫይረሱን ለመከላከል ከረዱ የመጀመሪያ ሕክምናዎች እና ክትባቶች ምርምር ላይም ከፍተኛ አስተዋጾ አድርጓል። ዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎን በሳይንስ የተሻለ ለማድረግ ጠንክሮ ሰርቷል፤ በሀገሪቱ ብዙ የምርምር ማዕከላትን አቋቁሞ በሽታዎችን ለመከላከል ረድቷል። የቤልጂየሙ ፕሮፌሰር ፒዮት ይህን ሽልማት ያገኘው ኤች አይ ቪ ኤድስን እና ሌሎች ትልልቅ የጤና ችግሮችን በመዋጋት ላሳዩት አስደናቂ አመራር ነው። እንደ የተባበሩት መንግስታት የኤድስ ፕሮግራም (UNAIDS) እና ሌሎች በመላው አለም ጤናን የተሻለ ያደረጉ ጠቃሚ የአለም ጤና መርሃ ግብሮች ትልቅ አካል ሆኖ ቆይቷል። የተባበሩት መንግስታት የኤድስ ፕሮግራምን በሚመራበት ጊዜ በዓለም ዙሪያ በኤድስ ላይ እርምጃ እንዲወስድ የሚገፋፉ መሪ ነበረ፤ በተጨማሪም 10 የተለያዩ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት (ተመድ) ቡድኖችን በማሰባሰብ የተሻለ ትብብር ለማድረግ ረድቷል።
translations.reference: He later helped design one of the first effective treatments against the virus and the deployment of experimental Ebola virus disease vaccines. He has worked tirelessly to build the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s scientific capacity, establishing multiple research facilities in the country, and has played key roles with WHO in fighting infectious diseases. Professor Piot from Belgium is being recognized for his extraordinary leadership in the response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and other significant global health challenges. He has played a vital role in launching major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others that have improved health outcomes around the world. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became the chief advocate for worldwide action against AIDS, also spearheading UN reform by bringing together 10 different UN organizations.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Following the virus, the initial treatments and vaccinations have also made a significant contribution to research. He has worked hard to improve the Democratic Republic of Congo scientifically, establishing many research centers in the country to help prevent diseases. Professor Piot of Belgium received this award for his remarkable leadership in combating HIV/AIDS and other major health problems. He has been a major part of the United Nations AIDS Programme (UNAIDS) and other important global health initiatives that have improved health worldwide. While leading the United Nations AIDS Programme, he was a leader pushing for action on AIDS around the world; furthermore, he helped to foster better cooperation by bringing together ten different United Nations agency teams."

translations.source: በተጨማሪም ፕሮፌሰር ፒዮት ቀደም ሲል የኢቦላ ቫይረስ እንዲገኝ እና ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ የታወቀውን ወረርሽኝ በቁጥጥር ስር ለማዋል አስተዋፅኦ አድርገዋል፤ በመስክ የበሽት ስርጭት ምርመራዎች ውስጥ ይሳተፋል። በርካታ የከፍተኛ ደረጃ ተነሳሽነቶችን መርቷል፤ ኮቪድ-19ን ጨምሮ ብዙ ከፍተኛ ደረጃ የማማከር ሚናዎች ውስጥ አገልግሏል እና በርካታ ሽልማቶችንም አግኝቷል። ፕሮፌሰር ፒዮት በአሁኑ ጊዜ የአውሮፓ ሃገሮች ዓለማቀፍ ድርጅት ፕሬዝዳንት ልዩ አማካሪ ሲሆኑ የአለም ጤና ፕሮፌሰር እና የቀድሞ የለንደን የንፅህና እና የሃሩሩ አካባቢዎች በሽታ ህክምና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው አገልግለዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ “እነዚህ ድንቅ ግለሰቦች የዕድሜ ልክ ቁርጠኝነት፣ የማያቋርጥ የሰብዓዊ መብት ተሟጋችነት፣ ለፍትሃዊነት ቁርጠኝነት እና ከራስ ወዳድነት ነፃ የሆነ አገልግሎትን ያሳዩ ናቸው።” ብለዋል። “በብዙ ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች፣ የጤና ቀውሶች እና ግጭቶች ጊዜ በሕዝብ ጤና እና በሳይንስ ውስጥ ያለው አመራር ዓለም አቀፍ ጤናን ለማራመድ ከምንም በላይ ወሳኝ መሆኑን እንገነዘባለን።
translations.reference: Professor Piot also played a part in the earlier discovery of the Ebola virus, and the containment of the first known outbreak, participating in field epidemiological investigations. He has chaired a number of high-level initiatives, served in numerous high-level advisory roles, including on COVID-19, and has received multiple awards. Professor Piot is the Special Advisor to the EC President, a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. “These outstanding individuals embody lifelong dedication, relentless advocacy, a commitment to equity, and selfless service of humanity and people’s health,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “In a time of multiple emergencies, health crises, and conflict, we recognize that leadership in public health and science are more important than ever to advance global health.

"Professor Piot has previously contributed to the discovery of the Ebola virus and controlling the first outbreak, participating in field investigations of disease transmission. He has led numerous high-level initiatives, served in many high-level advisory roles, including COVID-19, and received numerous awards. Professor Piot is currently a special advisor to the President of the European Countries International Organization, and has served as a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London Hygiene and Environment and Disease Treatment. WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said, "These remarkable individuals demonstrate a lifetime commitment, tireless advocacy for human rights, dedication to equity, and selfless service free from self-interest." He added, "We recognize that leadership in public health and science is more critical than ever in navigating global health in times of numerous sudden disasters, health crises, and conflicts."

translations.source: የእኛ ተሸላሚዎች ዛሬ ያንን ትጋት ያሳያሉ።” "የእነሱ የጋራ ታሪኮቻቸው እና ስኬቶቻቸው በዓለም አቀፍ፣ በክልላዊ እና በብሔራዊ ደረጃ ትብብርን ማጎልበት የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ መስጠትን ለማሳደግ ምን ያህል መሻሻል እንዳመጣ ያሳያል። እነዚህ ልዩ ተመራማሪዎች በስራቸው በየቀኑ እኛን ማነሳሳታቸውን ይቀጥላሉ። ”ሲል አክሏል። የሽልማት ሥነ ሥርዓቱ እ.ኤ.አ. እሑድ ግንቦት 21 ቀን 2023 በጄኔቫ የሚካሄደው 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ከፍተኛ ደረጃ የመክፈቻ ስብሰባ አካል ነው።
translations.reference: Our awardees today personify that passion.” “Their combined stories and successes show how much the world has progressed in terms of collaboration at the global, regional and country levels to strengthen health emergency preparedness, response and resilience. These outstanding scientists will continue to inspire us every day,” he added The ceremony for the awards form part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly being held on Sunday 21 May 2023 in Geneva.
translations.hypothesis:

"Our laureates demonstrate that resilience today.” "Their shared stories and successes show how much improvement has been made in fostering collaboration at the global, regional, and national levels to enhance preparedness and response to health emergencies. These exceptional researchers continue to inspire us in their daily work.” he added. The award ceremony will be held on Sunday, May 21, 2023, in Geneva, as part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ቤኒን እና ማሊ ትራኮማን (በባክቴሪያ የሚመጣ የዓይን በሽታ) ከህዝብ ጤና ስጋት ስላጠፉት አድንቋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ቤኒን እና ማሊ ትራኮማን (በባክቴሪያ የሚመጣ የዓይን በሽታ) ከህዝብ ጤና ስጋት ማጥፋታቸውን ያረጋገጠ ሲሆን ይህም በአለም ጤና ድርጅት የአፍሪካ ክልል አምስተኛ እና ስድስተኛ ሃገራት አድርጓቸዋል ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ትራኮማ እንደሌላቸው የገለጸባቸው አገሮች ጋና (እ.ኤ.አ. ሰኔ 2018)፣ ጋምቢያ (እ.ኤ.አ. ሚያዝያ 2021)፣ ቶጎ (እ.ኤ.አ. ግንቦት 2022) እና ማላዊ (እ.ኤ.አ. መስከረም 2022) ናቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኃላፊ ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ "ለቤኒን እና ማሊ እንዲሁም ከትራኮማ በሽታ እንዲላቀቁ የረዷቸውን አጋሮች ሁሉ እንኳን ደስ አላችሁ ብለዋል። "ከቤኒን እና ማሊ ስኬት በኋላ አሁንም በአፍሪካ 23 የትራኮማ ችግር ያለባቸው ሀገራት አሉ፤ ነገርግን እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2021 እስከ 2030 ድረስ ችላ የተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ዕቅድ መሰረት በሽታውን ለማስወገድ እየተቃረብን ነው።
translations.reference: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.”
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"The World Health Organization (WHO) has removed Benin and Mali from the list of countries facing public health concerns regarding trachoma (an eye disease caused by bacteria). The WHO has confirmed that Benin and Mali have eliminated trachoma (a bacterial eye disease), making them the fifth and sixth countries in the WHO's Africa region to do so. Countries declared trachoma-free by the WHO include Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022), and Malawi (September 2022). WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus stated, "Congratulations to Benin and Mali, and to all partners who have helped them eliminate trachoma." He added, "Despite Benin and Mali's success, there are still 23 countries in Africa affected by trachoma; however, we are getting closer to eliminating the neglected tropical disease according to the 2021-2030 roadmap."

translations.source: በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ቤኒን እና ማሊ አሁን ከ15 ትራኮማን ከህብረተሰብ የጤና ችግር ካጠፍ ሃገራት ጋር ተቀላቅለዋል። እነዚህም ካምቦዲያ፣ ቻይና፣ ጋምቢያ፣ ጋና፣ ኢራን፣ ላኦስ፣ ማላዊ፣ ሜክሲኮ፣ ሞሮኮ፣ ምያንማር፣ ኔፓል፣ ኦማን፣ ሳዑዲ አረቢያ፣ ቶጎ እና ቫኑዋቱ ናቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የ”ሴፍ” SAFE ስልት፤ ቤኒን እና ማሊ ትራኮማን ለመግታት የሴፍ (SAFE) ስልትን ተግባራዊ አድርገዋል። የ”ሴፍ” ስልት ማለት በትራኮማ የሚመጡ ችግሮችን ለማስተካከል ቀዶ ጥገና፣ በሽታውን ለማስቆም የመድሃኒት ህክምና፣ የፊት ንጽህና መጠበቅ እና አካባቢን የተሻለ ማድረግን የሚይዝ ምህጻረ ቃል ነው። (SAFE- Surgery for trichiasis, Antibiotic therapy, Facial cleanliness and Environmental change)
translations.reference: Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

On a global scale, Benin and Mali have now joined 15 countries that have eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These include Cambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Iran, Laos, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo, and Vanuatu. The World Health Organization's "SAFE" strategy; Benin and Mali have implemented the SAFE (SAFE) strategy to eliminate trachoma. The "SAFE" strategy is an acronym that encompasses surgery to correct problems caused by trachoma, medication to stop the disease, maintaining facial hygiene, and improving the environment (SAFE - Surgery for trichiasis, Antibiotic therapy, Facial cleanliness, and Environmental change).

translations.source: ፋይዘር (Pfizer ) የተባለው የመድሀኒት አምራች ድርጅት ትራኮማን ለማጥፋት ለሚሰሩ ቡድኖች በነጻ azithromycin (የትራኮማ መድሃኒት) ይሰጣል። በብሔራዊ የበሽታ መከላከል ፕሮግራም ስር ቤኒን ትራኮማን ለማስወገድ ጥረቶችን ከሌሎች ችላ የተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ጋር እንድ ላይ ለመዋጋት ጥረቶችን አድርጋለቸ። ቤኒን ትራኮማን ከማጥፋቷ በፊት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2009 ድራኩንኩላይስስ (ወይም የጊኒ ወርም በመባል የሚታወቀውን የተበከለ ውሃ በመጠጣት የሚተላለፍ በሽት) እና እ.ኤ.አ. በ2021 ጋምቢይንዝ ሂውማን አፍሪካን ትራይፓኖሶሚሲስ (ወይም የእንቅልፍ በሽታ በመባል የሚታወቀውን በነፍሳት አማካኝነት የሚተላለፍ በሽት) ማጥፋት ችላለች። ማሊ በአገሪቷ አንዳንድ አካባቢዎች በሚከሰቱ ግጭቶች እና በመንግስት ላይ በተደረጉ ለውጦች ብዙ ፈተናዎች ቢኖሩባትም ትራኮማ የሰዎችን ጤና ምን ያህል እንደሚጎዳ ተመልክታ ጉዳዩን ለማስቆም ችላለች። ለማሊ ትራኮማ ችላ ከተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ማጥፋት የቻለችው የመጀመሪያ በሽታ ነው፤ በዚህም ቢያንስ አንድ ችላ የተባለ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ማስቆም ከቻሉ 47 አገሮች ጋር ተቀላቅላለች ።
translations.reference: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Pfizer, a pharmaceutical manufacturer, provides azithromycin (a trachoma medication) free of charge to teams working to eliminate trachoma. Under the National Disease Prevention Program, Benin has made efforts to combat trachoma alongside other neglected tropical diseases. Before eliminating trachoma, Benin eradicated Dracunculiasis (also known as Guinea worm, a parasitic disease transmitted through contaminated water) in 2009 and Gambiense Human African Trypanosomiasis (also known as sleeping sickness, a parasitic disease transmitted by insects) in 2021. Mali, despite facing challenges such as conflicts in some areas of the country and changes in government, has recognized the extent to which trachoma affects people's health and has been able to stop it. Trachoma is the first neglected tropical disease that Mali has eradicated, joining 47 other countries that have been able to eliminate at least one of these diseases.

translations.source: "ይህ ለህብረተሰብ ጤና መሻሻል ትልቅ ስኬት ነው።" ብለዋል ዶ/ር ኢብራሂማ ሶሴ ፎል የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ችላ የተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ፕሮግራም ኃላፊ። "የቤኒን እና የማሊ ስኬት መሪዎች፣ የተለያዩ ዘርፎች እና ማህበረሰቡ ተባብረው ከሰሩ በሽታዎችን ለማስቆም እንዴት ጠቃሚ እንደሆነ ያሳያሉ።" ትራኮማን በመዋጋት ረገድ በአለፉት ዓመታት ትልቅ መሻሻሎች እየታዩ ነው። በአፍሪካ ለትራኮማ መድኃኒት የሚያስፈልጋቸው ሰዎች ቁጥር እ.ኤ.አ. በ2014 ከነበረው 189 ሚሊዮን እ.ኤ.አ. በሰኔ 2022 ወደ 105 ሚሊዮን፤ ይህም ማለት በ84 ሚሊዮን ቀንሷል። ለአዘጋጁ ማስታወሻ፦ እ.ኤ.አ. እስከ ሰኔ 2022 ድረስ ትራኮማ በ41 ሀገራት እንደ ትልቅ የማህበረሰብ የጤና ችግር ሆኖ ቀጥሏል፤ በእነዚያ ቦታዎች ወደ 125 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ ሰዎች እርዳታ ያስፈልጋቸዋል።
translations.reference: “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"This is a major achievement for public health improvement," said Dr. Ibrahim Socé Fall, Director of the Neglected Tropical Diseases program at the World Health Organization. "The successes of Benin and Mali demonstrate how useful it is when leaders, different sectors, and the community work together to stop diseases." Significant improvements have been seen in the fight against trachoma in recent years. The number of people in Africa who need medication for trachoma has decreased from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022; a reduction of 84 million.

*For the Editor's Note:* As of June 2022, trachoma remains a major public health problem in 41 countries; an estimated 125 million people in those areas need assistance.

translations.source: ትራኮማ በአብዛኛው በአፍሪካ፣ በመካከለኛው እና በደቡብ አሜሪካ፣ በእስያ፣ በምዕራብ ፓሲፊክ እና በመካከለኛው ምስራቅ በሚገኙ ዝቀተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው እና ሩቅ በሆኑ አካባቢዎች በብዛት ይገኛል። ከነዚህም አፍሪካ ትልቁን ድርሻ ትይዛለች፤ ወደ 105 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ የትራኮማ ተጠቂዎች ሲኖሩ ይህም በዓለም ላይ ካሉት የትራኮማ ተጠቂዎች 84% ይይዛል። በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ በበሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ምክንያት የሚመጣ አይነ ስውርነት ቀዳሚው ምክኒያት ትራኮማ ነው። “ክላሚዲያ ትራኮማቲስ” የተባለ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን በሽታውን ያመጣል። በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያኑ ከሰው ወደ ሰው በንክኪ (አይን ወይም አፍንጫቸውን ከነኩ) ይተላለፋል፤ ከሰው ንክኪም በተጨማሪ ዝንብ ሊሰራጭው ይችላል።
translations.reference: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Trachoma is predominantly found in low-income and remote areas of Africa, Latin America and the Caribbean, Asia, the Western Pacific, and the Middle East. Africa accounts for the largest proportion, with an estimated 105 million people affected by trachoma, representing 84% of the global trachoma population. Globally, trachoma is the leading cause of preventable infectious blindness. It is caused by a bacterium called "Chlamydia trachomatis." The bacteria is transmitted from person to person through direct contact (e.g., touching eyes or nose) and can also be spread by flies."

translations.source: እንደ እጅን በደንብ አለመታጠብ፣ በተጨናነቁ ቦታዎች መኖር እና ንጹህ ውሃ ወይም መጸዳጃ ቤት አለማግኘት ያሉ ሁኔታዎች በሽታው እንዲዛመት ያደርጋሉ። እ.ኤ.አ. በ1996 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2020 ትራኮማን ለማጥፋት የሚሰራ ቡድን አቋቋሞ ነበር። ቡድኑን ከሚደግፉ ሌሎች አጋሮች ጋር በመሆን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ትራኮማን ለማስቆም ”ሴፍ” (SAFE) የተሰኘ ስልት ሀገራት እንዲተገብሩ ይረዳል፤ በተጨማሪም የትራኮማን ስርጭት እንዲፈትሹ፣ እንዲከታተሉ እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እንዲያገኙ ያግዟቸዋል። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2020 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፍኖተ ካርታ እንደ አውሮፓውያኑ አቆጣጠር በ2030 ትራኮማን ለማጥፋት አዲስ እቅድ ያዟል።
translations.reference: Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Conditions such as inadequate handwashing, living in crowded places, and lack of access to clean water or sanitation contribute to the spread of the disease. In 1996, the World Health Organization (WHO) established a team working to eliminate trachoma by 2020. In collaboration with supporting partners, the WHO helps countries implement the "SAFE" strategy to control trachoma; it also assists them in surveying, monitoring, and securing financial support for trachoma. In 2020, the World Health Organization developed a new plan to eliminate trachoma by 2030 according to the European calendar."

translations.source: 152 ሚሊዮን ሕፃናት ባለፉት አስር አመታት ውስጥ ከጊዜአቸው በፊት ነው የተወለዱት ። ከሚወለዱም 10 ሕፃናት፣ 1ዱ ያለጊዜው የሚወለድ ነው - እናም በየ 40 ሰከንዱ ከእነዚህ ህጻናት ውስጥ 1ዱ ይሞታል ። ያለጊዜ ወሊድ መጠን ባለፉት አስር አመታት በየትኛውም የአለም ክልል ውስጥ ለውጥ አላሳየም። የግጭት፣ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ፣ የኮቪድ-19 ተጽእኖ በየቦታው ለሴቶች እና ህጻናት ስጋቶች እየጨመረ ነው። በዚህ ለተጎዱት ሕፃናት እና ቤተሰባቸን የተሻለ እንክብካቤ ከማድረግ ጎን ለጎን ያለጊዜ ወሊድን መከላከልን ለማሻሻል አስቸኳይ እርምጃ ያስፈልጋል።
translations.reference: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"152 million children have been born prematurely in the past ten years. Of every 10 children born, 1 is born prematurely – and 1 of these children dies every 40 seconds. The rate of preterm birth has shown no change in any part of the world over the past ten years. Conflict, climate change, and the impact of COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and children everywhere. Urgent action is needed to improve the prevention of preterm birth alongside providing better care for affected children and their families."

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ በ2020 ወደ 13.4 ሚሊዮን የሚገመቱ ሕፃናት ከጊዜያቸው በፊት የተወለዱ ሲሆን፣ ወደ 1 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉት ደግሞ ከወሊድ በፊት በሚከሰቱ ችግሮች ህይወታቸውን አጥተዋል ሲል የተባበሩት መንግስታት ኤጀንሲዎች እና አጋሮች ዛሬ ይፋ ባደረጉት አዲስ ዘገባ አሳውቀዋል ። ይህም በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ከጊዜያቸው በፊት ከሚወለዱት (ማለትም ከ37 ሳምንታት እርግዝና በፊት) ከ10 ህጻናት 1ዱ ጋር በአንፃራዊው እኩል ነው። ያለጊዜ መወለድ፡- የአለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የተባበሩት መንግስታት የህጻናት ገንዘብ ለጋሽ ድርጅት ከእናቶች፣ ጨቅላ ህጻናትና ልጆች ጤና ማህበር ጋር በመተባበር በሴቶች፣ ህጻናት እና ታዳጊዎች ላይ በአለም ለአስር አመታት ያለጊዜ መወለድ ላይ ሲሰራ ቆይቷል ፣ ከጊዜ በፊት መወለድ “ ትኩረት ያላገኘ አሳሳቢ አደጋ” ነው በሚል የማንቂያ ጥሪን አድርገዋል ፣ በመጠኑ እና በአሳሳቢነቱ ለረጅም ጊዜ ትኩረት ሳያገኝ የቆየ ሲሆን ፣ ይህም የልጆችን ጤና እና ሕልውና የመሻሻል እድገትን እያደናቀፈ ነው። ዘገባው ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና ዩኒሴፍ የተሻሻለውን ጥናታዊ ግምቶች ያካትታል፣ አስቀድሞ ያለጊዜ መወለድ ስርጭት ላይ የተሰራ ሲሆን፣ ከለንደን የንጽህና እና የሀሩር ክልል ህክምና ትምህርት ቤት ጋር በመተባበር የተዘጋጀ ነው። ባጠቃላይም፣ እ.ኤ.አ ከ2010 እስከ 2020 ድረስ 152 ሚሊየን ተጋላጭ ህጻናት ያለጊዜያቸው መወለዳቸውን እና፣ ባለፉት አስርት አመታት በአለም ላይ በየትኛውም ክልል ውስጥ ያለ ያለጊዜ ወሊድ መጠን ምንም ለውጥ እንዳላመጣ አረጋግጧል።
translations.reference: An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"According to a new report released today by United Nations agencies and partners, an estimated 13.4 million babies were born prematurely in 2020, and approximately 1 million died from complications related to preterm birth. This is roughly equivalent to 1 in 10 babies born before 37 weeks of gestation worldwide.

Preterm birth: The World Health Organization and the United Nations Children's Fund, in collaboration with the Partnership for Maternal, Newborn & Child Health, have been working on preterm birth globally for a decade, issuing a call to action stating that preterm birth is a "neglected yet alarming crisis." It has long been overlooked due to its moderate and concerning nature, hindering the improvement of children's health and survival. The report includes revised research estimates from the World Health Organization and UNICEF, previously worked on the prevalence of preterm birth, and was prepared in collaboration with the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. Overall, from 2010 to 2020, 152 million vulnerable babies experienced preterm birth, and confirms that the rate of preterm birth has not changed in any region of the world over the past decade."

translations.source: ያለጊዜ መወለድ በአሁኑ ጊዜ የህጻናት ሞት ዋነኛ መንስኤ ሲሆን ይህም ከ 5 ኛ የልደት በዓላቸው በፊት ከሚሞቱት ህጻናት ከ5ቱ ከ 1 በላይ የሚሆኑት እነዚህ ህጻናት ናቸው። ያለጊዜ ወሊድ ተጠቂ ሆነው የተረፉ ሰዎች የዕድሜ ልክ የጤና መዘዝ ፣ የአካል ጉዳት ሰለባ የመሆን እድላቸው ከፍ ያለ እና የእድገት መዘግየት ሊያጋጥሟቸው ይችላል። በክልል፣ በገቢ፣ በዘር አኳያ ያሉ የመዳን ክፍተቶች እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2012 በርዕሱ ላይ ካለው ጉልህ ዘገባ በመነሳት ፣ ይህ 'ከጊዜው በፊት አዲስ የተወለደው “አስርት ዓመታት ”' የተሰኘው ዘገባ ያለጊዜ መወለድን ስርጭት እና በሴቶች ፣ በቤተሰቦች ፣ በማህበረሰቦች እና በኢኮኖሚዎች ላይ ስላለው ከፍተኛ ተፅእኖ አጠቃላይ እይታን ይሰጣል ። በጣም ብዙ ጊዜ፣ ህጻናት የሚወለዱበት ቦታ በሕይወት የመትረፋቸውን እጣ ይወስናል።
translations.reference: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Preterm birth is currently the leading cause of child mortality, with more than one in five children who die before their fifth birthday being preterm. Individuals who survive preterm birth may experience age-related health complications, a higher risk of disability, and developmental delays.  Highlighting regional, income, and ethnic disparities in survival, building on a significant report on the topic in 2012, this report, titled "The Decade of Early Childhood: Preterm Birth," provides an overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its significant impact on women, families, communities, and economies.  Far too often, the place a child is born determines their chances of survival."

translations.source: ዘገባውም፣ ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት እጅግ በጣም ያለጊዜያቸው ቀድመው (ከ28 ሳምንታት በፊት) ከሚወለዱት ከ10 ህጻናት መካከል 1ዱ ብቻ ሲሆን የሚተርፈው፣ ይህም ከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት ከ10ሩ ከ9 በላይ የሚሆኑት እንደሚተርፉ ገልጿል። በዘር፣ በብሄር፣ በገቢ እና በጥራት ያለው እንክብካቤ ተደራሽነት ጋር የተያያዙ የእኩልነት ልዩነቶች፣ ከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት ውስጥም ቢሆን ከጊዜ በፊት ያለ የወሊድ ፣የሞት እና የአካል ጉዳት የመከሰት እድሎችን ይወስናሉ። በደቡባዊ እስያ እና ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ ሀገራት ከፍተኛ ያለጊዜ የመወለድ ቁጥር ያላቸው ሲሆን በእነዚህ ክልሎች ውስጥ ያሉ ያለጊዜው የሚወለዱ ሕፃናት ከፍተኛውን የመሞት አደጋ ይጋረጥቸዋል። እነዚህ ሁለት ክልሎች አንድ ላይ ሆነውም በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ከ 65 በመቶ በላይ ያለውን ያለጊዜ-መወለድ ቁጥር ይይዛሉ። ዘገባው የግጭት፣ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ እና አካባቢያዊ አደጋ፣ የኮቪድ-19 ተጽእኖ፣ እና የኑሮ ውድነት በየቦታው ለሴቶች እና ህጻናት ስጋቶችን እየጨመረ መሆኑን አጉልቶ ያሳያል።
translations.reference: The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The report indicates that among 10 children born prematurely (before 28 weeks) in low-income countries, only 1 survives, while in high-income countries, more than 9 out of 10 survive. Inequalities related to ethnicity, nationality, income, and access to quality care influence the risk of preterm birth, death, and injury, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have a high number of preterm births, and children born prematurely in these regions face a higher risk of mortality. These two regions together account for more than 65 percent of the world's total preterm births. The report highlights that conflict, climate change, environmental disasters, the impact of COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and children worldwide."

translations.source: ለምሳሌም፣ የአየር ብክለት በየአመቱ ለ6 ሚሊዮን የሚሆን ያለጊዜ-መወለድ አስተዋፆን እንደሚያበረክት ይገመታል። በአለም ውስጥ ያለጊዜ-ከሚወለዱ ከ10ቱ ሕፃናት ውስጥም ወደ 1 የሚጠጉት ሕፃናት በሰብዓዊ ቀውሶች በተጠቁ 10 በጣም ደካማ አገሮች ውስጥ ይወለዳሉ ሲል በዘገባው ውስጥ ያለ አዲስ ትንታኔ አመልክቷል። የእናቶች የጤና አደጋዎችም፣ ለምሳሌ በጉርምስና ዕድሜ ላይ በሚገኙ ወጣቶች የሚከሰት እርግዝና እና ቅድመ-ኤክላምፕሲያ(pre-eclampsia) ማለትም በእርግዝና ወቅት ሊከሰት የሚችል ከፍተኛ የደም ግፊት ችግር፣ ያለጊዜ-መወለድ ጋር በቅርብ የተሳሰሩ ናቸው። ይህም፣ ውጤታማ የቤተሰብ ምጣኔን፣ከ ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው እንክብካቤ በእርግዝና እና በወሊድ ጊዜ ጋር ጨምሮ ፣የጾታዊ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤና አገልግሎቶችን ማግኘት አስፈላጊ መሆኑን ያሳያል። የተግባር አጀንዳ፡ ተጨማሪ የሀገራት የመዋዕለ ንዋይ ፍሳሽ እና በወላጆች የሚመራ እንቅስቃሴ
translations.reference: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"For example, it is estimated that air pollution contributes to 6 million preterm births annually. A new analysis in the report indicates that up to 1 in 10 preterm babies worldwide are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises. Maternal health risks, such as teenage pregnancy and pre-eclampsia (a high blood pressure condition that can occur during pregnancy), are also closely linked to preterm birth. This highlights the importance of accessing effective family planning, along with high-quality care during pregnancy and childbirth, and sexual and reproductive health services. Agenda for action: increased country resource flows and parent-led initiatives."

translations.source: ያለፉት አስርት አመታት በወላጆች ፣በጤና ባለሙያዎች ፣በተማረው አካል ፣በኗሪው ማህበረሰብ እና በሌሎች መረቦች የተደገፈ የቅድመ-ወሊድ እና ሞተው የሚወለዱ ጽንሶች መከላከያ፤ የማህበረሰብ እንቅስቃሴ እድገት ታይቷል። በአለም ዙሪያም፣ ያለጊዜ-በመወለድ ለተጎዱ ቤተሰቦች የቆሙ ቡድኖች፣ የተሻለ እንክብካቤ እንዲያገኙ እና የፖሊሲ ለውጥ እንዲደረግ እና ሌሎች ቤተሰቦችን በመደገፍ ግንባር ቀደም ናቸው። በደቡብ አፍሪካ፣ ኬፕታውን፣ ከግንቦት 8-11፣ በተካሄደው አለም አቀፍ የእናቶች እና የአራሱ ጤና ስብሰባን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት፣ ዩኒሴፍ፣ ዩኤንኤፍፒኤ(UNFPA) እና ፒኤምኤንሲኤች(PMNCH) በመተባበር የሴቶችን እና የአራስ ሕፃናትን እንክብካቤ ለማሻሻል እና ያለጊዜ በመወለድ የሚደርሱ ጉዳቶችን ለመቀነስ የሚከተሉትን ተግባራት እንዲያከናውኑ ጠይቀዋል። : የመዋዕለ ንዋይ ፍሰትን መጨመር፡- አለም አቀፍ እና የሀገር ውስጥ ሀብቶችን በማመቻቸት ወደ የእናቶችና የአራሱን ጤና ጥበቃ ላይ በማድረግ፣ ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው እንክብካቤን በሚያስፈልግበት ጊዜ እና ቦታ እንዲሰጥ ማድረግ። የተፋጠነ ትግበራ፡- ለእናቶች እና ለአራስ ሕፃናት እንክብካቤ የተቀመጡ ሀገራዊ ፖሊሲዎችን በመተግበር የእድገት ግቦችን ማሳካት።
translations.reference: The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Over the past ten years, supported by parents, health professionals, the educated community, local communities, and other networks, there has been growth in the prevention of preterm and stillbirths. Around the world, groups of standing families affected by untimely births are at the forefront of ensuring better care, advocating for policy changes, and supporting other families. In Cape Town, South Africa, from May 8-11, at the International Conference on Maternal and Newborn Health, organized in collaboration by the World Health Organization, UNICEF, UNFPA, and PMNCH, it was requested that the following actions be undertaken to improve the care of women and newborns and reduce the harm caused by untimely births:

*   **Increase the flow of resources:** By facilitating international and domestic resources to be directed towards maternal and newborn health care, ensuring high-quality care is provided at the time and place it is needed.
*   **Strengthen collaboration:** Implementing national policies established for the care of mothers and newborns to achieve development goals."

translations.source: በስራ መስኮች መካከል ውህደትን መፍጠር: በህይወት ዑደት ውስጥ ትምህርትን ማሳደግ; ከየመስኮች ጋር የተዋሃደ የጋራ የገንዘብ ምደባን በመያዝ; ብልህ የኢኮኖሚ ኢንቨስትመንቶችን መደገፍ፣ በህይወት መንገድ ውስጥ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ መላመድ ምላሾችን ማጠናከር; እና የአደጋ ጊዜ ስርዓቶችን ቅንጅት እና የመቋቋም አቅምን ማሻሻል። በአገር ውስጥ የሚመራ ፈጠራ፡ በአገር ውስጥ የሚመራ ፈጠራ እና ምርምር ላይ መዋዕለ ንዋይን ማፍሰስ፣ የእንክብካቤ ጥራት እና ተደራሽነት ላይ ማሻሻያዎችን ይደግፋል። ጥቅሶች፡- ፕሮፌሰር ጆይ ላውን፣ ከለንደን የንጽህና እና የሀሩር ክልል ሕክምና ትምህርት ቤት ያለጊዜው መወለድ፦ የአስር አመታት ስራ ያለጊዜ መወለድ ላይ (እ.ኤ. አ. 2023) እና ያለጊዜው መወለድ (እ.ኤ. አ. 2012)፤ " ይህ አዲስ ዘገባ እንደሚያሳየው ባለፉት አስርት ዓመታት ተገቢው እርምጃ ባለመወሰዱ ምክኒያት 152 ሚሊዮን ሕፃናት ያለጊዜያቸው ተወልደዋል።"
translations.reference: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**Creating Integration Among Work Fields: Enhancing Learning Throughout the Life Cycle; Holding a Unified Financial Allocation Integrated with Fields; Supporting Smart Economic Investments, Strengthening Climate Change Adaptation Responses Throughout Life; and Improving Disaster Response Systems and Resilience.** **Domestically-Led Innovation:** Investing in domestically-led innovation and research supports improvements in the quality and accessibility of care. **Quotes:** Professor Joy Lawn, from the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine, "Late Stillbirths" (2023) and "Stillbirths" (2012); "As this new report shows, 152 million babies were stillborn due to the failure to take appropriate action over the past decade."

translations.source: አንዳንድ ክልሎች የበለጠ ተጎጂዎች ቢሆኑም ፣ ያለጊዜ-መወለድ በእያንዳንዱ ሀገር የጤና እድገትን ላይ አደጋ ይጥላል። ለአደጋ የተጋለጡ አራስ ሕፃናትን ለመንከባከብ ከፍተኛ መዋዕለ ንዋይ ማፍሰስ በሚሊዮን የሚቆጠሩ ቤተሰቦችን ከልብ መሰበር ያድናል። ያለጊዜ-መወለድን ለመከላከልም ተጨማሪ ስራ ያስፈልጋል፤ ይህ ደግሞ ሞተው የሚወለዱ ጽንሶችን እና የእናቶችን ሞት በመቀነስ ረገድ ዘንድ ያለውን ጉዞ እድገት ያሻሽላል። እነዚህ መንትያ ያለጊዜ-መወለድን መከላከያ እና እንክብካቤ ዘዴዎች፣ በጋራ ኢኮኖሚያዊ እና ማህበራዊ እድገትን ለማምጣት ጤናማ ግለሰቦችን እና ማህበረሰቦችን ያፈራሉ። "ቀጣዩ ትውልዳችን አሁን በምንሠራው በሁላችንም ተግባር ላይ የተመሰረተ ነው - ኢንቨስትመንቱ ትንሽ ላይሆን ይችላል ነገር ግን የዚህ ኢንቨስትመንት ትርፍ ለእያንዳንዱ ሀገር ትልቅ ይሆናል።"""
translations.reference: While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Although some regions are more affected than others, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Spending significant resources to care for vulnerable preterm babies breaks the hearts of millions of families. More work is needed to prevent preterm birth; this improves the journey of progress in reducing the deaths of preterm babies and mothers. These prevention and care strategies for preterm birth create healthy individuals and communities, leading to shared economic and social progress. "Our next generation is based on everything we are doing now - the investment may not be small, but the return on this investment will be great for every country."

translations.source: ታንዛኒያዊው ዶ/ር ናህያ ሳሊም ' ያለጊዜው የተወለደው' ተባባሪ ደራሲ እና በሃገር አቀፍ ደረጃ ለጨቅላ ህጻናት አጋዥ መፍትሄዎች እና ቴክኖሎጂዎች መሪ ናቸው። "አሁን ለዝምታ ምንም ምክንያት የለም። አሁን የማህበረሰቡ በጣም ተጋላጭ ክፍል የሆኑትን ጨቅላ ህጻናት ውጤቶችን ለማሻሻል መሳሪያው እና እውቀቱ በእጃችን አለን። መንግስቴ አሁን ኢንቨስት ሲያደርግ እና በገጠር አካባቢዎች ጭምር እንኳን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ ቁርጠኛ ሆኖ በማየቴ ኩራት ይሰማኛል። የሚቀጥሉት አስርት አመታት በዚህ እና በሁሉም ቦታ ያሉ ያለጊዜ-መወለድ ችግርን ለሚጋፈጡ ሰዎች የተለየ ሊሆን ይችላል እናም መሆንም አለባቸው።"
translations.reference: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.”
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Tanzanian Dr. Nahya Salim is a 'timeless' composer and a leader nationally in assistive solutions and technologies for young children. 'Now there is no reason for silence. Now we have the tools and the knowledge to improve the outcomes for the most vulnerable members of our community – young children. I am proud to see my government investing and determined to implement it even in rural areas. The next ten years can and should be different for those facing the challenge of delayed development, and they must be.'"



**Notes on the translation:**

*   **"ታንዛኒያዊው" (Tanzanian):**  Clearly indicates the person's nationality.
*   **"ዶ/ር" (Dr.):** Standard abbreviation for Doctor.
*   **"አለጊዜው የተወለደው" (al-giziw yeteweldew):** This phrase is a bit nuanced. It refers to children experiencing delayed development or those with developmental delays. I've translated it as "delayed development" to capture the meaning accurately.
*   **"ጨቅላ ህጻናት" (cheqla hichot):**  Means "young children" or "small children."
*   **"ለዝምታ ምንም ምክንያት የለም" (le zimta mign yechin yetem):**  "There is no reason for silence" - implying a call to action.
*   **"ተጋላጭ ክፍል" (tegalaach kehel):** "Vulnerable members"
*   **"በገጠር አካባቢዎች ጭምር" (begtir achihabiyot chimir):** "Even in rural areas" - emphasizes the commitment to widespread implementation.
*   **" ያለጊዜ-መወለድ ችግርን" (al-giziw-me-weldin chigirn):** "The challenge of delayed development."

translations.source: ዶ/ር አንሹ ባነርጄ፣ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የእናቶች፣ አራስ ሕጻናት እና ጎረምሶች ጤና እና እርጅና ዳይሬክተር “ለእነዚህ ጥቃቅን፣ በጣም ተጋላጭ ለሆኑ ሕፃናት እና ቤተሰቦቻቸው ጥራት ያለው እንክብካቤን ማረጋገጥ የህጻናትን ጤና እና ህልውና ለማሻሻል የግድ አስፈላጊ ነው። በተጨማሪም ያለጊዜው መወለድን ለመከላከል መሻሻል ያስፈልጋል - ይህ ማለት እያንዳንዷ ሴት ከእርግዝና በፊት እና በእርግዝና ወቅት ችግሮችን ለመለየት እና ለመቆጣጠር ጥራት ያለው የጤና አገልግሎት ማግኘት መቻል አለባት።" ስቲቨን ሎዌረር፣በ ዩኒሴፍ፣ የጤና ዳይሬክተር (a.i.) "ከእያንዳንዱ ያለጊዜ-መወለድ ከሚያስከትለው ሞት በኋላ የኪሳራ እና የልብ ስብራት አሻራ አለ።
translations.reference: Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Amharic text:

"Dr. Anshu Banerjee, Director of Maternal, Newborn, Child Health and Nutrition at the World Health Organization, says, “Ensuring quality care for these small, highly vulnerable children and their families is absolutely essential to improve child health and survival. Furthermore, improvements are needed to prevent stillbirths – that means every woman must be able to access quality health services to identify and manage problems before and during pregnancy.” Steven Lowe, UNICEF, Acting Director of Health, "After every stillbirth, there is a trail of loss and heartbreak."

translations.source: አለም ባለፉት አስርት አመታት ያስመዘገበቻቸው በርካታ እድገቶች ቢኖሩም ያለጊዜያቸው የሚወለዱ ትንንሽ ህጻናትን በመቀነስ ወይም የሞት አደጋን በመከላከል ረገድ ምንም አይነት እድገት አላደረግንም። ጉዳቱም አስከፊ ነው። ለነፍሰ ጡር እናቶች እና ያለጊዜያቸው የሚወለዱ ሕፃናትን የመንከባከብ አገልግሎትን የምናሻሽልበት እና እያንዳንዱ ልጅ ጤናማ ጅምር እንዲያገኝ እና በህይወቱ እንዲበለጽግ የምናረጋግጥበት ጊዜ ነው። ሄልጋ ፎግስታድ፣ የፒ.ኤም.ኤን.ሲ.ኤች (PMNCH) ዋና ዳይሬክተር፣ "ያለጌዜ መወለድ(Born too soon) የተሰኘው ፅሁፍ፣ ለምን ኢንቨስትመንትን እና ተጠያቂነትን ማሳደግ እንዳለብን ያሰምርበታል - በአለም ላይ ከአምስት አመት በታች ለሆኑ ህጻናት ሞት ትልቁ ምክንያትም ነው።
translations.reference: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Although the world has seen numerous advancements over the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of babies born too soon or preventing stillbirths. The problem is devastating. It is time to improve the care for pregnant mothers and babies born too soon, and to ensure that every child has a healthy start and thrives throughout their life. Helga Fogstad, Director of the Partnership for Maternal, Newborn & Child Health (PMNCH), stated, "Born too soon" highlights why we need to increase investment and accountability – it is the largest cause of death for children under five years old globally."

translations.source: የእናቶች እና የጨቅላ ሕፃናት ጤና እንዲሁም ህፃናት ሞተው እንዳይወለዱ በመክላከል ረገድ የነበረው መሻሻል እየቀነሰ መቶል ይህም በኮቪድ-19 አስከፊ ውድመት፣ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ፣ በተስፋፉት ግጭቶች እና የኑሮ ውድነትን መጨመር ምክኒያቶች ነው ። "ከ መንግስታት፣ ከጋሾች፣ ከግሉ ባለሀብት ሴክተር፣ ከሲቪል ማህበረሰቡ፣ ከወላጆች እና ከጤና ባለሙያዎች ጋር በሽርክና በጋራ በመስራት -"ጸጥ ያለው ድንገተኛ አደጋ ( silent emergency)" የማንቂያ ማስጠንቀቂያ ልንሰጥ እንችላለን፣ እናም የቅድመ መከላከል እና የእንክብካቤ ጥረቶችን ወደ ሀገራት የጤና ጥበቃ እና ልማታዊ ጥረት ቢሮዎች፣ የሰው ሀይል ግንባታን ወደ ሚደግፉ ቤተሰቦች፣ ማህበረሰቦች እና ኢኮኖሚዎች በግንባር ቀደምነት በሁሉም ቦታ ማምጣት ነው።" ገብርኤላ እና ጀሮም ፎስተር'፣ በላስ ቬጋስ፣ ዩናይትድ ስቴትስ፡ የ'ጃለን ፎስተር' ወላጆች እና የጃለን የስጦታ ፋውንዴሽን መስራቾች "ፋውንዴሽናችን የተመሰረተው በቡድን ቢ(B) 'ስትሬፕቶኮከስ' በመባል በሚታወቀው የአዳጊ ጽንስን እድገት (Prenatal onset) በሚቀጨው ባክቴሪያ ምክንያት በ 27 ሳምንቱ ውስጥ ያለ ጊዜው ማህጸን ውስጥ ሞቶ የተወለደው ልጃችን 'ጃለን ፎስተር'ን በማጣታችን ነው። ልጃችንን ማጣት በጣም አሳዛኝ ነበር፤ እናም በማህበረሰቡ ውስጥ ምንም ድጋፍ አልነበረም እና ያኔም ነው ልጃቸው ለሞቱባቸው ቤተሰቦች የመጽናኛ እና የተስፋ ቦታ ለመስጠት የወሰንነው።
translations.reference: Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The progress made in improving the health of mothers and young children and preventing child mortality is slowing down, which is due to the devastating impact of COVID-19, climate change, ongoing conflicts, and rising cost of living. "By working in partnership and collaboratively with governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can issue a warning about this 'silent emergency,' and prioritize preventative and care efforts to national health care and development offices, supporting families, communities, and economies that build human capital everywhere." - Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: Parents of Jalen Foster and founders of the Jalen Foster Foundation. "Our foundation was established after the loss of our daughter, Jalen Foster, who died during the 27th week of pregnancy due to a bacterium called Group B (B) Streptococcus, which affects the developing fetus. Losing our daughter was incredibly painful; there was no support in the community, and that's when we decided to create a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a child."

translations.source: ሌሎች ቤተሰቦች - ብቻቸውን- እኛ እንደተሰማን እንዲሰማቸው አልፈለግንም ነበር። ስለዚህ፣ ሌሎች በኪሳራ ውስጥ ያሉ ቤተሰቦችን ለመርዳት፣ ብዙ የነጻ አገልግሎቶችን ለመስጠት፣ እንደ ፎቶግራፍ፣ የሀዘን ድጋፍ እና የመታሰቢያ አገልግሎቶችን ለመስጠት ስንል የ'ጃለን የስጦተሰ ፋውንዴሽን'ን ጀመርን። ***ያለጊዜው የተወለደው (Born too soon) የመገናኛ ክምችትን ወደ መገልገያዎ ያውርዱ
translations.reference: We didn’t want other families to feel like we did – alone. So, we started Jalen’s Gift Foundation to help other families going through a loss, to provide multiple free services, such as photograph, grief support and help towards memorial services.” ***Download the Born too soon media resources
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"Other families – they didn't want to feel alone as we did. Therefore, to help other families in need, to provide many free services, such as photography, grief support, and memorial services, we started the 'Jalen's Gift Foundation'. ***Download the 'Born too soon' connection kit to your device."

translations.source: የተባበሩት አረብ ኢሚሬትስ እና የአለም ጤና ድርጅት በአየር ላይ መጓጓዣ ወሳኝ የህክምና ቁሳቁሶችን ለሱዳን አደረሱ የተባበሩት አረብ ኢምሬትስ እና የአለም ጤና ድርጅት 30 ቶን አስቸኳይ የህክምና ቁሳቁስ ዛሬ ለሱዳን አደረሱ። ለአደጋ ጉዳት ህክምና የሚሆኑ፣ ለድንገተኛ ቀዶ ጥገና የሚሆኑ መሳሪያዎችን እና አስፈላጊ መድሃኒቶችን የያዘ አውሮፕላን ዛሬ ማለዳ የፖርት ሱዳን አውሮፕላን ማረፊያ አርፏል። 444ሺ የዩኤስ ዶላር የሚገመተው ጭነት፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በሱዳን የተከሰተው ግጭት ከጀመረ በኋላ በአየር መጓጓዣ ወደ ሱዳን ማድረስ የቻለው የመጀመሪያው ነው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አቅርቦቶቹን ግጭቱ ከመባባሱ በፊት ለጤና ተቋማት አከፋፍሏል።
translations.reference: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

**The United Arab Emirates and the World Health Organization Delivered Crucial Medical Supplies to Sudan**

The United Arab Emirates and the World Health Organization delivered 30 tons of emergency medical supplies to Sudan today. An aircraft carrying equipment for treating trauma injuries, surgical instruments for emergency operations, and essential medicines landed at Port Sudan Airport this evening. The shipment, valued at $444,000, is the first to be delivered to Sudan by air by the World Health Organization since the conflict in Sudan began. The World Health Organization has distributed its supplies to health facilities before the escalation of the conflict.

translations.source: እነዚህም አቅርቦቶች ከተጎዳው ህዝብ ቁጥር አንፃር ከተሰጡ ከጥቂት ቀናት በኋላ አልቀዋል። በተለይም ሀገሪቱ ከገባችበት ነባራዊ ሁኔታ አንጻር ሲታይ፣ የአውሮፕላኑ መላክ የተባበሩት አረብ ኤምሬትስ ለሱዳን ህዝብ ድጋፍ ለማድረግ በምታደርገው ተከታታይ የእርዳታ ስራ አካል እና ከሱዳን ጋር ያላትን ጥልቅ ግንኙነት የሚያንፀባርቅ ነው። ይህ እርዳታ የተባበሩት አረብ ኤሚሬቶችን የሰብአዊ እርዳታ ራዕይ የሚያሳይ ሲሆን እናም በሰብአዊ ወንድማማችነት ከሌሎች ሀገራት ጋር ያለውን ግንኙነት አጥብቆ ሲያሳይ፣ በድንገተኛ ሁኔታዎች እና በችግር ጊዜም ሌሎችን ለመርዳት ያለውን ቁርጠኝነት ያሳያል። "ዶክተር ሪም ቢንት ኢብራሂም አል ሃሺሚ፣ የአለም አቀፍ ትብብር ሚኒስትር ዴኤታ፣ ሲናገሩ "" በሱዳን ያለውን ወቅታዊ ችግር ለመፍታት ፣ወሳኝ የሎጂስቲክስ ስራዎችን የአስቸኳይ የህክምና እና የምግብ እርዳታን ለመላክ፣ የተባበሩት የአረብ ኤሚሬቶች ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ጋር በቅርበት መስራታቸውን ቀጥለዋል ።" አሁን በተፈጠረው ግጭት ተሳታፊ የሆኑ ሁሉንም ወገኖች ጦርነቱን በአስቸኳይ እንዲያቆሙ እና በሱዳን ህዝብ ላይ ያልተነገረለትን ስቃይ እያስከተለ ያለውን ሁኔታ እንዳይባባስ እንዲያደርጉ ከመጠየቁም ጎን ለጎን፣ የተባበሩት አረብ ኤምሬቶች በችግር ጊዜ በፍጥነት እና በታታሪነት ለአገሮች ርዳታ እና ድጋፍ ለማድረግ ቁርጠኝነታቸውን አሳይተዋል ።
translations.reference: These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"These supplies, considering the number of affected people, were exhausted within a few days. Particularly, considering the country's current situation, sending the plane is part of the United Arab Emirates' ongoing humanitarian efforts to support the people of Sudan and reflects the deep connection between the two countries. This assistance demonstrates the United Arab Emirates' humanitarian vision and strongly showcases its relationship with other countries based on human brotherhood, demonstrating its commitment to helping others in emergency situations and times of crisis. "Dr. Reem bint Ibrahim Al Hashemy, Minister of International Cooperation, stated, "To address the current situation in Sudan and to send critical logistical supplies, emergency medical and food aid, the United Arab Emirates continues to work closely with the World Health Organization." In addition to calling on all parties involved in the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and prevent the situation affecting the people of Sudan from worsening, the United Arab Emirates has demonstrated its commitment to providing rapid and courageous assistance and support to countries in times of need."

translations.source: የተባበሩት አረብ ኢሚሬትስ በሱዳኑ ሁኔታ በተለይ ተጋላጭ ከሆኑት ቡድኖች ለተጎዱ ወገኖች በተለይም በግጭት ምክንያት ከፍተኛ ተጋላጭ ለሆኑ ወገኖች ለታመሙት፣ ለሕጻናት፣ ለአዛውንቶች እና ለሴቶች በማቅረብ ላይ ያተኩራል- እነዚህም እርዳታዎች በሕክምና እና በምግብ አቅርቦት ላይ በጣም አስቸኳይ ክፍተቶችን ለመሙላት ይውላሉ። ከአጋሮቻቸው እና ከአለም አቀፍ ማህበራት ጋር በመሆን፣ አገሪቱ በችግር ጊዜ የሱዳንን ህዝብ መርዳቷን በመቀጠል ላይ ሆኖም ፣ የተባበሩት አረብ ኢሚሬትስ በጥልቅ የያዙት የሰብአዊ እሴቶች ሰላምን፣ ደህንነትን እና መረጋጋትን ለማጠናከር ያላትን ጠንካራ እና የማያቋርጥ ቁርጠኝነት ለአለም ማስተላለፏን አረጋግጣለች። የጤና አቅርቦቶቹ ሰብዓዊ ርዳታ ለሚያስፈልጋቸው 165,000 ሰዎች ወዲያውኑ ለመድረስ በቂ የሆነ የአካል ጉዳት ፣ የአደጋ ጊዜ የቀዶ ጥገና አቅርቦቶች እና አስፈላጊ መድሃኒቶች ያካትታሉ። በመላ ሀገሪቱ የሚገኙ የጤና ተቋማት መሰረታዊ የጤና አቅርቦቶች እንዳቋረጡ እና በጸጥታ ችግር ምክንያት የሀገር አቀፍ የህክምና መደብሮች ተደራሽ እንዳልሆኑ ዘገባ አቅርበዋል። ከሌሎች ድጋፎች መካከል፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከጭነቱ ጋር የሚጓዙ ሁለት የአደጋ ጊዜ የሎጂስቲክስ ባለሙያዎችን በማሰማራት እነዚህ የጤና ጥበቃ ባለሙያዎችን ለመደገፍ እና ለተቸገሩት አገልግሎት እንዲሰጡ ለማገዝ የሚረዱትን አቅርቦቶች ወዲያውኑ ለ13 ዋና ዋና የጤና ተቋማት መድረስ እንዲያረጋግጡ አድርጓል ።
translations.reference: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The United Arab Emirates focuses on providing assistance to those affected in Sudan, particularly vulnerable groups, especially those highly vulnerable due to the conflict – including the sick, children, the elderly, and women. These aids are directed towards filling the most urgent gaps in medical and food supplies. In collaboration with its partners and international organizations, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of need. However, the United Arab Emirates reaffirms its strong and unwavering commitment to peace, security, and stability, conveying this deep-rooted humanitarian value to the world. The health supplies, sufficient to immediately reach 165,000 people in need, include disability aids, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medications. Reports indicate that health institutions throughout the country have ceased basic supply deliveries, and national medical transport services are inaccessible due to security concerns. Among other supports, the World Health Organization has facilitated the immediate delivery of supplies to 13 major health institutions to support these healthcare professionals and enable them to provide services to those in need, by deploying two emergency logistics experts to accompany the convoys."

translations.source: ለወባ እና ተላላፊ ላልሆኑ እንደ የስኳር በሽታ እና የደም ግፊት ያሉ፣ ካልታከሙ ገዳይ ለሚሆኑ በሽታዎች፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሌላ 30 ሜትር ቶን የሚሆን አቅርቦቶች አዘጋጅቷል። ይህ እና 23ሺ የደም መለገሻ ላስቲኮች ፣በአለምአቀፋዊው የሰብአዊነት ከተማ ውስጥ በአለም ጤና ድርጅት በአለም አቀፉ የሎጂስቲክስ ማእከል ውስጥ እየተከማቸ ሲገኝ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅትም ከሱዳን የጤና ሚኒስተር ጋር በመተባበር እኚህን አቅራቦቶች እንዴት ወደ ሱዳን እንደሚያደርስ እያሰበ ይገኛል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የድንገተኛ አደጋ መርሀግብር ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ማይክል ራያን፣ “በተባበሩት አረብ ኤምሬትስ የሚገኘው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አለምአቀፋዊ ሎጅስቲክስ ማዕከል በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለድንገተኛ አደጋዎች ፈጣን ምላሽ የሚሰጥ ማዕከል ሆኗል። በሁሉም 6 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በሚንቀሳቀስባቸው መልካምድራዊ ክልሎች ውስጥ ከ140 በላይ ሀገራትን በመደገፍ ማዕከሉ በጤና ስርዓቶች ላይ ጉዳት ለሚያስከትሉት የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች እንደ በበሽታዎች ወረርሽኝ፣ እንደ ጎርፍ እና የመሬት መንቀጥቀጥ ባሉ የተፈጥሮአዊ አደጋዎች እና ግጭቶች ለተጠቁ አባል ሀገራት የህይወት አድን መስመርን ይሰጣል ። በተባበሩት አረብ ኤምሬትስ የሚሰጠው ድጋፍ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) በእነዚህ የጤና አስቸኳይ ምላሾች ግንባር ቀደም ሆኖ እንዲቆም ያስችለዋል - በሚያስፈልገው ቦታ እና በማንኛውም ጊዜ እርዳታ ለተቸገሩ ሰዎች ልዩ የህክምና እርዳታን ለማድረስ ያስችለዋል።
translations.reference: WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"For diseases that are deadly if left untreated, such as malaria and non-communicable diseases like diabetes and high blood pressure, the World Health Organization (WHO) has prepared an additional 30 metric tons of supplies. This, along with 23,000 blood collection lancets, is being stored at the WHO's global logistics hub in the global humanitarian city. The WHO is also working with the Sudanese Minister of Health to determine how to deliver these supplies to Sudan. Dr. Michael Ryan, Director of the WHO's Emergency Programme, stated, "The WHO's global logistics hub in the United Arab Emirates has become a center for rapid response to emergencies worldwide. Supporting over 140 countries across all six WHO regions, the hub provides a lifeline to member states affected by health emergencies, such as disease outbreaks, floods, earthquakes, and conflicts. The support provided in the United Arab Emirates allows the WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – enabling it to deliver specialized medical assistance to those in need, wherever and whenever they are."

translations.source: የአለም አቀፉን የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋ ዝግጁነት፣ ምላሽ እና ተቋቋሚነት ስርአተ መዋቅር ንድፍን ለማጠናከር በሚደረገው ጥረት እንደ አንደ አካል፣ የተባበሩት አረብ ኢሚሬትስ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና አጋሮች እጅግ በጣም በሚያስፈልጋቸው ጊዜ በአለም ውስጥ እጅግ ተጋላጭ የሆኑትን ሀገራት እርዳታ ለማድረስ እጅ ለእጅ ተያይዘው እየሰሩ ነው። ” የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የምስራቅ ሜዲትራኒያን አካባቢ ዳይሬክተር የሆኑት ዶ/ር አህመድ አል ማንድሃሪ በበኩላቸው፣ “የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ከሱዳን የጤና ባለስልጣናት ጋር በመቀናጀት አስፈላጊ የሆኑ የጤና አቅርቦቶችን ሁሉንም የተፈቀዱ መንገዶች በመጠቀም ማድረስ ቀጥሏል። በግጭቱ ምክንያት ሆስፒታሎች፣ ፋርማሲዎች እና የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ የጤና አገልግሎት መስጫ ተቋማት አስፈላጊ የሆኑ የህይወት አድን መድሃኒቶችን አከማችተዋል። የተጨማሪው 30 ሜትሪክ ቶን የአሰቃቂ እና የአደጋ ጊዜ የቀዶ ህክምና አቅርቦቶች እንዲሁም አስፈላጊ መድሃኒቶች መምጣት፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት 13 ዋና ዋና የጤና ተቋማትን ለመደገፍ እና ለተቸገሩ የጤና እንክብካቤ አገልግሎት መስጫ ተቋማትን መልሶ ለማቋቋም ያስችለዋል። በዱባይ የሚገኘው የአለምአቀፍ የማከማቻና የማከፋፈያ ማዕከል ያልተቋረጠ የጤና ሸቀጦች አቅርቦትን ለማረጋገጥ ወሳኝ ሚና ይጫወታል እናም እነዚህን በጣም አስፈላጊ የሆኑ አቅርቦቶችን ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ በአየር ላይ በረራ ለማድረግ፣ በተባበሩት አረብ ኢምሬትስ ለተደረገልን ድጋፍ እናመሰግናለን።
translations.reference: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"As part of the effort to strengthen the framework for global health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience, the United Arab Emirates, the World Health Organization (WHO), and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver assistance to the world's most vulnerable countries at times of greatest need. Dr. Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, stated, "WHO is continuing to deliver essential health supplies to Sudan in coordination with Sudanese health authorities, using all authorized channels. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies, and primary health care facilities are storing essential life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgical supplies, as well as essential medicines, will enable WHO to support 13 key health facilities and to restore services to health facilities serving those in need. The International Logistics and Distribution Center in Dubai plays a critical role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities, and we thank the United Arab Emirates for their support in facilitating the initial airlift of these vital supplies."

translations.source: ያለንን ክልላዊ 'ጤናን ለሁሉም' የሚለውን ራዕያችን በተግባር የሚንያየው በእንደዚህ አይነት ድጋፍ ነው። የአለም ጤና ድርጅት በተባበሩት አረብ ኤምሬትስ በኩል የሚሰጠው የህክምና እርዳታ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2022 ከውጭ አገር ለሱዳን ከቀረበው አጠቃላይ የህክምና ቁሳቁስ 55 በመቶ የሚጠጋ ሲሆን፣ ይህም የተባበሩት አረብ ኤሚሬቶች የአለም አቀፍ የሰብአዊ ርዳታ ዋና ማዕከል መሆኗን ያሳያል።
translations.reference: It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

"The support of this kind puts our regional vision of 'Health for All' into practice. The medical assistance provided by the World Health Organization through the United Arab Emirates accounted for approximately 55 percent of the total medical supplies received by Sudan from abroad in 2022, demonstrating the United Arab Emirates' role as a leading global humanitarian aid hub."

translations.source: ከጤና ጋር ወደ ተያያዙ የዘላቂ እድገት ግቦች(SDG) ላይ በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ እድገት ላይ በሀገር ውስጥ የሚደረግ ትብብር ቁልፍ ነው። በ13ቱ የአለም አቀፍ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር ለጤናማ ህይወት እና ደህንነት ለሁሉም (SDG3 GAP) ፈራሚ ኤጀንሲዎች በጋራ ዛሬ አዲስ የሂደት ለውጥ ዘገባ እየተለቀቀ ነው። ዘገባው፣ “ምን ሰራ? ምን አልሰራም? ቀጥሎስ ምን አለ?” ሀገሪቷን ከጤና ጋር በተያያዙ የወጡ ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦችን (SDGs) የትግበራ ግስጋሴን ለማፋጠን ትብብር እና ድጋፍን በማጠናከር ረገድ ተሞክረው የሰሩትን እና ያልሰሩትን በመዘርዘር የአራት ዓመታት የጋራ ስራ የባለብዙ ወገን ኤጀንሲዎች በዋናነት ያንፀባርቃል። ይህ አመት ለዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች (SDGs) የመተግበሪያ አጋማሹ ነጥብ ነው፣ ነገር ግን እነዚህ ዓለም አቀፋዊ ግቦች ላይ ለመድረስ ወደ ኋላ ቀርታለች። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2019 ከተጀመረ ጀምሮ፣ ሶስተኛው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድ (SDG3 GAP) በፈራሚዎቹ ኤጀንሲዎች መካከል እንደ ዘላቂ የገንዘብ ነክ እና የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ ጤና አጠባበቅ ባሉ ቁልፍ ቦታዎች ላይ አዲስ የትብብር መዋቅሮችን መፍጠር አስችሏል።
translations.reference: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Collaboration at the national level is key to progress on health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) globally. Today, a new progress report is being released by the participating agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP) for the 13th time. The report, “What worked? What didn’t work? And what’s next?” primarily reflects the collaborative work of multi-agency stakeholders over four years, outlining what has been tried and what hasn’t been done to accelerate the country's progress on health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by strengthening cooperation and support. This year marks the mid-point of implementation for the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), but progress towards these global goals is lagging. Since its launch in 2019, the third Global Action Plan for Sustainable Development Goals (SDG3 GAP) has enabled participating agencies to create new collaborative frameworks in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care."

translations.source: ቢያንስ 67 አገሮች በአንድ ወይም ከዚያ በላይ በሶስተኛው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድ (SDG3 GAP) አፋጣኝ ጭብጦች ላይ ተሰማርተዋል። ""እሰካሁን ጠቃሚ እድገቶችን አድርገናል፣ ነገር ግን የብዙ ወገን ድርጅቶች ሀገራትን ለመደገፍ በጋራ የሚሰሩበትን መንገድ ለማሻሻል አሁንም ረጅም መንገድ ይቀረናል።" አገሮች የሚነግሩንን ሰምተንም መመሪያቸውን ተግባራዊ ማድረግ አለብን"፣ ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር እና የሶስተኛው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድ (SDG3 GAP) ርእሰ መምህራን ቡድን ሊቀመንበር፣ ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ተናግረዋል። "አጋሮችን ለትብብብራቸው እና በዚህ ዘገባ ውስጥ ለተካተቱት ታማኝ ራስን የመገምገም ድርጊት አመሰግናለሁ። """ ዘገባው፣ አገሮች ይበልጥ በተሳለጠው ድጋፍ ተጠቃሚ እንዲሆኑ እና በሶስተኛው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድ (SDG3 GAP) ውስጥ የተገቡትን ቃል ኪዳኖች እውን ለማድረግ ስድስት ቁልፍ የውሳኔ ምክሮችን ይጠቅሳል።"
translations.reference: At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"At least 67 countries have engaged on one or more priority areas of the Global Action Plan on Sustainable Development Goal 3 (SDG3 GAP)." "We have made valuable progress so far, but there is still a long way to go to improve the way multiple partner organizations work together to support countries." We must listen to what countries are telling us and implement their guidance," said WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Reference Group, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "I thank partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessment actions included in this report."

The report highlights six key decision points to ensure countries benefit from increased engagement and to fulfill the commitments embedded in the Global Action Plan on Sustainable Development Goal 3 (SDG3 GAP)."

translations.source: ከእነዚህም መካከል፡- የሶስተኛውን ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድ (SDG3 GAP) ማሻሻያ ዑደት ለጤናን ለማስቀጠል፣ እንዴት በአገር ደረጃ ተባብረን መስራት እንደምንችል የአባል ሀገራትን አስተያየት በመሰብሰብ እና ሌሎች ለተዛማጅ ምክሮችን ምላሽ መስጠት ። ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድን(GAP) እንደ የትብብር መድረክ አርጎ ማስጠበቅ; በቀዳሚ ደረጃ ጤና አጠባበቅ ላይ በሀገር ደረጃ ጠንካራ ትብብርን ማጎልበት እናም እንደ የአየር ንብረት መቋቋም የሚችሉ የጤና ስርዓቶች ያሉ አዳዲስ ጭብጥ ርዕሶችን ማሰስ; በአገር ደረጃ አዳዲስ አቀራረቦችን መከተል ለምሳሌም እንደ ተፅእኖ የማድረግ አቀራረብ ፣
translations.reference: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Among these, the revision cycle of the Third Global Action Plan for Sustainable Development Goals (SDG3 GAP) for health aims to gather input from member states on how to work collaboratively at the national level and respond to related advice. It also seeks to safeguard the Global Action Plan (GAP) as a platform for collaboration; prioritize strengthening national collaboration in healthcare, and explore new themes such as climate-resilient health systems; and adopt new approaches at the national level, for example, an impact-driven approach."

translations.source: ከመደበኛ ማህበረሰብ ጋር የበለጠ መቀራረብ ; እና በፖለቲካ አመራር፣ በአስተዳደር አቅጣጫ እና ትብብርን ለመደገፍ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ በማድረግ የትብብር ማበረታቻዎችን ለማጠናከር ከአባል ሀገራት ጋር መስራት። የዘገባ ቁልፍ ግኝቶች በሚቀጥለው የተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት ጠቅላላ ጉባኤ የዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች (SDG) ስብሰባ ላይ እና እኤአ በመስከረም 2023 በዓለማቀፋዊ የጤና ሽፋን ላይ በሚደረገው የከፍተኛ ደረጃ ስብሰባ ውይይቶች ላይ ይተነተናሉ። እነዚህ ቁልፍ ክንውኖች ከመድረሳቸው በፊት ያሉት ወራት በአባል ሀገራት እና በባለብዙ ወገን ኤጀንሲዎች የውሳኔ ምክሮቹን እንዴት ተግባራዊ ማድረግ እንደሚቻል መንገዶችን ለመለየት እና ጠንካራ ትብብርን ሙሉ በሙሉ ለማጎልበት እና ከጤና ጋር በተገናኘ ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦችን (SDGs) በሁለተኛው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች (SDG) የጊዜ ሰሌዳ አጋማሽ ላይ ያለ እድገትን ለማፋጠን በጋራ ለመግፋት እድል ይሰጣል ። ማስታወሻ ለአዘጋጁ፡.
translations.reference: engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"To further engage with the broader community; and to strengthen cooperation incentives by working with member states to provide financial support for political leadership, administrative guidance, and to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will be discussed at the upcoming United Nations General Assembly meeting on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and at the high-level meeting on global health coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity to identify ways for member states and multi-stakeholder agencies to implement decision-making recommendations and to fully leverage strong collaboration and jointly accelerate progress towards sustainable development goals (SDGs) related to health at the midpoint of the second sustainable development goals (SDG) timeline. translations.source: የሶስተኛው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድ (SDG3 GAP) 13ቱ ፈራሚ ኤጀንሲዎች አዳዲስ የአሰራር ዘዴዎችን እንዴት እንደሚተገበሩ፣ አሁን ያሉ የተሳካ የትብብር አቅጣጫ ያላቸውን ማጎልበትን እና ድጋፋቸውን በሀገራት የተያዙ እና በሀገራት በሚመሩ ብሄራዊ እቅዶች እና ስትራቴጂዎች ዙሪያ በጋራ እንደሚያመሳስሉ ይገልጻል። የሶስተኛው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ዓለም አቀፋዊ የድርጊት እቅድ (SDG3 GAP) ፈራሚዎች ቀጣይ የተዘረዘሩት ናቸው: ጋቪ፣ የክትባት አሊያንስ; ለሴቶች፣ ለልጆች እና ለወጣቶች የተቋቋመ ዓለም አቀፍ የፋይናንስ ተቋም (GFF); ኤድስን, ሳንባ ነቀርሳን እና ወባን ለመዋጋት የተቋቋመ አለምአቀፋዊ የገንዘብ ርዳታ (ግሎባል ፈንድ); ዓለም አቀፍ የሥራ ድርጅት (ILO);
translations.reference: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO);
translations.hypothesis:

The partners of the Third Sustainable Development Goals (SDG3 GAP) Action Plan are listed below: Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; the Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); The Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria; International Labour Organization (ILO);"

translations.source: የተባበሩት መንግስታት የጋራ የኤችአይቪ / ኤድስ መርሀግብር (UNAIDS); የተባበሩት መንግስታት የልማት ፕሮግራም (UNDP); የተባበሩት መንግስታት የህዝብ የገንዘብ እርዳታ (UNFPA); የተባበሩት መንግስታት የህጻናት የገንዘብ እርዳታ (UNICEF); ዩኒትኤይድ(Unitaid); የተባበሩት መንግስታት የሥርዓተ-ፆታ እኩልነት እና የሴቶችን ማጎልበት (UN Women);
