translations.source: አዲስ የዳሰሳ ጥናት ውጤቶች፣ የጤና ስርዓት መዋቅሮች ከወረርሽኙ ከእንደገና ማገገም መጀመራቸውን ያሳያሉተ ከሶስት አመታት የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ በኋላ፣ የጤና ስርዓቶች በሀገሮች ውስጥ የመጀመሪያዎቹን የጤና ስርዓት መዋቅሮች ከእንደገና ማገገሚያ ምልክቶችን ማሳየት መጀመራቸውን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት “የአራተኛው ዙር የአለማቸን የልብ ምት ጥናት በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ወቅት አስፈላጊ የጤና አገልግሎቶችን ቀጣይነት: እ.ኤ.አ ከህዳር 2022 እስከ ጥር 2023" በተሰኘው ጊዜያዊ ዘገባ ላይ አመላክቷል ። እ.ኤ.አ. በ2023 መጀመሪያ ላይ፣ ሀገራት በመደበኛው የጤና አገልግሎት አሰጣጥ ላይ እንቅፋቶች እየቀነሱ መሆናቸውን ዘገባ አድርገዋል፣ ነገር ግን ለወደፊት ለማገገም እና ጠንካራ የመቋቋም አቅም እንዲኖራቸው ኢንቨስት መደረግ እንዳለበት በጥብቅ ጠቁመዋል። ለአራተኛው ዙር የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የልብ ምት ዳሰሳ ምላሽ ከሰጡ 139 ሀገራት መካከል፣ በአማካይ ወደ አንድ አራተኛ የሚጠጉ አገልግሎቶች መቋረጣቸውን ሀገራት ዘገባ አድርገዋል። የሰሞንኛ አዝማሚያ ትንተና ማድረግ በሚቻልባቸው 84 አገሮች፣ የተስተጓጉሉ አገልግሎቶች በመቶኛ ሲሰሉ እ.ኤ.አ ከሐምሌ-መስከረም 2020 በአማካይ ከ 56 በመቶ ከነበረው በመቶ እ.ኤ.አ በህዳር 2022 - ጥር 2023 ወደ 23 ቀንሷል።
translations.reference: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

**New Research Findings Show Health Systems Beginning to Recover from the Pandemic**

Following three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries are beginning to show signs of recovering their initial structures, according to the World Health Organization (WHO). This is highlighted in a working paper titled "COVID-19 Pandemic Response: Continuity of Essential Health Services – Fourth Round Pulse Survey, November 2022 – January 2023." The report states that at the beginning of 2023, countries are reducing obstacles to regular health service delivery, but strongly emphasizes the need for investment to ensure future recovery and build resilience.

Of the 139 countries that responded to the WHO’s fourth round Pulse Survey, an average of about one-quarter of services were disrupted.  Among the 84 countries where recent trends could be analyzed, the percentage of disrupted services decreased from an average of 56 percent during July-September 2020 to 23 percent during November 2022 – January 2023.

translations.source: ቀጣይነት ያለው መሰናክሎች በሁለቱም በፍላጎት እና በአቅርቦት-ጎን ምክንያቶች ሲሆን፣ በህብረተሰቡ ውስጥ ያለው ዝቅተኛ የጤና እንክብካቤ ፍላጎት እንዲሁም የጤና ባለሙያዎች አቅርቦት ውስንነት እና ሌሎች የጤና አጠባበቅ ግብዓቶች እንደ ክፍት ክሊኒኮች ወይም ተደራሽ የሆኑ የመድኃኒት ክምችት እና ምርቶችን ይጨምራል። "በአብዛኛዎቹ አገሮች ውስጥ ያሉ የጤና ስርዓት መዋቅሮች ወረርሽኙ በተከሰተበት ወቅት በሚሊዮን ለሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ያመለጧቸውን አስፈላጊ የጤና አገልግሎቶችን ወደ ነበሩበት መመለስ መጀመራቸውን ደስ የሚል ዜና ነው" ሲሉ የተቀናጀ የጤና አገልግሎት ዳይሬክተር የሆኑት ዶ/ር ሩዲ ኢገርስ ተናግረዋል። "ነገር ግን የጤና አገልግሎቶች መልሰው እንዲያገግሙ፣ ሁሉም ሀገሮች ይህንን ክፍተት መዝጋት መቀጠል እና ለወደፊቱ የበለጠ ዝግጁ እና ጠንካራ የጤና ስርዓቶችን ለመገንባት በዚህ የተማርነውን ትምህርቶች ተግባራዊ ማድረግ አለብን።" በመልሶ ማገገም ወቅት የሚታዮ የመጀመሪያ ምልክቶች በዚህ አዲስ የዳሰሳ ጥናት፣ እ.ኤ.አ ከ2020-2021 ከተዘገበበት ጊዜ ጀምሮ ጥቂት ሀገራት ሆን ብለው በሁሉም የአገልግሎት መስጫ መድረኮች እና አስፈላጊ የህዝብ ጤና ተግባራት ላይ ቅነሳ ማድረጋቸውን ዘገባ አድርገዋል፣ ይህም ከወረርሽኙ በፊት ወደነበረው የአገልግሎት ደረጃ እና ሰፊ የመዋቅር አካሄድ እንዲመለስ የተደረገ ትልቅ ጅምርን ያሳያል።
translations.reference: Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The persistent barriers exist due to both demand-side and supply-side factors, including low health service demand within the community, limited supply of health professionals, and other health inputs such as outreach clinics or accessible medicine supplies and products. "It is welcome news that health systems in most countries are beginning to revert essential health services to those millions of people who were disrupted during the pandemic," said Dr. Rudiger Egert, Director of Integrated Health Services. "However, for health services to fully recover, all countries must continue to close this gap and apply the lessons learned to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future." The initial signs seen during recovery, as reported in this new research study, indicate that a few countries have intentionally reduced services across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since the period reported from 2020-2021, demonstrating a significant step towards returning to the pre-pandemic level of service and a broad structural approach."

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በ2022 መገባደጃ ላይ፣ አብዛኞቹ አገሮች የወሲብ፣ የመዋለድ፣ የእናቶች፣ የአራስ ሕፃናት እና የታዳጊዎች ጤና አገልግሎትን ጨምሮ የአመጋገብ; የበሽታ መከላከያ; የተላላፊ በሽታዎች (ወባን፣ ኤችአይቪን፣ ቲቢን እና ሌሎችን በግብረ ሥጋ ግንኙነት የሚተላለፉ በሽታዎችን ጨምሮ); ችላ የተባሉ የሞቃታማ ክልል በሽታዎች; ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች; የአእምሮ፣ የነርቭ እና የአነቃቂ እና አደንዛዠ እፆች አጠቃቀም መዛባትን አመራር; የአረጋውያን እንክብካቤ; እና የባህላዊ እና/ወይም ከመደበኛ ህክምና ተጓዳኝ የተጨማሪ እንክብካቤ የጤና ጥበቃ አገልግሎቶች የመልሶ ማገገም ምልክቶችን ማሳየታቸውን ዘገባ አድርገዋል። በአገራዊ የአቅርቦት ሰንሰለት ውስጥ የስርዓተ መዋቅራቸው መዛባትን የሚዘግቡ ሀገራት ቁጥር ባለፈው አመት ውስጥ ከነበረው ግማሽ ከሚጠጋው አሀዝ (ከ59 ምላሽ ሰጪ ሀገራት 29) ወደ ሩብ ገደማ (ከ66 ምላሽ ሰጪ ሀገራት 18) ቀንሷል። የመልሶ ማገገም ምልክቶች ቢኖሩም፣ በሁሉም ክልሎች እና በተለያየ የገቢ ደረጃዎች ላይ በሚገኙ ሀገራት መሀል ፣ እናም በአብዛኛዎቹ የአገልግሎት መስጫ ቦታዎች እና የመከታተያ አገልግሎት መስጫ አካባቢዎች ላይ የአገልግሎት መስተጓጎል ታይቷል ። አገሮች በመጨመር ላይ በሚገኙ በወረርሽኙ ወቅት ያልተሰሩ የአገልግሎት ክምችቶች ላይ በመስራት ላይ ናቸው - ብዙውን ጊዜም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችን ለመለየት ፣ ለመመርመር እና ለማከም የሚሆኑ አገልግሎቶች ላይ ሲሆን - ይህም ሰዎች ወቅታዊ የጤና እንክብካቤን ለማግኘት መዘግየትን ስለሚያስከትል ወደ አሉታዊ የሆኑ መዘዞች ያመራል ። አስፈላጊ የጤና አገልግሎት አሰጣጥን መልሶ እንዲያገግም ማድረግ በጣም አስፈላጊ ነው ምክንያቱም የእነዚህ አገልግሎቶች መዛባት - የጤና ጥበቃን ማስተዋወቅ ጨምሮ፣ በሽታን መከላከል፣ ምርመራ፣ ህክምና፣ ማገገሚያ እና ማስታገሻ የመሳሰሉ የጤና አገልግሎቶች - በሕዝብ እና በግለሰብ ደረጃ፣ በተለይም በተጋላጭ የህብረተሰብ ክፍሎች ውስጥ ከበሽታው የበለጠ የከፋ የጤና ችግሮች ሊኖሩት ይችላል።
translations.reference: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"As of the end of 2022, most countries reported signs of recovery in health service delivery, including nutrition; reproductive, maternal, newborn, and child health services; immunization; infectious diseases (including sexually transmitted infections such as HIV, tuberculosis, and syphilis); neglected tropical diseases; non-communicable diseases; mental, neurological, and substance use disorders; care of the elderly; and additional care related to traditional and/or non-conventional medicine. The number of countries reporting disruptions to their national supply chain structures has decreased from nearly half (29 out of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 out of 66 responding countries) in the past year. While signs of recovery exist, service disruptions were observed across all regions and among countries at different income levels, and in most service delivery locations and areas providing follow-up services. Countries are working to address the backlog of services that were missed during the pandemic – often focusing on services for the detection, diagnosis, and treatment of non-communicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as it delays people from accessing timely health care. Restoring essential health service delivery is crucial because disruptions to these services – including health promotion, disease prevention, screening, treatment, rehabilitation, and palliative care – can lead to health problems that are worse than the pandemic itself at the population and individual levels, especially in vulnerable populations."

translations.source: የኮቪድ-19 አገልግሎቶችን ወደ አስፈላጊ የጤና አገልግሎቶች ውስጥ ማቀናጀት በስርአተ መልሶ ማገገሚያ እና ሽግግር ሂደት ውስጥ ሌላኛው ጠቃሚው የጉዞ ጅምር ነው፣ አብዛኞቹ ሀገራትም የኮቪድ-19 አገልግሎቶችን ከመደበኛ የጤና አገልግሎት አሰጣጥ ስርዓት ጋር በማዋሃድ እድገትን አሳይተዋል። ከ80 እስከ 90 በመቶ የሚሆኑ ሀገራት የኮቪድ-19 ክትባትን፣ የምርመራን እና በበሽታው የተያዙ ሰዎች ጉዳይ የአስተዳደር አገልግሎቶችን እንዲሁም የድህረ-ኮቪድ-19 አገልግሎቶችን ወደ መደበኛ አገልግሎት አሰጣጥ ሙሉ በሙሉ አዋህደዋል። አሁንም፣ አብዛኞቹ አገሮች (ከ83 ምላሽ ሰጪ አገሮች 80 በመቶዎቹ) አስፈላጊ የሆነውን የኮቪድ-19 መሳሪያዎችን (ለምሳሌ የኮቪድ-19 ምርመራ፣ ቴራፒዩቲክስ፣ ክትባቶች እና የግል መከላከያ መሣሪያዎች - PPE) ተደራሽነትን ጨምሮ፤ በጣም የተለመዱትን መሰናክሎችን የሚወክሉት የጤና ጥበቃ የሰው ሀይል ችግር እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እጥረትን መልሶ ለማገገም ከከለከሉ ማነቆ ጉዳዮች ቢያንስ አንዱን ዘገባ አድርገዋል። መልሶ ለማገገም፣ ለማመቋቋም እና ቀድሞ ዝግጁ ሆኖ ለመጠበቅ ተጨማሪ ድጋፍ ያስፈልጋል አብዛኞቹ ሀገራት በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ወቅት የተማሩትን ተግባራዊ ማድረግ የጀመሩ ሲሆን፣ ከእነዚህም መካከልም በርካታ አዳዲስ የፈጠራ የአገልግሎት መቆራረጥ ቅነሳ ስልቶችን ወደ መደበኛ የጤና አገልግሎት አሰጣጥን ተቋማዊ ማድረግን ይጨምራል።
translations.reference: Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services is another crucial step in the recovery and transition process, and most countries have shown progress by integrating COVID-19 services with the regular health service delivery system. From 80 to 90 percent of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, testing, and case management services for those infected, as well as post-COVID-19 services, into routine service delivery. However, most countries (80 percent of 83 responding countries) have reported at least one impediment to recovering COVID-19 equipment accessibility (e.g., COVID-19 testing, therapeutics, vaccines, and personal protective equipment - PPE), including the most common challenges of health workforce shortages and financial constraints. To recover, establish, and be prepared for the future, additional support is needed. Most countries have begun to implement lessons learned during the COVID-19 pandemic, including institutionalizing several new innovative strategies for service disruption reduction into routine health service delivery."

translations.source: እነዚህም፣ የቴሌሜዲሲን የጤና ጥበቃ አቀራረቦችን ማስተግበርን፣ የቤት ውስጥ እንክብካቤን ወይም የራስን ጤና አጠባበቅ ግኝቶችን ማስተዋወቅ፣ የጤና ባለሙያዎችን አቅርቦት የማጠናከር አቀራረቦች፣ አቅምን የመገንባት እና ድጋፍን የማሰባሰብ ዘዴዎችን መጠቀም፣ መድሃኒቶችን እና አቅርቦቶችን በመግዛት እና በማድረስ ላይ የተገኙ ፈጠራዎች ማስተግበር፣ የለት ከለት የማህበረሰብ ግንኙነቶችን ማጠናከር እና አጋርነት ከግሉ ዘርፍ አቅራቢዎች ጋር መፍጠር ናቸው። ሶስት አራተኛ የሚሆኑት ሀገራት የረዥም ጊዜ የመልሶ ማገገም፣ የመቋቋም እና የቀድሞ ዝግጁነት ስርዓተ መዋቅርን ለማስፈፀም ተጨማሪ የገንዘብ ድልድል እንደሚያስፈልግ ዘገባ አድርገዋል። ሀገራት በኮቪድ-19 አውድ ውስጥ እና ከዛም በላይ ያሉ ቀሪ ተግዳሮቶችን ለመቅረፍ ፤ በተለይም ከጤና የሰው ሃይል ማጠናከር ላይ፣ የጤና አገልግሎትን የመከታተል አቅምን ማሳደግ ላይ፣ የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ የጤና እንክብካቤን- ተኮር ሞዴሎች መንደፍ ላይ ፣ አስተዳደር ላይ፣ ፖሊሲ እና እቅድ ላይ እና የገንዘብ ነክ እቅድ እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ላይ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ድጋፍ እንደሚያስፈልግ ገለጸዋል። ለአዘጋጆች ማስታወሻ ፡ በአራተኛው ዙር የአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) የአለም የልብ ምት ዳሰሳ ላይ፣ 222 ሀገራት፣ ግዛቶች እና አካባቢዎች እ.ኤ.አ በህዳር 2022 እና በጥር 2023 መካከል ደረጃውን የጠበቀ በድረ-ገጽ የተመሰረተ የአሰሳ ጥናት ላይ ምላሽ እንዲሰጡ ተጋብዘዋል። አራተኛው የዳሰሳ ጥናት ቀደም ካሉት እ.ኤ.አ የ2020 እና 2021 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የልብ ምት ዳሰሳዎች ላይ ተከትሎ ሲመጣ፡ 1ኛው ዙር (እ.ኤ.አ ከግንቦት-መስከረም 2020)፣ 2ኛ ዙርው (እ.ኤ.አ ከጥር - መጋቢት 2021) እና ዙር 3 (እ.ኤ.አ ከህዳር - ታህሣሥ 2021) ሲሆኑ፣ እነዚህም ዳሰሳዎች ወረርሽኙ አስፈላጊ የሆኑ የጤና አገልግሎቶችን ቀጣይነት ምን ያህል እየጎዳ እንደነበር እና አገሮችም እንዴት አይነት እርምጃ እየወሰዱ እንደሆነ አሳይተዋል ።
translations.reference: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"These include implementing telemedicine healthcare approaches, promoting home care or self-health management initiatives, approaches to strengthen the supply of health professionals, building capacity and mobilizing support mechanisms, implementing innovations related to procurement and delivery of medicines and supplies, strengthening daily community connections, and forging partnerships with private sector providers. Three-quarters of countries have reported the need for additional financial resources to implement long-term recovery, resilience, and preparedness frameworks. Countries have stated that they require the support of the World Health Organization (WHO) to address the remaining challenges within and beyond the COVID-19 context, particularly in strengthening the health workforce, increasing the capacity to monitor health services, establishing primary health care-focused models, governance, policy and planning, and financial planning and resource mobilization. For Editors' Note: In the fourth round of the World Health Organization (WHO) Pulse Surveys, 222 countries, territories, and areas were invited to respond to a standardized, web-based assessment survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey follows the previous WHO Pulse Surveys of 2020 and 2021 – Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) – which showed the extent to which the pandemic was disrupting essential health services and the types of actions countries were taking."

translations.source: የልብ ምት ዳሰሳዎቹ እንደ የአድሎአዊነት እና የተወካይነት ዘገባን የማድረግ ውስንነት ቢኖራቸውም፣ የዚህ ጥረት ጥንካሬ ደሞ ሁሉን አቀፍ እና መረጃን በፍጥነት የሚያደርስ መሆኑ ነው። "ሀገር" የሚለው ቃል ሁሉንም አገሮች፣ ግዛቶችን እና አካባቢዎችን እንደሚጨምር ሆኖ መረዳት አለበት። በአራቱም የ ልብ ምት አሰሳ ጥናት ዙሮች ውስጥ ቢያንስ አንድ የዳሰሳ ጥናት ክፍል ምላሽ በሰጡ ከ84 ሀገሮች፣ ግዛቶች ወይም አካባቢዎች በተሰጡ ምላሾች ላይ በመመስረትም የአዝማሚያ ትንተናዎች ተጠናቅረው ተጠናቀዋል።
translations.reference: While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Although the heart rate assessments have limitations in terms of representativeness and generalizability, the strength of this effort lies in its comprehensiveness and ability to provide rapid information. It should be understood that the term "country" includes all countries, regions, and areas. Trend analyses were completed based on responses from at least one survey component within each of the four heart rate assessment cycles, drawing on responses from 84 countries, regions, or areas."

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **"Aztolaawiinet" and "Yetewakayinet"** are difficult to translate directly. I've interpreted them as "representativeness" and "generalizability" respectively, as they seem to relate to the scope and applicability of the data.
*   **"Yilb Mit Dasseasawo"** is translated as "heart rate assessments."
*   **"Minga"** is translated as "effort" to convey the sense of work or undertaking.
*   **"Hager"** is translated as "country" but with the clarification that it encompasses regions and areas as well.



I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.source: ለሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) የተሰኘውን ድርጅት 50ኛ ዓመት ክብረ በዓል መግለጫ፡ ስዊድን እና የታላቋ ብሪታንያ እና የሰሜን አየርላንድ ዩናይትድ ግዛት የሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) የተሰኘውን ድርጅት 50ኛ ዓመት እና በርካታ ስኬቶቹን የሚያከብሩ የፖሊሲ እና የማስተባበሪያ ኮሚቴ (PCC) አባላትን መንፈስ ለመግዛት፣ ስዊድን እና የታላቋ ብሪታኒያ እና የሰሜን አየርላንድ ዩናይትድ ኪንግደም የሚከተለውን የምስጋና እና የእንኳን አደረሳችሁ ማስታወሻ በ36ኛው የሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) የተሰኘውን ድርጅ ትየፖሊሲ እና የማስተባበሪያ ኮሚቴ (PCC) ስብሰባ ላይ አንብበዋል። የእኛ ትውልድ በሳይንስና በቴክኖሎጂ ከቀደምት ትውልዶች የበለጠ እድገትን አይቷል እናም በፅንስ መከላከል አብዮት መሪነት እና በሥነ ተዋልዶ ጤና፣ በጤና ፈጠራዎች እንዲሁም በሴቶች መብቶች እና በጾታ እኩልነት ላይ የተደረጉ እድገቶችን ተከትሎ፤ በርካታ የእውቀት፣ የቴክኖሎጂ፣ የህክምና እና የማህበራዊ ለውጦችን ምስክር ሆነን አይተናል ። ነገር ግን፣ የግብረስጋ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤና እና መብት (SRHR) መስክ ከፍተኛ ተግዳሮቶች ቀጥለዋል፣ እናም፣ በእርግጠኝነት፣ አንዳንድ እድገቶችም ዛሬ እንደገና ጥያቄ ውስጥ ናቸው። የሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP)-ሰራተኞች፣ አጋሮች እና ደጋፊዎች እስከ ዛሬ ለተመዘገቡት በርካታ ስኬቶች አድንቆታችንን እንገልፃለን እናም በግማሽ ምዕተ-አመት ውስጥ ሙሉ እና እኩል የግብረስጋ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤና እና መብቶችን ለማስከበር የተከናወኑትን ግዙፍ እድገቶች የሚደግፉ ማስረጃዎችን በማዘጋጀቱ እውቅናን እንሰጣለን።
translations.reference: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**Statement Commemorating the 50th Anniversary of the Human Resources Planning (HRP) Organization:**

To capture the spirit of the members of the Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) celebrating the 50th anniversary of the Human Resources Planning (HRP) organization, and its numerous successes, the Kingdom of Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland will read the following message of gratitude and congratulations at the 36th HRP Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) meeting.

Our generation has witnessed more progress than previous generations thanks to science and technology, and we have been witnesses to numerous advancements in knowledge, technology, medicine, and social change following the leadership in the reproductive health revolution, advancements in sexual and reproductive health, health innovations, as well as progress in women's rights and gender equality. However, the field of sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) continues to face significant challenges, and undoubtedly, some of the gains made today are being questioned again.

We express our deep appreciation to the Human Resources Planning (HRP) staff, partners, and supporters for the numerous successes achieved to date, and we recognize the immense progress made in preparing evidence to support the realization of full and equal sexual and reproductive health and rights over the past half-century.

translations.source: ሶስት ዋና ዋና የስኬቶችን ምድቦችንም መዘርዘር እንፈልጋለን። በመጀመሪያ ደረጃ፣ በሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) የተደረገው ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው ጥናትና ምርምር በሕዝብ ጤና ፖሊሲ፣ ደረጃዎች እና የተለያዩ የህዝብ ፍላጎቶችን በማስተናገድ ላይ ተጨባጭ ተጽእኖን አሳድሯል። በሁለተኛ ደረጃ የሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅዱ በተለይም በሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ ህብረት አማካኝነት የጥናትና ምርምር ተቋማትን በአገር ውስጥም ሆነ በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ አጠናክሮል፤ እንዲሁም በርካታ ተመራማሪዎች ከፍተኛ ጥራት ያለው የጾታ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤና ጥናትና ምርምር እንዲቀጥሉ እና እንዲያሳድጉ የሚያስችላቸውን የምርምር ክህሎቶች እንዲያገኙ አስችሏል። በሶስተኛ ደረጃ፤ በችግር፣ በግጭት እና የፆታ እኩልነት ላይ የተቃውሞ አቅም በበዛበት አለም፣ የሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) የሴቶች እና ልጃገረዶች እና የተጋላጭ ህዝቦችን መሰረታዊ መብቶች እንዲከበሩ አመቻችቷል። ሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) በሰው ልጅ ተዋልዶ ላይ ምርምር ለማድረግ በተባበሩት መንግስታት ስርአት ውስጥ ዋናው መሳሪያ ሆኖ በአለም አቀፍ ጾታዊ እና ስነ ተዋልዶ ጤና ላይ ልዩ መሪ ሆኖ ቀጥሏል።
translations.reference: We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"We also want to list three main categories of achievements. Firstly, a high-quality study and research conducted under the Human Resource Plan (HRP) has had a tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and addressing various public needs. Secondly, the Human Resource Plan, particularly through the Human Resource Plan Consortium, has strengthened research institutions both nationally and internationally, and has enabled numerous researchers to acquire research skills that allow them to continue and expand high-quality research on sexual and reproductive health. Thirdly, in a world characterized by conflict, crisis, and gender inequality, the Human Resource Plan (HRP) has facilitated the protection of the fundamental rights of women, girls, and vulnerable populations. The Human Resource Plan (HRP) serves as the primary tool within the United Nations system for research on human reproduction and continues to be a unique leader in global sexual and reproductive health."

translations.source: ሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) እና አጋሮቹ እ.ኤ.አ. በ1994 በተካሄደው የህብረተሰብ እና ልማት ስብሰባ ወቅት የግለሰቦችን በተለይም የሴቶች እና የልጃገረዶች መብት፣ የዘላቂ ልማት መሰረት፣ ለማድረግ የገባውን ቃል ለማየት እንድንቃረብ አስችለዋል። ለሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) ዋና ፀሀፊዎች፣ አጋሮቹ፣ ተባባሪ የገንዘብ ለጋሽ አጋሮች እና የአለም አቀፍ የትብብር ማዕከላት እና ባለሙያዎች በሴቶች፣ ወንዶች፣ ህጻናት፣ ጎረምሶች፣ ወጣቶች እና የ ሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) ለማገልገል ቁርጠኛ የሆነባቸው የተለያዩ ማህበረሰቦች ላይ ለውጥ ለማምጣት ለተደረገው ጥረት መሳካት የበኩላቸውን አስተዋጽኦ ለማበርከት ላደረጉት እገዛ አድናቆት እና ምስጋና መግለጽ እንፈልጋለን። የፖሊሲ እና የማስተባበሪያ ኮሚቴ (PCC) አባላትን በመወከል፣ እ.ኤ.አ የ2030 አጀንዳ ስኬት የሚመሰረትበትን የሰው ሀይል ምደባ እቅድ(HRP) ግኝቶችን ለመደገፍ ከእርስዎ ጋር ያለንን አጋርነት እናረጋግጥ።
translations.reference: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The Human Resources Planning (HRP) and its partners have enabled us to approach the commitment made during the Population and Development Conference held in 1994, to establish the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, as a foundation for sustainable development. We wish to express our admiration and gratitude for the contribution of the main authors of the Human Resources Planning (HRP), its partners, associated financial donor partners, and international collaborative centers and experts, in helping to achieve the efforts to bring about change in women, men, children, adolescents, youth, and various communities dedicated to serving the Human Resources Planning (HRP). On behalf of the Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC), we reaffirm our partnership with you to support the findings of the Human Resources Planning (HRP), which forms the basis for the success of the 2030 Agenda."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝን ቅደመ ዝግጁነት ለማሻሻል አዲስ የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃን ጀመረ አገሮች ለወደፊት ወረርሽኞች በተሻለ ሁኔታ እንዲዘጋጁ ለመርዳት፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) እንደ ጉንፋን(influenza) ወይም ኮሮናቫይረስ ላሉ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታን አምጪ ተዋህስያን ምላሽ ለመስጠት የተቀናጀ ዕቅድ ላይ መመሪያ የሚሰጥ አዲስ የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃን ዛሬ ጀምሯል። አዲሱ የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET)፣ በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ እና በሌሎች የቅርብ ጊዜ የህዝብ ጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች፣ ለጋራ ትምህርት እና ለጋራ እርምጃዎች ተብሎ የተፈጠሩ የቅርብ ጊዜ መሳሪያዎችን እና አቀራረቦችን ያካትታል። በድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃው ውስጥ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በበሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ቡድኖች መካከል ብዙ አቅሞች እና ባህሪያት የተለመዱ በመሆናቸው፣ ሀገራትን የወረርሽኝን እቅድ ለማውጣት ከሰው ሰው የመተላለፊያ ዘዴን የመጠቀም አቀራረብን ይጠቀማል። የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET)፣ በአለም ጤና ጉባኤ ውሳኔዎች ላይ እንደተገለጸው፣ የተቀናጀ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽን ለማበረታታት እና ለማጠናከር የሙያዊ መመሪያ እና ድጋፍ ጥሪን ይመልሳል።
translations.reference: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

**The World Health Organization (WHO) has launched a new first action plan to improve pandemic preparedness.** To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, the World Health Organization (WHO) has launched a new first action plan today, providing guidance on an integrated plan to respond to respiratory disease pathogens such as influenza or coronavirus. The new Preparedness and Response for Emerging Threats Action Plan (PRET) incorporates recent tools and approaches developed for shared learning and action following the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Within the action plan, the World Health Organization uses an approach of utilizing person-to-person transmission methods to help countries develop pandemic plans, as many characteristics and strengths are common among pathogen groups. As stated in World Health Assembly resolutions, the Preparedness and Response for Emerging Threats Action Plan (PRET) responds with professional guidance and support to encourage and strengthen integrated preparedness and response.

translations.source: የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ እና ሌሎች የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች እንዳሳዩት ፣ የተዘጋጁ የቅድመ ዝግጅት እቅዶችን በእጃቸው መያዝ እና ከሌሎች እንደ ግብርና ካሉ መስኮች ጋር የተሻለ ቅንጅት እና ትብብር ማድረግን ጨምሮ፣ ሀገራት ተላላፊ በሽታዎችን ለመከላከል ለተግባር የተዘጋጁ መሆን አለባቸው። "የዝግጅት፣ የመከላከል እና ምላሽ ተግባራት የጤና መስኩ ጠቅላይ ሀላፊነት ብቻ መሆን የለባቸውም" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ተናግረዋል። “የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች በተለያዩ ዘርፎች ላይ ተጽእኖ እንደሚያሳድሩ ሁሉ የእኛም የዝግጁነት እና የምላሽ ጥረታችን በተለያዩ ዘርፎችን፣ የትምህርት ዓይነቶችን እና በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን መካከል መዘርጋት አለባቸው። የማህበረሰብ ተሳትፎ እና ፍትሃዊነት የጥረታችን ማዕከል መሆናቸው፣ በተለይም ለተገለሉ እና ለአደጋ በተጋለጡ ህዝቦች ላይ በጣም አስፈላጊ ነው። የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) በልዩ በሽታዎች ላይ ከማተኮር ይልቅ ከሰው ሰው የመተላለፊያ ዘዴን የመጠቀም አቀራረብን በመከተል፣ የወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት አቀራረብ ዝግመተ ለውጥ ማሳያ ነው።
translations.reference: The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"As the COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown, countries must be prepared to take practical action to prevent infectious diseases, including having prepared contingency plans and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. "Preparedness, prevention, and response activities should not be the sole responsibility of the health sector," said WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Just as health emergencies affect various sectors, our preparedness and response efforts must be spread across various sectors, types of hazards, and disease-causing agents. Community engagement and equity must be at the center of our efforts, especially for marginalized and vulnerable populations. The Pandemic Preparedness and Response Technical Assistance (PRET) demonstrates a paradigm shift in pandemic preparedness approach by following an approach that utilizes the human-to-human transmission mechanism rather than focusing on specific diseases."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እንደአስፈላጊነቱ በተወሰኑ በሽታዎች ላይ መመሪያዎችን ማዘጋጀቱን እና ማሰራጨቱን ይቀጥላል። የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) ይፋዊ ጅማሬ በአባል ሀገራት፣ አጋሮች እና የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ፅህፈት ቤት ከተወከሉ ከ120 በላይ ግለሰቦች ጋር በተካሄደው የመጨረሻ ቀን ስብሰባ ላይ በመካሄድ ላይ ነው፣ የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) ወደፊት የሚወስደውን መንገድ ለመወሰን፣ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን የወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት ዓለም አቀፍ የትግበራ ፍኖተ ካርታ ማዘጋጀትን ጨምሮ ይወሰናል። የ የዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) የመጀመሪያ የጥናት ርዕስ ጉንፋንን፣ ኮሮና ቫይረስን፣እና የመተንፈሻ ሲንሲቲያል ቫይረስን ጨምሮ፣ የመተንፈሻ አካል በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን ላይ ያተኩራል። እየቀጠለ ያለውን የ ኮቪድ-19 ወርሽን እና የአቪያን ጉንፋን(influenza) ያስከትላል ተብሎ የሚጠበቀውን ጉዳት በግምት ውስጥ በማስገባት ፣ ይህ ሞጁል አገሮች የተግባር እና የክምችት አቅም መኖራቸውን ለማረጋገጥ፣ የአተነፋፈስ ወረርሽኝ እቅዳቸውን በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ እንዲገመግሙ፣ እንዲሞክሩ እና እንዲያሻሽሉ ያስችላቸዋል። በዚህ የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ የሚነሱትን፣ እንደ አርቦቫይረስ የመሳሰሉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ቡድን የመለየት ሂደትም በመካሄድ ላይ ነው።
translations.reference: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The World Health Organization continues to prepare and disseminate guidelines on specific diseases as needed. The initial action plan for preparedness and resilience to risk (PRET) was finalized through a concluding meeting held with over 120 individuals representing member states, partners, and the World Health Organization office. During this meeting, the future direction of the initial action plan for preparedness and resilience to risk (PRET) was determined, including the preparation of an international roadmap for pandemic preparedness for respiratory pathogen diseases. The initial study topic of the initial action plan for preparedness and resilience to risk (PRET) focuses on respiratory pathogen diseases, including Nipah virus, Coronavirus, and Respiratory Syncytial Virus. Taking into account the ongoing COVID-19 variants and the anticipated impact of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to significantly assess, test, and improve their respiratory pandemic plans to ensure they have operational and stockpiling capacity. The process of identifying pathogen groups, such as the arbovirus group, is also underway within this initial action plan."

translations.source: ይህንንም ተከትሎ ለጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋ ዝግጁነት፣ ምላሽ እና መቋቋም (HEPR) ዓለም አቀፋዊ መዋቅራዊ ንድፍን ለማጠናከር ተለይተው ከተቀመጡ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ከአሥሩ የውሳኔ እቅድ ጉዳዮች ይቀርባሉ። የየዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) አዲስ ወረርሽኞችን ለመከላከል አዲስ ዘመንን ያመጣል እናም የሁሉንም አባል ሀገራት የጤና ድንገተኛ ዝግጁነት፣ የመከላከል እና የምላሽ አቅሞችን እና ብቃቶችን ለመደገፍ የሚያደርገውን የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ተግባራት ዝግመተ ለውጥን ይወክላል። ይህ ተነሳሽነት በአሁኑ ጊዜ በአለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት እየተደራደረ ያለውን የወረርሽኝ ድንጋጌዎችን ራዕዮች እና አላማዎችን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ ያስችላል። በስብሰባ ተሳታፊዎች መካከል የሚገኙ ግብአቶችን እና ውይይቶችን ተከትሎ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ባለድርሻ አካላትን የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተዋህስያን ወረርሺኝ መከላከልን ለማጎልበት ወደዚህ አለም አቀፍ ጥሪ አቅርቧል። የድርጊት ጥሪ
translations.reference: This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Following this, the ten priority areas of the decision-making agenda are identified to strengthen the global structural framework for Health Emergency Preparedness, Response, and Resilience (HEPR). The new Pandemic Prevention and Response Tool (PRET) for risk preparedness and resilience brings a new era to prevent new pandemics and represents a paradigm shift in the World Health Organization's (WHO) core functions to support all member states' health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. This initiative will enable the WHO member states to implement the vision and objectives of the pandemic regulations currently being negotiated. Following inputs and discussions among the meeting participants, the WHO has made this global call to action to enhance the prevention of respiratory pathogen outbreaks among stakeholders. Call to Action."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት፣ አባል ሀገራት እና አጋሮች በጋራ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ተግባራት በመለየት ተግባራዊ በማድረግ ሀገራዊ፣ ክልላዊ እና አለም አቀፋዊ ወረርሽኞች እና ታዳጊ ተላላፊ በሽታ ስጋቶችን ዝግጁነት ለማጠናከር እየሰሩ ነው። በወረርሽኝ መከላከል እና ምላሽ ላይ በጣም የተለመደ የሆነውን የሽብር እና የቸልተኝነት ዑደት ለማስወገድ የማህበረሰቡ እና የሁሉም መስኮች ሚና እና የተቀናጀ እና ቀጣይነት ያለው መነሳሳት እንደሚያስፈልግ እንገነዘባለን። ከቀደምት አለምአቀፋዊ ወረርሽኞች እና ክልላዊ ወረርሽኞች በተለይም በመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ምክንያት የተወሰዱ ትምህርቶችን እናስታውሳለን። ወረርሽኞች መከላከል እና የጤና እንክብካቤ ለሁሉም ሰዎች እንዳይደርስ በመገኘት፣ እነዚህ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን የበሽታ እና የሞት አደጋን የማስከተል፣ የጤና ስርአቶችን የማጨናነቅ፣ የአለም ኢኮኖሚን የማዛባት እና ኢፍትሃዊነትን የሚያባብሱ ትልቅ አለም አቀፍ ስጋትን የማስከተል አቅም አላቸው እና ለወደፊትም ይኖራቸዋል። ይህ የተግባር ጥሪ በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለወረርሽኞች እና ለሚከሰቱ ስጋቶች ዝግጁነትን ለማፋጠን ነው።
translations.reference: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The World Health Organization, member states, and partners are working to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats by identifying and implementing priority actions. We recognize the need for community engagement and the role of all sectors, as well as integrated and sustained action to eliminate the recurring cycle of panic and neglect that is so common in pandemic prevention and response. We remember the lessons learned from past global and regional pandemics, particularly those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have the potential to cause significant global risks, leading to disease and death, straining health systems, disrupting the global economy, and exacerbating inequities, and this potential remains for the future. This call to action aims to accelerate global preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats."

translations.source: ውጤታማ ዝግጁነት በጠንካራ እቅድ እና በተቀናጀ ተግባር ላይ የተመሰረተ ነው። በሚከተሉት ተግባራት ላይ የምናሳየው የጋራ ቁርጠኝነት እ.ኤ.አ በታህሳስ 2025 መሻሻልን በክትትል ማዕቀፉ (ለአደጋ ስጋት ዝግጁነት እና ተቋቋሚነት (PRET) እንደተገለጸው እንድናይ ያስችላል፡- ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ተግባራት ተከዋኝነትን የሚያረጋግጡ እና ካለፉት ክስተቶች የተማሩትን ያገናዘቡ የዘመኑ የዝግጁነት እቅዶችን ማውጣት ። በመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ሊያስከትሉ የሚችሉትን አደጋ በመገንዘብ፣ በየዝግጁነት እና የተቋቋሚነት ለአደጋ ስጋት የድርጊት የመጀመሪያ እርምጃ (PRET) ሞጁል #1 ውስጥ በተገለጹት ጭብጦች ላይ በመመርኮዝ የመተንፈሻ አካልን በሽታ አምጪ ወረርሽኝ ለመከላከል ማቀድ፡ የመተንፈሻ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋስያንን ወረርሽኝን ለመከላከል ማቀድ ቅድሚያ የሚሰጠው ጉዳይ ነው።
translations.reference: Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Effective preparedness is based on a strong plan and coordinated action. The common commitment we demonstrate in the following activities will allow us to see improvement by December 2025, as indicated by the monitoring framework (for Disaster Risk Preparedness and Resilience (PRET)). Priority activities include developing current preparedness plans that ensure resilience and incorporate lessons learned from past events. Recognizing that respiratory pathogens can cause hazards, planning to prevent a respiratory pathogen pandemic, focusing on the topics outlined in Action Module #1 of the Disaster Risk Preparedness and Resilience (PRET) framework, is a priority. Planning to prevent a pandemic of respiratory pathogens is a top priority."

**Key notes on the translation:**

*   I've tried to maintain the formal tone of the original Amharic.
*   I've kept the acronym "PRET" as it is likely a specific term within the context of disaster preparedness.
*   I've aimed for clarity and accuracy in conveying the meaning.

translations.source: ስልታዊ ቅንጅት እና ትብብር በማድረግ በወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት እቅድ ውስጥ በባለድርሻ አካላት መካከል ያለውን ግንኙነት ማሳደግ። ይህም ፍትሃዊ ስርዓቶችን መገንባትን; የጋራ ልምምድ ማካሄድ; እና ስለ ጥሩ ልምዶች፣ ተግዳሮቶች እና እድሎች መረጃን መለዋወጥን ይጨምራል። ያለፉትን በአለምአቀፋዊ ወረርሽኞች እና ክልላዊ ወረርሽኞች የታዩ ክፍተቶችን በመቅረፍ ቀጣይነት ያለው ኢንቨስትመንቶችን፣ የገንዘብ ድጋፍን እና የወረርሽኙን ቅድመ ዝግጅት መከታተል። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምላሽ በፖለቲካ አቋም ቁርጠኝነት፣ በማህበረሰብ ተሳትፎ እና በገንዘብ ምን ሊገኝ እንደሚችል አሳይቷል።
translations.reference: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"By fostering strategic coordination and collaboration, strengthening the linkages between stakeholders within pandemic preparedness plans. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint practices; and exchanging information on best practices, challenges, and opportunities. Addressing gaps observed in past global and regional pandemics through continued investments, financial support, and monitoring of pandemic preparedness. The COVID-19 response has demonstrated the potential of political commitment, community engagement, and financial resources."

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **"سيلاتيقي قنجميت" (Silatīgī qənǧimt):** Translated as "strategic coordination" to capture the meaning of a planned and collaborative effort.
*   **"ባለድርሻ አካላት" (bāldirša akālāt):** Translated as "stakeholders" which is a common term in preparedness planning.
*   **"ፍትሃዊ ስርዓቶችን" (fithawi sirsit):** Translated as "equitable systems" to convey the idea of fairness and inclusivity.
*   **"የጋራ ልምምድ" (yegara limmimd):** Translated as "joint practices" to indicate collaborative exercises or drills.
*   **"የወረርሽኙን ቅድመ ዝግጅት" (yewerrishīny qidim ziggijit):** Translated as "pandemic preparedness" to maintain consistency.



I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.source: የዚህ ሥራ ዋና ነገር ለቀጣዩ ወረርሽኝ በጋራ ለመዘጋጀት እኩልነትን ማረጋገጥ ነው። በዚህ የድርጊት ጥሪ ውስጥ የተገለፀውን እድገት ለማሳየት የህብረተሰቡ ሁሉ እርምጃ ያስፈልጋል። ትግበራው ስለዚህም፣ የማህበረሰቦችን የመቋቋም አቅም ማጠናከር አለበት; በመደበኛ ስርዓቶች ላይ መግባት, መቆየት እና መገንባት አለበት; እና ለአደጋ ጊዜ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ ሰፊ አቅሞችን መጠቀም አለበት። ይህ የድርጊት ጥሪ የተለቀቀው በጄኔቫ፣ ስዊዘርላንድ ውስጥ እ.ኤ.አ በ24-26 ሚያዝያ 2023 በተካሄደው አለምአቀፋዊ የሆነ፣ ለወደፊት ለሚመጡት የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ አምጪ ወረርሽኞች ዝግጁነት እና ተቋቋሚነት (PRET) ላይ በተደረገ ስብሰባ ላይ ነው።
translations.reference: At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The main focus of this work is to ensure equity in preparing for the next pandemic. The action required to demonstrate the progress outlined in this call to action necessitates the involvement of the entire community. Therefore, the strategy must strengthen the resilience of communities; integrate, remain, and build upon existing systems; and utilize broad approaches to disaster preparedness and response. This call to action was released following a global conference on Pandemic Preparedness and Resilience (PRET) held in Geneva, Switzerland, from May 24-26, 2023."

translations.source: ወባን ለመዋጋት የሚመከሩ መሳሪያዎችን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አሳስቧል የዓለም የወባ ቀንን በማስመልከት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) ከወባ በሽታ ህይወትን ለመታደግ አዳዲስ እና ነባር እርምጃዎችን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ ጥሪ አቅርቧል። በጋና፣ኬንያ እና ማላዊ ለወባ ለከፍተኛ ለበሽታ እና ለሞት የተጋለጡ ወደ 1.5 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ ህጻናት አሁን በአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) አስተባባሪነት የመጀመሪያውን የወባ ክትባት RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S) አግኝተዋል ። እ.ኤ.አ በ 2019 የተጀመረው የመጀመሪያው የወባ ክትባት ዙር ፣ በጣም ተጋላጭ ለሆኑ ሰዎች የወባ መከላከልን ተደራሽነት ፍትሃዊነት በማሳደግ ህይወትን እየታደጉ ይገኛሉ። በሰፊው ተግባራዊ ከሆነም፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ ክትባቶቹ በየዓመቱ በአሥር ሺዎች የሚቆጠሩ የሕፃናትን ሕይወት ሊታደጉ እንደሚችሉ ይገምታል።
translations.reference: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

To combat malaria, the World Health Organization (WHO) has called for the implementation of recommended tools, marking World Malaria Day. In commemoration of World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) has called for the implementation of new and existing measures to save lives from malaria. Approximately 1.5 million children in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi, who are at high risk of malaria disease and death, have now received the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), under the coordination of the World Health Organization (WHO). The first round of the malaria vaccine, which began in 2019, is saving lives by making malaria prevention accessible to the most vulnerable populations. If implemented widely, the World Health Organization estimates that malaria vaccines could save tens of thousands of children's lives each year.

translations.source: "ወባን ለማጥፋት የሚረዱ መሳሪያዎች፣ የአስተላላፊ ትንኝ ቁጥጥር፣ የመከላከያ መድሃኒቶች፣ ምርመራ እና ህክምናን ያካተቱ የክትትል ክምችቶች አሉን፣" ሲሉ ዶክተር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ተናግረዋል። “እነዚህ፣ በየአመቱ በአስር ሺዎች የሚቆጠሩ ህጻናትን ሊያድኑ ከሚችሉ፣ ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና ውጤታማ ክትባት ጋር ተቀላቅለው ይገኛሉ። በዘላቂ ኢንቨስትመንት እና በተጠናከረ ጥረት ለአደጋ ለተጋለጡትን በማዳረስ፣ በብዙ አገሮች አሁን ወባን ለማጥፋት ተደርሷል። """ የዓለም የወባ ቀን 2023 " አሁን ዜሮ ወባን የማዳረስ ጊዜ: መዋዕለ ንዋይ ማፍሰስ፣ አዲስ ግኝት መፍጠር፣ መተግበር( Time to deliver zero malaria:invest, inovate, implement" በሚል መሪ ቃል እየተከበረ ነው። በዚህ ጭብጥ ውስጥ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ በሽታን ለመከላከል፣ ለመመርመር እና ለማከም፣ በተለይ በወባ ተጋላጭ በሆኑ ህብረተሰብ ዘንድ የበለጠ ውጤታማ በሆነ መንገድ እንዲተገበር የሚያስችሉ መሳሪያዎች እና ስልቶች እንዲመጡ እያነሳሳ ይገኛል።
translations.reference: “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"We have tools to eliminate malaria, including vector control, preventative medicines, surveillance stockpiles that include testing and treatment," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. "These, when combined with safe and effective vaccines, have the potential to save tens of thousands of children every year. Through sustained investment and intensified efforts, malaria elimination has been achieved in many countries." The World Malaria Day 2023 is being celebrated under the theme "Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement." Within this framework, the World Health Organization is working to develop tools and strategies that can be implemented more effectively to prevent, detect, and treat malaria, particularly within malaria-vulnerable communities.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ በታህሳስ 2022 በታተመው የመጨረሻው የአለም የወባ ሪዘገባ መሰረት፣ እ.ኤ.አ በ2021 ወደ 247 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ አዳዲስ የወባ በሽታ የተያዙ ሰዎች እንዳሉ ይገመታል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የአፍሪካ ክልል የበሽታውን ከባድ ሸክም መሸከሙን ቀጥሏል -እ.ኤ.አ በ 2021 ከጠቅላላው በወባ በሽታ የተያዙ ሰዎች 95 በመቶ (234 ሚሊዮን) እና ከጠቅላላው ሞት 96 በመቶውን (593 000) ይሸፍናል ። በአፍሪካ ክልል 80 በመቶ የሚጠጋው የወባ ሞት ከአምስት አመት በታች የሆኑ ህጻናት ናቸው። አዳዲስ ስልቶች እና መሳሪያዎች አገሮች ለአብዛኛዎቹ ተጋላጭ ለሆኑ ህብረተሰቦች የወባ መከላከል አገልግሎት ተደራሽነትን በማስፋት ረገድ መጠነኛ መሻሻል አሳይተዋል።
translations.reference: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Based on the final global malaria report published in December 2022, it is estimated that around 247 million new cases of malaria occurred in 2021. The World Health Organization's (WHO) African Region continues to bear the brunt of the disease – accounting for 95 percent (234 million) of all malaria cases and 96 percent (593,000) of all deaths in 2021. In the African Region, nearly 80 percent of malaria deaths are among children under five years of age. New strategies and tools have shown modest improvements in expanding access to malaria prevention services for most vulnerable populations in countries.

translations.source: ምንም እንኳን መጠነኛ መሻሻል ቢኖረውም፣ ብዙ ለወባ የተጋለጡ ሰዎች አሁንም በሽታውን ለመከላከል፣ ለመለየት እና ለማከም የሚያስችል አገልግሎት አያገኙም። የወባ መከላከል አገልግሎት ተደራሽነትን በማስፋፋት ላይ ያሉ ተግዳሮቶች በተለይም ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ ሀገራት በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ፣በሰብአዊ ቀውሶች ፣ በተገደበ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ፣ በደካማ የክትትል ስርዓቶች እና የወባ መከላከያ መሳሪያዎች ውጤታማነት ላይ ተዕጽኖ አድርጓል። እነዚህን ስጋቶች ለመቅረፍ እና ሀገራት የበለጠ የሚቋቋሙ የወባ መከላከል መርሀግብሮችን በመገንባት ለመደገፍ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አዲስ ስትራቴጂዎችን እና ማዕቀፎችን በቅርቡ አሳትሟል፣ ከእነዚህም መካከል፡- በአፍሪካ ውስጥ የፀረ ወባ መድሐኒት አለመቀበልን የሚያካትት አዲስ ስልት; በከተማ አካባቢ የአኖፌሌስ ስቴፈንሲ ስርጭትን ለማስቆም አዲስ ተነሳሽነት;
translations.reference: Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Although there has been moderate improvement, many people at risk of malaria still do not receive the services needed to prevent, detect, and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria prevention services have been exacerbated, particularly in sub-Saharan African countries, by the COVID-19 pandemic, humanitarian crises, limited financial support, weak surveillance systems, and the effectiveness of malaria control tools. To address these risks and support countries in building more resilient malaria control programs, the World Health Organization has recently published new strategies and benchmarks, including: a new approach addressing the lack of antimalarial medicines in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of *Anopheles stephensi* in urban areas;"

translations.source: በከተማዎች፣ ወባ መከላከል ላይ የከተማ መሪዎችን ለመምራት በ አለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) እና በ ዩኤን-ሀቢታት(UN-Habitat) በጋራ የተዘጋጀ አዲስ ማዕቀፍ። ሀገራት የወባ ክትትል ስርዓታቸውን ለመገምገም እና የኢንቨስትመንት ቦታዎችን ለመለየት የሚረዳ አዲስ መሳሪያ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ መከላከል ምክሮቹን ግልጽነት፣ ተለዋዋጭነት እና ተደራሽነትን ጨምሯል። የተቀናጀው የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ መመሪያዎች አሁን በሁለት ዲጂታል መድረኮች ይገኛሉ፡ 'MAGICapp' እና "Malaria Toolkit" መተግበሪያ። የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ከፍተኛ ተጽእኖ ያለው ውጤት እንዲያመጡ ሀገራት አቅጣጫዎቻቸውን ከአካባቢያቸው ሁኔታ ጋር በማጣጣም እንዲያስተካክሉ ይመክራል።
translations.reference: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"In cities, a new framework prepared jointly by the World Health Organization (WHO) and UN-Habitat to guide city leaders in malaria prevention. The World Health Organization has enhanced the clarity, adaptability, and accessibility of its malaria prevention advice, with a new tool to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify investment opportunities. The integrated WHO malaria guidance is now available on two digital platforms: the 'MAGICapp' and the "Malaria Toolkit" application. The WHO advises countries to adjust their strategies to align with their local context to ensure impactful results."

translations.source: ለአዳዲስ የግኝት ተስፋዎች እ.ኤ.አ የ2030 የአለም የወባ ግቦችን ለማሳካት፣ አዳዲስ የወባ ክትባቶችን እና የቀጣይ ትውልድ መሳሪያዎችን በማዘጋጀት እና በማሰማራት ላይ የሚደረግ ቀጣይ ኢንቨስትመንቶች ቁልፍ ሚና ይሆናሉ። ሁለተኛው የወባ ክትባት R21/Matrix-M (R21) ክትባት ከፀደቀ፣ በአቅርቦትና በፍላጎት መካከል ያለውን ከፍተኛ ልዩነት ለመዝጋት እና የህፃናትን ህመም እና የወባ ሞትን የበለጠ ለመቀነስ ያስችላል። በመካሄድ ላይ ያለው ከR21 ምዕራፍ 3 ሙከራ ተጨማሪ ቁልፍ የደህንነት እና የውጤታማነት መረጃ ከተገኘ እና ለአለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) ከቀረበ በኋላ የ R21 የወባ ክትባት የተሟላ እና ቀልጣፋ የባለሙያዎችን ግምገማ መቀጠል ለ ለአለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) ቅድሚያ የሚሰጠው ጉዳይ ነው። የ RTS,S የወባ ክትባት በአፍሪካ ውስጥ ባሉ ተጨማሪ አገሮች ውስጥ የደረጃ በደረጃ መግቢያ እ.ኤ.አ በ 2024 መጀመሪያ ላይ ይጀምራል ተብሎ ይጠበቃል።
translations.reference: Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"To achieve the global malaria targets for 2030, new funding commitments are crucial. Continued investments in developing and deploying new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will play a key role. If the second malaria vaccine, R21/Matrix-M (R21), is approved, it will help close the significant supply-demand gap and further reduce malaria illness and death in children. The ongoing Phase 3 trial of R21 will continue to be a priority for the World Health Organization (WHO) after additional key safety and efficacy data is obtained and submitted. The phased rollout of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional African countries is expected to begin in early 2024."

translations.source: በአስተላላፊ ትንኝ መቆጣጠሪያ ቦታ ውስጥ፣ በ R & D ቧንቧ ውስጥ 28 አዳዲስ ምርቶች አሉ። በመሞከር ላይ ካሉት መንገዶች ለምሳሌ በጸረነፍሳት የታከሙ አዳዲስ አይነት መረቦች፣ የወባ ትንኞችን ለመሳብ የታለሙ ማጥመጃዎች፣ የግርግዳ ላይ መከላከያዎች፣ ገዳይ የቤት ማባበያዎች (የእንጨት ቱቦዎች) እና የወባ ትንኞች የዘረመል ምህንድስና ያካትታሉ። ተመራማሪዎች በከፊል የአርቴሚሲኒን መቋቋም መከሰትን እና መስፋፋትን ምክንያት አርገው፣ በፀረ-ወባ መድሐኒቶች መስክ ከኤሲቲ-ውጭ (non-ACT) የሆኑ ሕክምናዎች እድገት ቅድሚያ እየሰጡ ነው። "እንደ ""ሶስት ድርብ ኤሲቲዎች (triple ACTs)" ያሉ የቀጣይ ትውልድ መድኃኒቶችን በማዘጋጀት የአርቴሚሲን እና የሁለት አጋር መድኃኒቶች ጥምረት በመጠቀም የመድኃኒት የመቋቋም አደጋን ለመቀነስ በመካሄድ ላይ ነው። ለአርታዒው ማስታወሻ፡ ስለ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወባ ቀን ዘመቻ ለበለጠ መረጃ፡ ይጎብኙ፡ https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Amharic document:

Within the vector-borne disease control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. These include products under development such as insect-repellent nets, lures designed to attract mosquitoes, ridge protectors, household insecticides (wooden tubes), and mosquito genetic engineering. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT (artemisinin-based combination therapies) treatments in the anti-malarial field, having investigated the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. There is ongoing work to reduce the risk of drug resistance by preparing next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," using artemisinin and combinations of two partner drugs. For the editor's note: For more information about the World Health Organization's World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

translations.source: የ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ፕሮግራም ወደ ቀጣዩ የእድገት ደረጃ ተሸጋግሯል በአፍሪገን፣ ደቡብ አፍሪካ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ማዕከል ምርቃት። አጋሮች በኬፕ ታውን ስለ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባት ልማት እና የእውቀት መጋራት ቀጣዩን ምዕራፍ ለመወያየት ተሰብስበዋል። የ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ፕሮግራም ላይ የሚሰሩ ከ 200 በላይ አለምአቀፍ ተሳታፊዎች፣ በዚህ ሳምንት በኬፕ ታውን ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ ፊት ለፊት ተገናኝተዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ፣ የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስትር እና የንግድ እና ኢንዱስትሪ ሚኒስትር ኢብራሂም ፓቴል፣ የደቡብ አፍሪካ ፓቴል ዶ/ር ጆ ፋህላ ፤ ከገንዘብ ድጋፍ ሰጪ ሀገራት ከፍተኛ ባለስልጣናት ጋር በመቀላቀል የመሩት፣ ይህ ልዩ ስብሰባ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የመድኃኒት ፓተንት ፑል (MPP) እ.ኤ.አ በሰኔ 2021 ከጀመሩበት ጊዜ ጀምሮ ያለውን ሂደት ገምግሟል።
translations.reference: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"The mRNA Technology Transfer Program has moved to the next stage of growth within the African Union, with the African mRNA Technology Center for Manufacturing in South Africa. Partners have gathered in Cape Town to discuss the next chapter of mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. More than 200 international participants involved in the mRNA Technology Transfer Program have met in person for the first time this week in Cape Town. World Health Organization Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Health Minister and Trade and Industry Minister Ibrahim Patel, South Africa's Dr. Joe Phaahla, and senior officials from donor countries, led this unique gathering, which assessed the progress made since the World Health Organization and the Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) began their work in June 2021."

translations.source: "ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ላላቸው ሀገራት የክትባት እና የሌሎች ህይወት አድን የጤና ምርቶችን ፍትሃዊ ተደራሽነት ለመስጠት ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ዘላቂ ሞዴል ለመደገፍ ከአጋሮቻችን ጋር በኬፕ ታውን በመገኘቴ ደስተኛ ነኝ" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶክተር ቴድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ገልፀዋል ። "በዚህ ፕሮጀክት ውስጥ በተሳተፉት ሁሉ ስኬት በጣም ኮርቻለሁ; ሁለት ዓመት ባልሞላ ጊዜ ውስጥ ተባብረን ስንሠራ በጋራ እንደሚሳካልንም አሳይተናል። የስብሰባው ተሳታፊዎች በፕሮግራሙ ውስጥ ከ 15 አገሮች የተውጣጡ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ አጋሮችን፣ ዋና ባለሙያዎችን፣ ኢንዱስትሪዎችን፣ የሲቪል ማህበረሰብ ተወካዮች እና የገንዘብ ሰጪዎችን ያካትታሉ። በአምስት ቀናቱ ስብሰባ ውስጥ፣ ተሳታፊዎች ያሳዩትን እድገት ያጋራሉ እና ለፕሮግራሙ ዘላቂነት እንደ የአእምሮአዊ ንብረት ጉዳዮች እና የቁጥጥር ጉዳዮች እንዲሁም ስለ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂዎች ሳይንስ እና በሌሎች የበሽታ አካባቢዎች ከኤል.ኤም.አይ.ሲዎች(LMICs) ጋር ተዛማጅነት ያላቸውን እንደ ኤች አይ ቪ እና ቲዩበርክሎዝስ ቁልፍ መተግበሪያዎች ላይ ይወያያሉ ። የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ፣ በጤና ምርቶች በተለይም በክትባቶች ተደራሽነት ላይ ከፍተኛ ኢፍትሃዊነት እንዳለ አመልክቷል።
translations.reference: “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"I am delighted to be in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to ensure equitable access to vaccines and other life-saving health products for low- and middle-income countries," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. "I am very proud of the success of everyone involved in this project; in less than two years, we have shown that we can succeed together by working collaboratively. The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners, key experts, industry representatives, civil society representatives, and financiers from 15 countries participating in the program. During the five-day meeting, participants will share the progress they have made and discuss sustainability aspects of the program, such as intellectual property issues and regulatory matters, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas, such as HIV and tuberculosis. He noted that the COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the significant inequity in access to health products, particularly vaccines."

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ ከመጋቢት 2023 ጀምሮ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ኮቪድ-19ን የአለም አቀፍ ስጋት የህዝብ ጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋ (PHEIC) ካወጀ ከሶስት አመታት በኋላ፣ 69.7 በመቶ የሚሆነው የአለም ህዝብ ቢያንስ አንድ የ ኮቪድ-19 ክትባት ወስደዋል። በተለይም ይህ መጠን አሁንም ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች (LICs) ከ 30 በመቶ በታች እንደሆነ ነው። ይህ ፕሮግራም፣ ከኤል.ኤም.አይ.ሲዎች(LMICs) ዘላቂ የማምረት አቅም ስርጭትን በማሳደግ፣ ክልላዊ እና ክልላዊ ትብብሮችን በማጎልበት እና በተበጀ እና ሁሉን አቀፍ ስልጠና እና የባለሙያዎች ድጋፍ የሀገር ውስጥ የሰው ሃይል በማዳበር እና በማብቃት ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባቶችን ፍትሃዊ ተጠቃሚ ለማድረግ የበኩሉን አስተዋፅኦ ማድረግ ነው። በ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ማስተላለፊያ ሞዴል ውስጥ ልዩ የሆነው ቴክኖሎጂዎችን ለብዙ ተቀባዮች ማጋራት የሚፈቅደው በአገር ውስጥ እና በክልላዊ ምርት እርዳታ የሚያስፈልጋቸውን በፍጥነት እንዲደርሱ የሚያስችል ሁለገብ ሂደት ነው። የደቡብ አፍሪካ የጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስትር ዶ/ር ፋህላ “ዛሬ ታሪካዊ ወቅት ነው፤ ይህ መርሃ ግብር ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸዎን ሃገራት በአለምአቀፍ የትብብር ትስስር ለማጎልበት ያለመ ነው” ብለዋል።
translations.reference: As of March 2023, more than three years after WHO declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), 69.7% of the global population had received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. Notably, this proportion still remains below 30% in low-income countries (LICs) . This Programme aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the distribution of sustainable manufacturing capacity across LMICs, enhancing regional and inter-regional collaborations, and developing and empowering a local workforce through tailored and inclusive training and expert support. What is unique in the mRNA Technology Transfer model is the multilateral process that allows sharing of technologies to multiple recipients so that through local and regional production those in need can be reached rapidly. Dr Phaahla, Minister of Health South Africa, said: “What we see here today, is a moment in history, a Programme that is aimed at empowering LMICs through a global collaborative network.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"Starting from March 2023, after three years since the World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), at least one COVID-19 vaccine has been administered to 69.7 percent of the world's population. Notably, this rate is still below 30 percent in low-income countries (LICs). This program aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the sustainable manufacturing capacity of LMICs, strengthening regional and continental partnerships, and developing and empowering a domestic workforce through tailored and comprehensive training and expert support. What is unique about the mRNA technology transfer model is its versatile process that allows for the sharing of technologies with multiple recipients, enabling them to quickly access domestically and regionally produced vaccines. South Africa's Minister of Health, Dr. Phaahla, stated, "Today is a historic moment; this program is aimed at strengthening international cooperation for low- and middle-income countries."

translations.source: በአንፃራዊ ሁኔታ በአጭር ጊዜ ውስጥ የተገኘውን እድገት በማየቴ በጣም ደስተኛ ነኝ እና - እንደ ደቡብ አፍሪካ ያሉ ጠንካራ የባዮማኑፋክቸሪንግ አቅም ያላቸው እና በጋራ ለመስራት ፣ አንዱ ከሌላው ለመማር እና ለማካፈል ፈቃደኛ የሆኑ፣ ከተለያዩ ሀገራት የሚደረገውን ድጋፍ በደስታ ተቀብያለሁ።" የከፍተኛ ትምህርት እና ሳይንስ ሚኒስትር ብሌድ ንዚማንዴ ፣ "እኛ የያዝነው ከኮቪድ-19 ባለፈ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ማስተላለፊያ ማዕከል ራዕይ ነው። የምንገነባው ችሎታዎች ሌሎች ወደፊት ወረርሽኞችን ለመቋቋም የሚያስችል ኃይልን ለመስጠት ያለመ ሲሆን፣ ክትባታቸውም በተመሳሳይ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA)ን የቴክኖሎጂ መድረክ ሊጠቀም ይችላል። በአካባቢያችን እየተስፋፉ ያሉ እንደ ወባ፣ ሳንባ ነቀርሳ፣ ኤችአይቪ እና ኤድስ ባሉ በሽታዎች ላይም ዓይኑን ያሳረፈ ራዕይ ነው። ይህ አጋጣሚ ዶ/ር ቴድሮስ እና ዶ/ር ማትሺዲሶ ሞኢቲ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የአፍሪካ ክልላዊ ዳይሬክተር ቻርለስ ጎሬ የኤምፒፒ ዋና ዳይሬክተር እና በአፍሪገን ሪባን የመቁረጥ ሥነ ሥርዓት ላይ የተሳተፉት የተከበሩ ሚኒስትሮች በተገኙበት አፍሪገን የሚገኘውን ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሃብ ተቋም በኬፕ ታውን መከፈቱ ለፕሮግራሙ ጠቃሚ ምዕራፍ ነው፣ ።
translations.reference: I am thrilled to see the progress made in such a relatively short time and welcome the support from so many different countries - countries like South Africa that have a strong vibrant biomanufacturing capacity and that are willing to work together, learn from and share with each other.” Minister Blade Nzimande of Higher Education and Science, said: “Ours is the vision of the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub beyond just COVID-19. The capabilities we are building are looking to empower us to deal with other future pandemics whose vaccines could use the same mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that has set its eyes on diseases that are prevalent in our environments such as Malaria, Tuberculosis and HIV and AIDS.” This occasion also marks an important milestone for the Programme with the inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen in Cape Town in the presence of Dr. Tedros and Dr. Matshidiso Moeti WHO Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore Executive Director of MPP and the Honorable Ministers who took part in a ribbon cutting ceremony at Afrigen.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

"I am very pleased to see the progress achieved in a relatively short time, and I welcome the support from various countries with strong biomanufacturing capabilities like South Africa, willing to work together, learn from each other, and share. " – said the Minister of Higher Education and Science, Blade Nzimande. "What we are holding is a vision for an mRNA technology transfer hub, beyond COVID-19. What we are building is intended to empower others to withstand future pandemics, and their vaccination can also utilize the mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that also addresses diseases that are increasingly prevalent in our region, such as malaria, smallpox, HIV and AIDS. This opportunity is a crucial chapter for the mRNA technology hub, AfriGen, located in Cape Town, which was inaugurated in the presence of Dr. Tedros, Dr. Matshidiso Moeti, Director-General of the World Health Organization, and Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore, CEO of MPP, and honorable ministers who participated in the AfriGen ribbon-cutting ceremony."

translations.source: የአፍሪገን ዋና ዳይሬክተር ፕሮፌሰር ፔትሮ ቴርባንቼ እንዳሉት፡ “መላው የአፍሪጀን ቡድን ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጅ መድረክ ተጠናቆ ወደዚህ ወሳኝ ምዕራፍ በመድረሳቸው በጣም ተደስተዋል። ይህ መድረክ ከጫፍ እስከ ጫፍ ባለው ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባት ልማት እና የማምረቻ ተቋም ውስጥ የሚገኝ ሲሆን ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ሀብ ኮቪድ 19 ክትባት እጩ አፍሪቫክ 2121 በአሁኑ ጊዜ በማሳደግ ደረጃ ላይ ይገኛል። ባለፉት 18 ወራት ውስጥ አፍሪገን በዚህ ፕሮግራም በነቃ የአጋሮች እና የአማካሪዎች ኔትወርክ ድጋፍ የማይታመን ለውጥ አድርጓል። የ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ክትባቶችን ለማምረት በ ከኤል.ኤም.አይ.ሲዎች(LMICs) ውስጥ ዘላቂ አቅም የመገንባት ዓላማን በማገልገል ከፍተኛ የጥራት ደረጃዎችን ለማሟላት አቅማችንን እና ብቃታቸወንን አሳድገናል። አፍሪገን የኮቪድ-19 ክትባት ማምረቻ ሂደትን በላብራቶሪ ደረጃ በተሳካ ሁኔታ አቋቁሞ በአሁኑ ጊዜ ያን ሂደት በደረጃ I/2 ክሊኒካዊ ሙከራዎች ወደ ጂኤምፒ(GMP) ደረጃ ለመጠቀም የክትባት ስብስቦችን ለማምረት ተስማሚ ደረጃ ላይ ይገኛል።
translations.reference: Professor Petro Terblanche, Executive Director of Afrigen said: “The entire Afrigen team are thrilled to reach this important milestone with the completion of the mRNA technology platform. This platform is housed within the end-to-end mRNA vaccine development and production facility where the mRNA Hub Covid 19 vaccine candidate AfriVac 2121 is currently in scale-up phase. Over the last 18 months, Afrigen has undergone an incredible transformation with the support of a network of partners and mentors enabled by this Programme. We have grown our capability and capacity to meet the highest quality standards of mRNA vaccine development, serving the objective to build sustainable capacity in LMICs to produce mRNA vaccines”. Afrigen has successfully established a COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at a laboratory scale and is currently scaling up that process to a level suitable for manufacturing vaccine batches to be used in Phase I/ II clinical trials to GMP standards.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Amharic document into English:

According to the CEO of Africanregen, Professor Petro Terbanche, "The entire Africanregen team is delighted to have completed the mRNA technology platform and reached this crucial stage. This platform encompasses the entire mRNA vaccine development and manufacturing facility, and mRNA has a COVID-19 vaccine candidate, AfriVac 2121, currently in scale-up. Over the past 18 months, Africanregen has made an incredible transformation through this program, thanks to the support of a network of partners and mentors. We have strengthened our capabilities and capacity to meet the highest quality standards, aiming to build sustainable capacity for mRNA vaccine production within LMICs (Low and Middle-Income Countries). Africanregen has successfully established the COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at the laboratory level and is currently ready to transition that process to GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) standards for Phase I/2 clinical trials to produce vaccine batches."

translations.source: በትይዩ ሂደት፣ አፍሪገን የስልጠና እና የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግርን ለኔትወርክ አጋሮች ማከናወኑን ይቀጥላል። የገንዘብ አቅራቢዎቹ ፕሮግራሙን በጠቅላላ በ 117 ሚሊዮን ዩኤስ ዶላር በመደገፍ ወሳኝ ሚና ይጫወታሉ እናም ፈረንሳይ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) ቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ስራ የመጀመሪያዋ ነች። በስብሰባው ላይ የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን፣ ቤልጂየም፣ ጀርመን፣ እንዲሁም ኖርዌይ፣ ካናዳ፣ አፍሪካ ህብረት፣ ደቡብ አፍሪካ እና የኤልማ ፋውንዴሽን ተወካዮች ተገኝተዋል። የግሎባል አፌርስ የካናዳ የደቡብ እና የምስራቅ አፍሪካ ዋና ዳይሬክተር ካሮላይን ዴላኒ፡ "ካናዳ ኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር መርሃ ግብር ቀጣይነት ያለው ድጋፍ እንዳረጋገጠች እና ዛሬ በስብሰባው ላይ የ 15 ሚሊዮን የካናዳ ዶላር ተጨማሪ መዋጮ ማድረጓን በደስታ እንገልፃለን። ይህ የካናዳ አጠቃላይ መዋጮ ለፕሮግራሙ እስከ 45 ሚሊዮን የካናዳ ዶላር ያደርሰዋል።
translations.reference: In a parallel process, Afrigen will continue to carry out training and technology transfer to the network partners. The funders play a crucial role in supporting the Programme with total funding to date at US$ 117 million and France being the first to fund the mRNA technology transfer work. Present at the meeting were representatives from the European Commission, Belgium, Germany, as well as Norway, Canada, African Union, South Africa, and the ELMA Foundation. Caroline Delany, GlobalAffairs Canada’s Director General for Southern and Eastern Africa said: “Canada reaffirms its continued support of the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme and we are delighted to announce a further contribution of 15 million Canadian dollars at the meeting today. This brings Canada’s total contribution to the Programme up to 45 million Canadian dollars.

translations.source: እነዚህ ገንዘቦች የ ሀቡን ሥራ በደቡብ አፍሪካ እና በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ የኔትወርክ አጋሮችን ይደግፋሉ። በአከባቢ እና በክልል ደረጃ የአቅም ግንባታ አስፈላጊነትን አጥብቀንም እናምናለን"፣ሲሉ የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን ምክትል ዋና ዳይሬክተር ሚስተር ማርቲን ሲሼል ተናግረዋል። "ኢሲ(EC) ቀደም ሲል የኤም.አር.ኤን.ኤ(mRNA) የቴክኖሎጂ ሽግግር ማዕከልን ለማቋቋም 40 ሚሊዮን ዩሮ ያበረከተ ሲሆን በቅርቡ ደግሞ የክትባትን የማምረት አቅምን ለማሳለጥ የ15,5 ሚሊዮን ዩሮ ሌላ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ከኢ.አይ.ቢ(EIB) ጋር ተፈራርሟል። ይህ በአውሮፓ ህብረት የጤና ምርቶች ተነሳሽነት ላይ ከ1 ቢሊዮን ዩሮ በላይ ከተሰበሰበበት በአውሮፓ ህብረት ግሎባል የመግቢያ በር ስትራቴጂ ስር ያለው አጠቃላይ ኢንቨስትመንት አካል ነው። በሁሉም የአለም ክልሎች ከፍተኛ ደረጃ ያላቸውን ክትባቶች ማምረት ብቻ ሳይሆን ፈጣን እና ፍትሃዊ የሆነ ክትባቶችን ተደራሽ ለማድረግ አሁን ሊከሰቱ የሚችሉ የቁጥጥር እና የፍላጎት ማነቆዎችን መፍታት አስፈላጊ ነው ።"
translations.reference: These funds support the running of the Hub in South Africa as well as network partners around the world. We firmly believe in the importance of building capacity at local and regional level”. Mr Martin Seychell, Deputy Executive Director European Commission, said. “The EC has already contributed €40 million to the establishment of the mRNA technology transfer Hub and has recently signed another grant with the EIB for €15,5 million to facilitate the expansion of vaccines manufacturing capacity. This is part of the overall investment under the EU Global Gateway strategy, where more than €1 Billion have been mobilised under the Team Europe Initiative on Manufacturing health products. It is now particularly important to also address possible regulatory and demand bottlenecks, to ensure not only production of vaccines of the highest standard in all regions of the world, but also rapid and equitable access to those vaccines.”
translations.hypothesis:
